title: Paid Summer Marketing Intern
description: Continental Properties is looking for a Summer 2026 Marketing Intern to join our team of professionals at our home office in Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin. You will work with our Marketing team, our onsite apartment communities, and other Continental employees on daily marketing activities. You will create awareness and drive demand for our apartment communities contributing to their success. Position Specifics: Full-Time Internship during Summer 2026 Pay: $16.00 - $18.00 per hour Essential Responsibilities: Social Media Create paid Facebook ads and graphics for organic Facebook posts. Email Marketing Lead email marketing campaigns to boost apartment tours & leases Outreach Marketing Assist onsite teams with outreach marketing; create flyers, graphics, social media posts. Auditing Auditing of all online assets including social media pages, online reviews, community websites & ILS sites Reporting Work with Marketing team members on expense reporting, Google Ads/social media monthly reports. Skills for Success: In progress of obtaining a Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing or related field Experience with basic content management systems (CMS), Search Engine Optimization (SEO), Search Engine Marketing (SEM), and HTML Experience using Facebook, Instagram, and other social media platforms Why You'll Love Life at Continental: Our award-winning culture fosters innovation and empowers our team members to lead. Here are some of the reasons that our team members continue to vote for us as a Top Workplace: Foundational Experiences: Through our internship program, you will build upon your skills and work on projects that make an impact! Learn more about our dynamic internship program here! Career Growth : You'll have the tools, training, and opportunities to build a foundational career in your field of interest. As an intern, you gain experience through hands-on training, meaningful project work, collaboration, and applicable on-the-job experience. In addition, interns are exposed to all areas of our business to further their learning and professional growth. Balance: We recognize that balance varies for everyone, and we support our team members with that in mind. Recharge with vacation time, paid holidays, and half-days on Fridays during designated months. Community & Connection: Build relationships through informal gatherings, lunches, community and company events, volunteer opportunities, and a vibrant social culture. Learn more about recent Continental events here! Culture of Belonging: The Continental IDEA (Inclusion, Diversity, Equity, & Allies) is our commitment to evolving our culture where team members feel a sense of belongingness each day. Our You Belong Here Initiative and Business Resource Groups focus on fostering an inclusive and equitable work environment for all. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We comply with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), and all applicable state and local fair employment practices laws, and are committed to providing equal employment opportunities to qualified individuals with disabilities. For the benefit of our residents, the communities we serve, and our co-workers, all applicants are required to pass a post-offer criminal background check prior to joining the Continental team* Equal Opportunity Employer This employer is required to notify all applicants of their rights pursuant to federal employment laws. For further information, please review the Know Your Rights notice from the Department of Labor.

title: Social Media & Email Marketing Manager
description: Job Description: We are seeking a Social Media & Email Marketing Manager to join our Easterseals NH & VT team! The individual in this key position will lead and own Easterseals NH & VT’s digital engagement strategy across social media (12+ accounts) and marketing channels. This role is responsible for developing, executing, and continuously optimizing multi-channel strategies that drive growth in audience engagement, brand awareness, and conversion, and serves as the organizations subject matter expert in social media strategy, marketing automation, CRM optimization, and digital analytics. The Social Media & Email Marketing Manager will translate performance data into strategic insights and actionable recommendations that support organizational priorities and advance the organizations mission. This role will report to the Senior Director of Creative. Essential responsibilities include: Email Marketing: Ability to build, manage, and optimize complex email automations and customer journeys, including drip campaigns and form-filled triggered workflows, based on behavior, segmentation, and lifecycle stage. Expertise in list management, segmentation strategies, tagging, and data hygiene to ensure targeting accuracy and compliance. Strong understanding of email deliverability best practices, including sender reputation, authentication, and spam mitigation. Experience developing and executing A/B and multivariate testing strategies (subject lines, content, timing, CTAs) and applying insights to improve performance. Proficiency in campaign reporting and analytics, including open rates, click-through rates, conversions, and attribution; ability to translate data into actionable recommendations. Social Media: Develop, implement, and maintain a multi-platform social media strategy that increases engagement, strengthens brand awareness, and drive audiences to action. Manage and continuously optimize more than 12 active social media accounts across multiple platforms, ensuring consistent branding, messaging, and quality across all channels. Create, schedule, and publish compelling content for 12+ social media accounts via Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, TikTok ensuring alignment with audience demographics and platform best practices. Produce digital assets including graphics, visuals, short-form reels and simple animations to enhance storytelling and elevate campaign performance. Maintain a comprehensive editorial calendar to plan, sequence, and strategically time social media posts, ensuring consistent cadence, optimal posting windows, and alignment with organizational priorities. Manage paid social media promotions, optimizing targeting, messaging, and budget allocation to maximize reach and conversions. Monitor, tag, and analyze social performance using tools such as Sprout Social providing actionable insights and data-driven recommendations. Use social listening and analytics tools to track audience behavior, sentiment, and trends, proactively informing content and campaign strategy. Regularly report on social media metrics, engagement trends, and how social efforts are converting audiences into measurable actions. Content Generation: Develop and produce high quality, on brand content for social media, email campaigns, blogs, and additional digital channels, tailoring messaging and format to align with platform best practices and audience needs. Educate and mentor MarCom Project Managers in developing content assets for digital platforms; in coordination with MarCom colleagues, collaborate with program-based content creators from across the organization. Additional Responsibilities: Collaborate with Senior Director of Marketing and Communications and MarCom Project Managers to build the content calendar and communicate plans with internal teams. Perform additional duties as requested. Attend organizational events, both fundraisers, as well as awareness opportunities to capture content for future use. Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in digital marketing, multimedia communications, or a related field Three (3) to five (5) years of professional experience providing social media strategy, execution and assessment for an organization with multiple social media platforms preferred Demonstrated experience developing multi-channel marketing strategies tied to measurable KPIs, audience targeting, and acquisition Experience managing and optimizing paid social media budgets, including targeting strategy, bid optimization, and ROI analysis Experience using Sprout Social (or comparable social media management platforms) to plan, execute, tag, and analyze social campaigns Three (3) to five (5) years of professional experience with Active Campaign or comparable email marketing and automation platforms (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, HubSpot, or Constant Contact) Ability to create engaging social media reels and simple animations that enhance storytelling, increase audience engagement, and align with organizational brand guidelines Adept at assessing new technology (making go/no go recommendations to the organization); able to suggest and implement new tactics to develop brand awareness and engagement, like promotions and competitions Demonstrated strong writing skills with the ability to craft clear, engaging, and on brand content for email marketing campaigns, social media posts, and blog articles Proficient use of project management software such as Smartsheet to organize tasks, manage timelines, track progress, and support collaboration across multiple projects and teams High-level attention to detail, strategic thinker, well organized and proactive, excellent professional judgment Strong written and verbal communication skills Strong analytical and research skills, including the ability to establish success metrics and track outcomes against them The ideal candidate will demonstrate a commitment to Easterseals NH & VT’s mission and will be an active participant in the Marketing & Communications Department This role interfaces and collaborates closely with MarCom team members as well as Easterseals NH & VT program personnel. An ability and willingness to communicate and work effectively with others is required Proficient on major social media platforms, Active Campaign (or similar), Microsoft Office suite; some familiarity with WordPress helpful, experience using project management software such as Smartsheet (or similar) for tasking, ability to meet tight deadlines and multi-task among multiple projects Valid driver’s license and reliable transportation Hours: This is a full-time position, Monday–Friday, 8:30 a.m.–5:00 p.m. Hours may vary on occasion to support events, which may require early morning or evening availability. Compensation: $65,000 - $75,000 annually, based upon experience. What’s in it for you? We offer a comprehensive benefits package for full-time, eligible employees, including: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life & Disability PTO begins accruing on your first day! Up to 9 paid holidays annually, plus one floating holiday of your choice. For programs that remain open during holidays, staff scheduled to work will receive holiday pay in accordance with program guidelines. 403(b) employer match up to a maximum of 3% Tuition reimbursement after one year of employment Student loan repayment for qualifying degrees after one year of employment Wellness programs: nutritional counseling services, reimbursement programs for weight loss & tobacco cessation We are proud to be a recovery-friendly workplace and a NH Veteran-Friendly Business. To learn more about this role, please click on the blue “Apply Now” button below to submit your application. Have questions about the role? Schedule time to chat with our Talent Acquisition Specialist, Bill: www.calendly.com/eastersealsnh-bill/15min Difficulty submitting your application or scheduling time to chat? Contact Bill at 603.670.6817 or email wstavropoulos@eastersealsnh.org EOE #INDESNH

title: SMM Manager — Social Media Content
description: Job Summary We are seeking a dynamic and creative Social Media Marketing (SMM) Manager — Social Media Content to elevate our brand presence across digital platforms. In this role, you will develop and execute innovative social media strategies that engage audiences, boost brand awareness, and drive online growth. Your expertise in content creation, graphic design, and digital marketing tools will be vital in crafting compelling narratives and visuals that resonate with diverse audiences. Join us to lead vibrant social campaigns, analyze performance metrics, and foster meaningful relationships with our community through energetic and impactful content. Duties Develop comprehensive social media content plans aligned with brand goals, incorporating SEO best practices and keyword research to optimize reach. Create engaging multimedia content including graphics, videos, photography, and written posts using Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator) and video editing tools. Manage daily social media activities across platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and others using tools like Hootsuite and Buffer for scheduling and management. Monitor social listening channels to gauge audience sentiment, identify trends, and gather insights for continuous content improvement. Implement targeted Facebook Advertising campaigns to increase engagement and conversions while managing ad budgets effectively. Conduct web analytics using Google Analytics and other tools to track campaign performance, refine strategies, and report on key metrics. Engage with followers by responding to comments, messages, and inquiries promptly to foster strong relationship management. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including public relations, branding, e-commerce, and graphic designers to ensure consistent messaging across all channels. Proofread all content for accuracy, clarity, and brand consistency before publishing. Stay updated on the latest social media marketing trends, platform updates, and industry best practices to keep our content fresh and innovative. Experience Proven experience in social media management or digital marketing roles with a focus on content creation for diverse platforms. Strong skills in graphic design using Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator; familiarity with video editing software is a plus. Demonstrated ability to develop engaging content that aligns with branding strategies and marketing objectives. Knowledge of SEO principles, keyword research techniques, and web analytics tools such as Google Analytics. Experience managing social listening tools and executing social media advertising campaigns on Facebook or other platforms. Excellent communication skills with an eye for proofreading and detail-oriented content review. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Join us as we craft vibrant stories that captivate audiences worldwide! Your creativity will help shape our digital voice while driving measurable results through energetic engagement strategies. If you’re passionate about social media marketing — from content creation to relationship management — this is your opportunity to make a meaningful impact! Job Types: Full-time, Part-time Pay: From $500.00 per month Expected hours: No less than 6 per week Benefits: Employee assistance program Flexible schedule Paid time off Professional development assistance Work Location: Remote

title: Project Manager, eCommerce
description: Jellycat is a privately owned design-led, gift brand headquartered in London. We create innovative luxurious products in the soft toy, accessory, baby and book categories that are loved around the world. We have an established and growing global customer base and we work with our partners to maximize the potential and reach of Jellycat throughout, sharing joy as we go! As we continue to grow throughout North America, we continue to develop and improve the presence and awareness of our brand. This role is for a highly motivated and dynamic eCommerce Project Manager who possesses a strong inclination towards hands-on involvement and a profound commitment to collaboration. Working closely with both our Global VP of eCommerce and wider global eCommerce team, the role requires a Commercially minded individual who can develop and manage comprehensive eCommerce and Digital Marketing projects. These projects will include platforms and integrations, UX, SEO and CRM as well as supporting with the overall Global eCommerce strategy. This role is designed for a leader who thrives in a fast-paced environment, actively seeks out project responsibilities, and excels at bringing together diverse teams to achieve common goals. The ideal candidate will be someone who does not shy away from diving deep into project details and works closely with team members at all levels to drive project success. You'll be: Leading Projects from Strategy to Delivery Leading eCommerce and Digital Marketing projects from conception to completion, partnering with the eCommerce Business Analyst to ensure requirements are clear, documented, and stakeholder‑approved. Ensuring commercial projects are delivered on time, within scope, and within budget across global time zones. Embracing hands‑on involvement in all project phases and actively engaging with team members, stakeholders, and vendors to maintain momentum. Fostering Collaborative Project Team Management Cultivating a culture of collaboration within the project team. Facilitating regular team meetings and workshops to support open communication, problem‑solving, and team alignment. Acting as a mediator to resolve conflicts and promote a positive working environment. Engaging and Aligning Stakeholders Building and maintaining strong relationships with the Global eCommerce Director and all project stakeholders. Ensuring stakeholder needs and expectations are clearly understood and addressed through continuous engagement and communication. Proactively Managing Risk & Enabling Problem‑Solving Identifying potential project risks and issues early. Leading collaborative problem‑solving and decision‑making processes with the right team members and stakeholders involved. Driving Continuous Improvement Promoting a culture of continuous improvement through regular feedback loops. Implementing project management best practices and leveraging team insights and industry trends to enhance efficiency and effectiveness. Optimizing Resource Management Securing and allocating project resources — including cross‑functional partners and budgets — to ensure appropriate skills and capacity. Participating actively in the selection and onboarding of project team members. You'll have: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Project Management, or related field. Alternatively clear demonstratable equivalent experience. PMP certification is a plus. A minimum of 3 years of experience in eCommerce and Digital Marketing project management, with a demonstrated history of successful project delivery. Experience of working with agile teams a distinct advantage. Experience with Shopify Experience with Amazon is desirable Exceptional leadership skills with a strong focus on mentorship and team development. Experience with Operations & 3PL integrations desirable Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with an ability to engage cross-functionally effectively with team members, stakeholders, and vendors at all levels from CEO to operational team members. Experience of resource management with a mix of in-house, 3rd party and contracted resources. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a hands-on approach to tackling challenges. Strong commercial awareness and able to demonstrate ability to control costs and ensure quality in project deliverables. Proficiency in project management software and tools. Experience of Monday.com is an advantage. An unwavering commitment Our Perks & Benefits: Discretionary Bonus - This role will be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual bonus scheme with awards based on both company and individual performance. Medical, Dental, Vision and other Benefits - Jellycat offers medical coverage through Blue Cross Blue Shield of MN (along with a generous employer Health Savings Account contribution), dental coverage through Delta Dental of MN as well as vision coverage through VSP. Jellycat also offers company paid Short Term Disability, Long Term Disability, Basic Life Insurance, and the opportunity to elect additional voluntary life insurance. 401(k) - Eligible for Safe Harbor 401(k) Plan with 100% employer match up to 5% and immediate vesting. Paid Time Off (PTO) - Jellycat offers up to 30 days of combined vacation and sick time at the start of each calendar year. Employee Assistance Program - Committed to the health and wellbeing of all our employees, we offer a comprehensive and confidential support program. Paid Parking & Building Amenities - Full-time parking space is provided and paid for by the company in the TractorWork’s covered ramp attached to the skyway. TractorWork’s offers several amenities for employees including an on site medical clinic and pharmacy, state of the art work out facility at no charge, both Bar La Grassa and Snack Bar are located in the building, Foodsby food delivery is available daily, and several great coffee shops, lunch spots and walking paths within minutes of our door, and excellent morning coffee served as you walk in the building at no charge. Regular Social & Team Events - We have a very imaginative and active social committee who meet regularly to put on events that help us mark special occasions and celebrate our successes as a team. Physical Requirements: Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer Compensation: $65k - $80k Jellycat Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer.** We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and make employment decisions without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other protected status. We are committed to a fair, inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive.

title: Data Analyst Senior Associate, Sellside Ads Marketing
description: Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. This role may also be located in our Playa Vista, CA campus. Applicants in the County of Los Angeles: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Mountain View, CA, USA; New York, NY, USA; Los Angeles, CA, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA.### Minimum qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. 4 years of experience in Business Intelligence or Data Analytics. 4 years of experience in SQL and Data Visualization (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, or similar technologies). Preferred qualifications: Experience with data warehousing or data modeling concepts. Experience with big data technologies (e.g., Hadoop, Spark, Python, or R). Experience with translating business requirements to BI solutions. Experience with explaining technical analysis to stakeholders. About the job The team's mission is to fund the world's information by enabling online publishers, app developers and media buyers grow their businesses using our portfolio of products including AdSense, AdMob, and Google Ad Manager. We provide industry-leading online advertising solutions for our partners, who represent the largest publishers and buyers. We do this by enriching an open and healthy digital advertising ecosystem, developing trusted products our partners can rely on, and exceeding their expectations to strengthen long-term relationships. The Sellside Ads Marketing team is dedicated to helping publishers succeed by creating sustainable businesses with advertising. We do this by helping our partners get the most out of Google’s ad platforms for publishers by educating them about the advanced features on our platforms, delivering unique insights to help them make better business decisions, and helping them navigate the changing advertising ecosystem. Google’s Global Partnerships team powers extraordinary user experiences through partnerships that solve users’ needs, advance our partners’ goals, and further Google’s mission. Our team provides meaningful solutions — from helping publishers and developers grow their businesses through monetization solutions, to helping our long-term partners anticipate and navigate change in a way that delivers economic and reputational value. We build and distribute products through partnerships across a multitude of product areas, including Ads, Search & Assistant, Geo, Platforms & Ecosystems, Devices & Services, Health, Retail, Payments, Next Billion Users, and more. We collaborate across Google, ensuring a coordinated approach when engaging with our most complex and strategic partners. The US base salary range for this full-time position is $117,000-$167,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google. Responsibilities Consult with users or decision makers to identify data sources, required data elements, or data validation standards. Design business intelligence solutions and infrastructure to facilitate data gathering, storage, and retrieval with minimal guidance. Partner with upstream data providers to establish new source data pipelines. Follow and improve upon the local best practices. Develop data models to assist in the visualization and interpretation of data. Develop reports, queries, and dashboards that access and consolidate information from a variety of data sources with minimal guidance. Design and implement specific features and components with minimal guidance. Perform data analyses utilizing relevant tools, leveraging custom data infrastructure or existing data models with minimal guidance. Work with clients to understand their needs and clarify requirements or direct to more suitable channels. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

title: Account Executive, Roku Ads Manager
description: Teamwork makes the stream work. Roku is changing how the world watches TV Roku is the #1 TV streaming platform in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico, and we've set our sights on powering every television in the world. Roku pioneered streaming to the TV. Our mission is to be the TV streaming platform that connects the entire TV ecosystem. We connect consumers to the content they love, enable content publishers to build and monetize large audiences, and provide advertisers unique capabilities to engage consumers. From your first day at Roku, you'll make a valuable - and valued - contribution. We're a fast-growing public company where no one is a bystander. We offer you the opportunity to delight millions of TV streamers around the world while gaining meaningful experience across a variety of disciplines. About the team The world is cutting the cord and streaming its TV online, a shift that has dramatically changed how advertisers target, reach and measure their preferred audiences in ways that broadcast TV and cable never could. Roku Ads Manager is our answer to the growing demand in streaming advertising. Our self-serve platform just entered General Availability (GA) in September 2024 and has picked up major momentum. Our Ads Manager team is small, gritty, and focused on scaling the best streaming ad platform in the market. We move fast, pivot quickly, and work hard. We rally around decisions, challenge assumptions, and push the limits on what advertising can do for growth marketers, performance agencies, and any other business interested in making the big screen work for their brands’ objectives. The Roku Ads Manager team is looking for an Account Executive to help us scale our platform. About the role TV ads were once reserved for large national brands. Roku believes that it’s better for streamers, brands, and our company that all businesses have a path to advertising in streaming. Roku Ads Manager is our answer to democratizing streaming advertising, creating a self-serve path for businesses of any size to get their brand on the big screen. Roku Ads Manager is looking for an Account Executive to help lead demand generation and client growth for our self-serve streaming ad platform. Below are some key roles and responsibilities: Prospecting: Identify and target large pools potential advertisers who could benefit from Roku Ads Manager’s self-serve ad platform. Use creative solutions to segment the market, build addressable lead list cohorts, and prioritize those for maximum return on outreach (i.e. sign ups + revenue). Outreach: Initiate contact with potential advertisers through various channels, including email, platform-direct messaging, zoom calls, and social media. Clearly communicate the value proposition of our self-serve ad platform and how it can meet the advertiser's objectives. Qualification: Qualify leads to ensure they align with the ideal customer profile and are likely to benefit from the platform. Once qualified, ensure these advertisers have the resources and info they need to start spending, offering onboarding services if required. Onboarding: Assist qualified new advertisers in setting up their accounts and campaigns, as needed. This includes answering questions, recommending best practices, and demoing the platform directly to brands. Once onboarded, you’ll have the chance to grow the leads you feel have potential for meaningful revenue impact. Nurture & Support: Cultivate relationships with top brands to encourage long-term engagement and loyalty. Collaborate with advertisers to optimize their campaigns for better performance. Gather feedback from brands, beneficial to both advertiser growth and Roku product improvements. Answer questions from advertisers via our support channels. Upselling: Identify opportunities for upselling additional features or services to existing advertisers. Collaborate with other teams to maximize revenue from existing clients. Reporting: Generate and provide regular reports to management on key metrics, conversion rates, and the overall success of our platform. Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) to assess the success of ad campaigns on the platform. In summary, Ads Manager AE's play a pivotal role in driving customer acquisition, ensuring successful onboarding, and fostering ongoing relationships with advertisers to maximize revenue and satisfaction. AE's are directly responsible for building relationships and helping clients and agencies drive business results through consultative selling, education, and client service. Ideal candidates will have the ability to drive advertisers’ marketing strategy coupled with a willingness to roll up their sleeves and execute the tactics. Success in this position requires exceptional prioritization skills to differentiate service by priority accounts while delivering best-in-class customer service to everyone. You will be an individual contributor, while also maintaining an ownership mindset when it comes to influencing the product, working cross-functionally, and scaling. The role is fast-paced in a particularly high-growth area, requiring the ability to work autonomously in an ever-changing environment. Sound like fun? For Santa Monica Only - The estimated annual salary for this position is between $56,00 and $85,000 annually. Compensation packages are based on factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, certifications, and specific geographical location. This role is eligible for incentive compensation/commissions, health insurance, equity awards, life insurance, disability benefits, parental leave, wellness benefits, and paid time off. What you’ll be doing Prospect potential advertisers and agencies who can benefit from Roku’s solutions to grow their business Actively participate in all phases of the sales lifecycle including identifying and developing leads; meeting with future and existing clients; developing value propositions; and closing business Operate at scale, managing a high volume of accounts by identifying opportunities and prioritizing service according to revenue and potential Understand clients’ business and translate marketing objectives into results for advertisers, growing advertiser investment on the Roku self-serve ad platform Communicate effectively with advertisers, as well as internal Roku stakeholders; identify and communicate opportunities internally to improve Roku’s products based on advertiser feedback We’re excited if you have Working knowledge of digital advertising marketplace, buying models, and technology 2+ years working in sales, growth marketing and/or a client facing role. Bonus points for working in client-facing roles at ad networks or mobile gaming companies Demonstrated ability in crafting and sending a high volume of personalized emails and overcoming objections Strength in ability to communicate value on the phone, in email, and over video demos Thrive when operating at scale, using data to drive operations and execution The ability to effectively collaborate with internal support and cross functional teams, while autonomously managing your own book of business Ambition for building from the ground up and seeking constant improvement for the business A naturally curious mindset, eager to learn and grow in a fast paced, dynamic environment A self-starter who’s hungry to attack new challenges that come with building a new line of business A growth hacker who believes in helping brands of any size grow with streaming ads Our Hybrid Work Approach Roku fosters an inclusive and collaborative environment where teams work in the office Monday through Thursday. Fridays are flexible for remote work except for employees whose roles are required to be in the office five days a week or employees who are in offices with a five day in office policy. Benefits Roku is committed to offering a diverse range of benefits as part of our compensation package to support our employees and their families. Our comprehensive benefits include global access to mental health and financial wellness support and resources. Local benefits include statutory and voluntary benefits which may include healthcare (medical, dental, and vision), life, accident, disability, commuter, and retirement options (401(k)/pension). Our employees can take time off work for vacation and other personal reasons to balance their evolving work and life needs. It's important to note that not every benefit is available in all locations or for every role. For details specific to your location, please consult with your recruiter. Accommodations Roku welcomes applicants of all backgrounds and provides reasonable accommodations and adjustments in accordance with applicable law. If you require reasonable accommodation at any point in the hiring process, please direct your inquiries to EmployeeRelations@Roku.com. The Roku Culture Roku is a great place for people who want to work in a fast-paced environment where everyone is focused on the company's success rather than their own. We try to surround ourselves with people who are great at their jobs, who are easy to work with, and who keep their egos in check. We appreciate a sense of humor. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. In short, at Roku you'll be part of a company that's changing how the world watches TV. We have a unique culture that we are proud of. We think of ourselves primarily as problem-solvers, which itself is a two-part idea. We come up with the solution, but the solution isn't real until it is built and delivered to the customer. That penchant for action gives us a pragmatic approach to innovation, one that has served us well since 2002. To learn more about Roku, our global footprint, and how we've grown, visit https://www.weareroku.com/factsheet. By providing your information, you acknowledge that you want Roku to contact you about job roles, that you have read Roku's Applicant Privacy Notice, and understand that Roku will use your information as described in that notice. If you do not wish to receive any communications from Roku regarding this role or similar roles in the future, you may unsubscribe at any time by emailing WorkforcePrivacy@Roku.com.

title: Director, Digital Marketing
description: Overview At CHAGEE, we’re awakening a tea movement . Grounded in authentic heritage , fueled by cutting-edge innovation , and crafted to nourish body and spirit. [This is not bubble tea. We are not café culture] This is tea, reborn. Rooted in history. Reinvented for the modern world. The Modern Teahouse is a new third place - authentic, refined and imaginative. An artisanal space that nurtures with a quiet devotion to care, connection, and well-being. CHAGEE Modern Teahouses are some of the most beautiful spaces across Asia, and we are starting our expansion in North America. This experience should blend seamlessly in the online space with our website, app and loyalty program. Not to mention every other digital touchpoint as we develop programmatic marketing capabilities. The Digital Marketing Lead is responsible for owning CHAGEE’s end-to-end digital strategy to drive awareness, acquisition, conversion, and retention. This role oversees SEO/SEM, performance marketing, website and app user experience, digital partner channels, and data-driven optimization. You’ll be responsible for our digital presence end-to-end , the full online-to-offline (OMO) strategy. The ideal candidate brings experience in startup environments, can move from 0→1, and has proven they can scale brands through data-driven and customer-centric approaches. Key Responsibilities Digital Strategy & Planning Own the overall digital marketing strategic plan to achieve CHAGEE’s growth and performance goals. Translate brand priorities, product launches, and loyalty initiatives into integrated digital campaigns. Manage digital budget allocation across paid, owned, and earned channels for optimal ROI. Performance Marketing & Acquisition Lead SEO & SEM strategy to increase qualified traffic to CHAGEE’s website and app. Plan, execute, and optimize paid social, search, display, and retargeting campaigns. Manage acquisition funnels from awareness through conversion, including A/B testing of creative, landing pages, and CTAs. Owned Digital Channels Oversee website content and UX to ensure it is optimized for discoverability, engagement, and conversion. Own app engagement content and UX in collaboration with Product Lead (for features) and Loyalty Lead (for member campaigns). Coordinate digital storefronts and content for delivery partners (e.g., menu listings, promotional placement). Cross-Functional Campaign Integration Partner with Product Lead to promote menu product and program launches through digital campaigns. Partner with Loyalty Lead to amplify loyalty program acquisition and engagement through targeted digital activations. Ensure consistent storytelling, visual identity, and brand voice across all digital touchpoints. Analytics & Optimization Own digital marketing KPIs and reporting dashboards. Monitor CAC, ROAS, traffic quality, and conversion rates, implementing continuous improvements. Analyze customer journey data to identify drop-off points and new growth opportunities. Required Experience At least 12+ years of experience in digital marketing, preferably in multi-location retail or F&B. Proven track record in SEO/SEM, paid media, and CRO. Experience managing web/app UX and collaborating with product and design teams. Strong analytical skills with proficiency in tools like Google Analytics, Data Studio, and ad platform dashboards. title: Specialist, Influence Project Management
description: Specialist, Influence Project ManagementAt Chanel, we are focused on creating an inclusive culture that nurtures personal growth, contributing to collective progress. We strongly encourage your application, as we value the perspective, experience and potential you could bring to CHANEL. About the role: The Specialist, Influence Project Management supports the execution and optimization of CHANEL’s influencer and social media initiatives within the Fragrance & Beauty division. This role is ideal for an organized, agile, and creative individual with strong campaign management skills. As a key partner to internal teams, agencies, and external collaborators, you will ensure seamless project flow, compliance, and high-quality execution across all influencer and social media activities. What impact you can create at CHANEL: Social Media & Partner Management Maintain and update product launch and seeding calendars; share with internal and external partners to ensure alignment Manage daily posting on CHANEL-owned social media platforms, ensuring content is timely and brand-aligned Liaison for collaboration posts with key partners, providing timelines, posting requirements (date, time, affiliate links, suggested copy, technical specs), and ensuring quality uploads Actively track user-generated content (UGC) using management tools; organize and archive content for future posting Maintain and update contact databases (CRM), including address changes and social handles Coordinate PR mailing lists, monitor delivery, and confirm receipt of influencer packages Schedule and facilitate appointments between key beauty experts and influencers, encouraging collaborative content creation Maintain influencer tracking tools (e.g., Lefty) for each activation: set-up, clean-up, and collaborate with influencer marketing agencies/internal stakeholders for campaign analysis and reporting Maintain internal dashboards with affiliate platforms (e.g., ShopMy, LTK), identify key performance drivers, and coordinate dashboard updates with agency partners Track contract days for all contractual partnerships; keep team informed of timelines and deliverables Ensure full compliance with brand, FTC, and third-party guidelines across all influencer activities, especially for high-target CHANEL partners Administrative & Project Support Process and track invoices via Coupa, including setting up new vendors for billing Maintain daily budget tracking, raise flags for under- or overspending, and provide quarterly updates to the team Manage Legal service agreements and Partner with Legal on contracts for influencer partnerships (Beauty Experts, Influencers, Content Creators); manage DocuSign process and partnership tracking Coordinate and submit creative briefs to local AD teams to support influencer seedings and events Help create PowerPoint presentations with information from senior leaders on an ad hoc basis Technical Requirements Strong proficiency in MS Office applications, especially Excel and PowerPoint, for creating clean, professional reports and presentations Proficiency with Adobe InDesign, Photoshop, and Premiere Pro preferred Expertise with top social media platforms and a solid understanding of photo and video content specifications Quick to learn and master analytic/reporting tools (e.g., Lefty, Emplifi, Tribe) Strong understanding of social media KPIs; able to analyze data and identify key trends and partners in the beauty and social media landscape You are energized by: Developing advanced project management, analytical, and communication skills Deepen your understanding of influencer and social media best practices Building meaningful relationships with influencers, agency partners, and internal stakeholders Gaining hands-on experience with leading tools, platforms, and processes in the beauty industry’s digital landscape What You Can Bring to the Team A collaborative spirit and willingness to work across teams and with external partners Strong ability to anticipate project needs and proactively resolve issues Dependable execution of projects with attention to deadlines and high standards of quality Data-driven mindset to spot trends, measure effectiveness, and optimize future initiatives Professionalism and creativity in representing CHANEL across all influencer and social media touchpoints Position Logistics: 1-2 years of experience in social media, either in-house or at a creative agency Bachelor’s Degree required Strong organizational and time management skills; able to multitask and prioritize in a fast-paced environment Detailed knowledge of product and brand Ability to meet strict deadlines and adapt quickly to changing priorities Agile and flexible, with an enthusiasm for the dynamic world of social media Social media trendspotting and a creative, forward-thinking mindset Outstanding written and verbal communication skills Efficient note taker and avid learner with meticulous attention to detail Chanel, Inc. reserves the right to edit, change, or make exceptions on designations where circumstances where deemed appropriate. Compensation: The anticipated base salary range for this position is $61,400 through $92,000. Base salary is one component of the total compensation for this position. Other components [may/will] include additional compensation, benefits and perks. Benefits and Perks: Wellbeing resources include dedicated paid time off for wellbeing: (2-week August Office Closure and a Wellbeing fund) Family and care giving benefits (inclusive of parental leave, fertility support, MilkStork, and Care.com Membership) Generous paid time off policies to include vacation, holiday, sick and volunteer days 401K and other incentives Robust healthcare offerings; medical, dental, vision, MDLIVE (virtual care), One Medical, Flexible Spending Accounts (Health Care & Dependent Care), Health Savings Account and Employee Assistance Program Life insurance, Accidental Death & Dismemberment, Short Term Disability, Long Term Disability, Health Advocate, International Business Travel Accident & Medical, and Commuter Transit & Parking Additional Information: Chanel is a private company whose values are grounded in creating the conditions for people to perform at their best and feel fulfilled and confident in their work. We offer a unique work environment where individuals are encouraged to better understand the brand, the business, and motivations, so that together we can unlock the possibilities of growth. This is reflected in: Diversity and Inclusion: At CHANEL, we are intentional in promoting Diversity & Inclusion. We foster respect, empathy and dignity for all. We believe strongly that the diversity of our people across the full spectrum of human differences is essential to our organisation and the connections we have with each other and our clients. We offer Employee Resource Groups in the US that are voluntary, open to all, employee-led groups formed around a shared identity or lived experience, whose aim is to foster a diverse, inclusive, and equitable community aligned with the values and missions of the communities they support. Chanel Community: CHANEL Community empowers our employees to channel their passions, talents and sense of purpose to contribute to and learn from our communities. Employees are encouraged to take time off annually to volunteer through CHANEL Community. CHANEL also matches employee donations to select charitable organizations. Sustainability: CHANEL Mission 1.5° is our climate action plan. It is focused on transforming the business in line with the ambitions of the Paris Agreement on climate change to limit the average global mean temperature increase to 1.5 degrees Celsius above preindustrial levels. Employees are encouraged to contribute to our US Sustainability efforts at the corporate level and within the divisions, through organized taskforces and initiatives. Arts and Culture: We are committed to extending our legacy of cultural engagement. Global support includes the CHANEL Culture Fund, which supports a select group of leading art culture institutions across the globe, and the CHANEL Next Prize for the next generation of creative talent. US support currently includes Annual sponsorship of the MoMA film program, Tribeca Film Festival programs and Through Her Lens, Support of the Academy Gold Fellowship for Women and Sponsorship of the BAAND Together Dance Festival at Lincoln Center. Fondation Chanel: Since 2011, Fondation CHANEL’s mission is for women and girls to be free to shape their own destiny. Through multi-year partnerships with financial and technical support, Fondation CHANEL is committed to improving the safety and autonomy of women and adolescent girls around the world, impacting over a million women and girls in its first 10 years. For more information, please navigate to the Fondation Chanel website here Career and Leadership Development: We have dedicated in-house teams focused on supporting the onboarding of employees, developing leadership skills via custom programs like Imagine Chanel People, Heart of Leadership and group and individual coaching, and blended online and live classes offered on our Bloom platform and by skilled trainers, such as Shape Your Career, to develop career building skills. Chanel, Inc. benefits and perks are dependent on eligibility and subject to modification by Chanel at any time.

title: Marketing Intern

Understanding of technical or engineered products Familiarity with data analysis and reporting (PowerBI, Datarails) What You’ll Gain Real-world experience in aerospace and advanced manufacturing marketing Exposure to B2B, technical, and industrial marketing strategies Mentorship from experienced marketing and industry professionals Opportunity to contribute to meaningful, real projects Supervisory Responsibilities: No direct reports Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. title: Sr. Manager, Marketing & Creative
description: Calling all disrupters, challengers and creators. From our beginning 30 years ago—with an old sewing machine and some seatbelts cut out of junk cars— Chrome Industries has always done things differently. We equip and empower people to make and move on their own terms, every day…no matter the conditions. We build bold, thoughtfully designed, durable gear that lasts longer than you thought possible. We're constantly looking for ways to challenge the status quo and push boundaries—in how we design, make and market our products. Whether you're riding through the city, skating to a local show or just taking the long way home, we've got your back. If you want to do bold work, move fast, and be part of a team that's building something meaningful, join our movement. Position: Sr. Manager, Marketing + Creative Location: Chrome Industries Global Headquarters in Portland, Oregon (on-site) Duration: Regular, Full-Time Compensation: $136,900 - $144,500 What You'll Do The Senior Manager of Marketing + Creative is responsible for defining and evolving the look, feel, and expression of the Chrome Industries brand across all marketing touchpoints. This role sets the creative and brand strategy that supports business growth, product storytelling, and consumer engagement, while ensuring consistent execution across channels. Operating at both a strategic and executional level, the Senior Manager leads and develops internal team members and external agency partners to deliver high-quality marketing campaigns and content on time and within budget. This role partners closely with cross-functional leaders to translate business objectives into impactful, integrated marketing initiatives. Essential Responsibilities Defines and evolves the overall brand and creative strategy for Chrome Industries, establishing the look, feel, and expression of the brand to engage current and future consumers. Develops and executes integrated brand and product marketing campaigns that support business growth objectives, product launches, and seasonal priorities. Partners with the E-Commerce team to create and implement content and channel strategy across key platforms, including the Chrome website, social media (e.g., Instagram, TikTok), email marketing, affiliate and influencer programs, and emerging channels. Translates company and marketing objectives into clear creative direction, briefs, and priorities that guide internal teams and external partners. Leads, coaches, and develops direct reports, providing clear expectations, performance feedback, and growth opportunities as the marketing team evolves. Plans and manages marketing resources and budgets, ensuring campaigns and content are delivered on time, within budget, and aligned to business priorities. Oversees relationships with external agencies and creative partners, including public relations, freelance designers, photographers, and videographers, to ensure high-quality execution and brand consistency. Partners cross-functionally with Product, Sales, E-commerce, and Leadership teams to align marketing strategies with go-to-market plans and revenue objectives. Monitors and evaluates campaign performance and brand impact, using insights to refine strategies, improve effectiveness, and inform future initiatives. Performs other leadership and marketing responsibilities as required to Support the growth and evolution of the business. Travel: Yes, 20% of the time Qualifications Bachelor's Degree Marketing, Design, Business or related fields. Required Six (6) years experience in advertising, brand/general marketing and/or business development preferred. Six (6) years Prefer experience with premium consumer products, either with a brand or agency. Any equivalent combination of experience and education which clearly indicates the ability to perform the essential functions of the position may substitute on a year for year basis. Knowledge, Skills and Abilities Demonstrated experience developing and delivering impactful marketing campaigns within premium consumer categories. Deep understanding of modern consumer acquisition and engagement channels, including social media, influencer and affiliate marketing, events, and emerging platforms, with the ability to select and optimize channels based on audience and objectives. Strong capability to lead and manage creative teams, guiding the development of artwork, photography, video, and copy to ensure brand consistency, quality, and effectiveness. Proven experience managing, coaching, and developing people, including setting expectations, providing feedback, and supporting professional growth. Ability to translate business and brand strategy into clear creative direction, briefs, and priorities for internal teams and external partners. Working expertise with creative and design tools (e.g., Adobe Illustrator). Proficiency in marketing and creative project management, including planning, prioritization, resource allocation, and timeline management across multiple initiatives. Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders and align partners around shared goals. What we're looking for in our people: Challenge the status quo and push against the expected Equally comfortable leading a team or project and rolling up your sleeves and getting your hands dirty to get the work done Thrive on working hard and playing hard Followers and clock-watchers need not apply Experience in the bag category and sport/outdoor/bike industry a plus Why join the Chrome team? Benefits TBD Other Requirements Please note this job description is not designed to contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without cause. Fuerst Group, KEEN and Chrome are equal opportunity employers. We value an inclusive and diverse community. Qualified candidates of all backgrounds are encouraged to apply and will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability.

title: Search Engine Optimization Specialist
description: SEO Specialist, Get Found Fast Get Found Fast is an established SEO and digital marketing agency in the Denver Metro area with a diverse portfolio of clients. We are expanding our team to better meet our clients' needs. Get Found Fast is looking for a proactive, self-motivated, and detail-oriented SEO Specialist with strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously to join our dynamic SEO department. In this role, you’ll be instrumental in optimizing client websites to drive organic traffic and improve search engine visibility. You’ll work closely with the SEO team to develop and execute effective strategies that deliver measurable, meaningful results. This is an excellent opportunity for those looking to grow and develop in the dynamic and expanding SEO industry. A Little About Us Get Found Fast is a Denver-based digital marketing agency that’s digitally focused and people-oriented. We specialize in building strong, lasting relationships and creating impactful digital presences for our clients. Our team of dedicated specialists is deeply committed to delivering results that drive success, combining innovative strategies with a personalized touch. Our culture is built on collaboration, innovation, and having fun while delivering exceptional outcomes. We value work-life balance, professional growth, and celebrating wins together. If you want to join a supportive team that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, we’d love to meet you! What you’ll be doing with our SEO Team Learn our SEO processes and grow your existing SEO knowledge and expertise. Conduct thorough market and competitor research to understand your client’s market and their consumers. Keyword research development and implementation that best aligns with your client’s goal and the search intent of their consumers Develop and implement SEO campaigns from launch to long-term success Embraces the future of SEO, including AI, and find ways to incorporate these new technologies and harness them for our clients’ benefit Execute a range of on-site, off-site, and technical SEO tasks, including, but not limited to Optimizing existing or developing new content Performing regular technical SEO audits Creating 301 redirect maps Creating optimal SEO title and meta descriptions Writing Schema strategies Identifying opportunities for internal linking Conducting backlink research and assisting with outreach strategies. Google Business Profile optimization Assist in creating and maintaining detailed SEO performance reports for clients, highlighting key performance indicators (KPIs) and progress over time. Stay current on industry trends and search engine algorithm updates to ensure compliance and maximize campaign strategies. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including account managers, web developers, and paid search managers, to ensure holistic client success. What we’re looking for is an individual who Has 1-3 years of SEO experience Has a Bachelor's degree in marketing, Advertising, Communications, or related. Or real-world proven experience in SEO implementation and marketing strategy Experience in utilizing AI tools for their clients’ campaigns and research. Familiar with basic website backend and content implementation Is Detail-Oriented. You know the devil (and the fun!) is in the details. Not only do you not overlook what others might think of as minutiae, but you also like to look at a problem from every angle before choosing a strategy. Is flexible. Your ability to manage multiple projects and work efficiently in a fast-paced, results-driven environment. You’re also comfortable with ambiguity and finding client solutions. Is constantly curious and a lover of knowledge. You have an affinity for technology and a drive to learn to continue providing the best for our clients. A Problem-Solver: We need someone keen and quick, with troubleshooting abilities, who doesn’t back down from a challenge and is ready and willing to get down and dirty. You can tackle problems and be happy to help others understand how you arrived at that point. Effectively Communicative & Collaborative: whether with your fellow employees or our clients, you know how to break down complicated concepts into bite-sized morsels or keep folks up-to-date as you slay a campaign. You feel confident and comfortable presenting your strategies and thoughts to our clients and in group settings. You listen intently to concerns, new ideas, and suggestions. Personable: You like getting to know new folks and industries, and you enjoy contributing to the greater conversation. We’re looking for a team player who is a cheerleader and a leader. An Affinity for Technology Preference will be given to candidates in the Denver Metro area. Ideally, you’d bring to the table… Exemplary writing skills that interweave our clients’ diverse voices, but keep a focus on the technical needs of SEO. Hands-on experience with web analytics tools and search engine platform tracking/benchmarking tools (Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Google Business Profile, etc.) Understanding of technical-SEO fundamentals (page optimization and schemas, URL structures, site crawl performance, links, etc.) Google Analytics Certification is a plus, but not required. Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and common CMS platforms such as WordPress is a plus, but not required. Experience with SEO tools like SEMrush, Screaming Frog, and Ahrefs. Google Search Console, Google Analytics, and Google Tag Manager are a plus, but not required. Bonus points for experience with clients in the construction and medical industries Experience with keyword research/data mining tools and competitive analysis. Working knowledge of how to use generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and Claude.ai efficiently But we provide training, so you must be up for the challenge. Our Benefits Package Salary: $50,000-$60,000 Hybrid work model (remote and in-person) Full-time employees are eligible for Medical/Dental/Vision Benefits Full-time employees are eligible for our Simple IRA matching program Room for growth and development, “on-site” training A fantastic team that is invested in your development and success Incredible clients who look to you to better their digital presence

title: Director, Marketing User Experience & Design (PL)
description: Your opportunity At Schwab, you’re empowered to make an impact on your career. Here, innovative thought meets creative problem solving, helping us “challenge the status quo” and transform the finance industry together. The Marketing Technology Planning & Enablement (MTPE) organization is a centralized function focused on defining, orchestrating, and delivering a modern, scalable ecosystem of capabilities and solutions that enable marketers to continue to drive growth within an ever-changing digital landscape and business environment. In this role, you will lead and shape the center of excellence for Marketing User Experience & Design, which is responsible for designing scalable components, templates across digital marketing channels, including web and email, and for establishing design standards for marketing and education experiences for all audiences. We are looking for a proven leader and design visionary who will advance a program of work, inspire and lead a team, and excel in cultivating cross-functional partnerships in a matrixed, relationship-driven operating model. We are embarking on a journey to advance how we engage clients in their channel of choice with dynamic, compelling experiences. The successful candidate will have a strong background in visual aesthetics, interaction design, and data-driven decision-making to align user experience with business growth. This role is responsible for: User Experience Design & Strategy Leadership: Serve as a design visionary and define the overarching user experience strategy across digital channels, ensuring it aligns with company brand, marketing, and business objectives. Team Leadership: Lead, recruit, and mentor a team of UI/UX designers, researchers, and creative talent, fostering a culture of high performance and continuous improvement. Empower teams to do their best work by embracing curiosity, resiliency, and optimism with a “what now, what next, what if” approach. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with product, development, and marketing teams to ensure consistent, seamless design solutions & experiences at scale across all platforms and client journey touchpoints. Design Quality & Process: Establish, document, and activate design standards, best practice playbooks, and workflows to improve efficiency and maintain design quality and consistency. Business Value & Growth Enablement: Create intuitive, engaging, and aesthetically pleasing interfaces that optimize business outcomes (e.g. conversion rates, brand perception, etc.). Thought Leadership: Advocates a strong point of view and compelling design position but is not rigidly fixed when presented with new information and perspectives – creating the right balance of giving stakeholders voice & visibility while also taking decisive action to drive forward progress. Innovation & Creativity: Lead with curiosity and challenger mindset – questioning status quo, finding new/better ways to solve problems, seeking inspiration and delivering inspiration in the spirit of continuous improvement. Data & Insights-Driven Approach: Oversee user research, usability testing, and data analysis to turn insights into tangible enhancements for digital products and marketing campaigns. Synthesize data and information across multitude of sources and stakeholders to inform work and optimize outcomes. What you have Required: 10+ years of interaction design experience and/or creative leadership focused on digital channels or products Experience directly managing multi-disciplinary user experience & design teams Strong portfolio demonstrating digital design strategy, process, and thinking Proficiency in design tools, such as Figma, and deep knowledge of user-centric design methodologies, research techniques, and prototyping Outstanding communication skills with demonstrated ability to translate complex messages into a compelling story for a variety of different audiences Experience building and delivering communication to executive audiences Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience Preferred: Experience designing scalable components and templates for a large corporate enterprise Ability to effectively operate in a collaborative multi-disciplinary team within an iterative process and adept at working through ambiguity or challenges in a dynamic work environment Proven ability to build trusted relationships in pursuit of shared objectives, and motivate teams with and without direct channels of authority Highly organized and detail oriented self-starter with ability to prioritize our most critical activities and drive toward timely delivery Challenger spirit and innovative mindset - questioning status quo and continuously seeking out opportunities to raise the bar on what and how to deliver the strongest outcomes What’s in it for you At Schwab, you’re empowered to shape your future. We champion your growth through meaningful work, continuous learning, and a culture of trust and collaboration—so you can build the skills to make a lasting impact. Our Hybrid Work and Flexibility approach balances our ongoing commitment to workplace flexibility, serving our clients, and our strong belief in the value of being together in person on a regular basis. We offer a competitive benefits package that takes care of the whole you – both today and in the future: 401(k) with company match and Employee stock purchase plan Paid time for vacation, volunteering, and 28-day sabbatical after every 5 years of service for eligible positions Paid parental leave and family building benefits Tuition reimbursement Health, dental, and vision insurance

title: Digital Marketing Operations Administrator
description: About the Role We are a digital marketing agency with deep roots in the media industry as part of Ogden Newspapers. We're seeking a meticulous Digital Marketing Operations Administrator who thrives on precision and operational excellence to join our team. You'll manage the operational backbone of our digital marketing campaigns, helping our digital media buyers execute flawless campaigns from order intake through delivery and invoicing. This role requires an understanding that the details matter—a misplaced decimal, an incorrect targeting parameter, or a wrong date can derail an entire campaign. Why This Role? This position offers a comprehensive, ground-floor education in the digital marketing and media industries. You'll gain hands-on exposure to every stage of the campaign lifecycle—from how deals are structured and sold, to how campaigns are built and optimized, to how performance is measured and monetized. The operational skills you'll develop—multi-project management, cross-functional coordination, and systematic problem-solving—are invaluable and transferable across industries. You'll work directly with sales, creative, and account teams, building a 360-degree understanding of how digital marketing operates. Key Responsibilities Campaign & Order Management Process and manage orders Conduct thorough pre-launch QA checks to identify potential issues before campaigns go live Coordinate campaign specifications, timelines, and deliverables across internal teams Track campaign status and proactively address any issues or discrepancies Creative Asset Management Organize and maintain digital creative asset library with consistent naming conventions and file structures Verify all creative assets meet technical specifications, size requirements, and quality standards Coordinate creative submissions from clients and sales teams Catch creative errors (wrong dates, incorrect URLs, formatting issues) before assets are trafficked Communication & Coordination Serve as primary operational contact for sales representatives on campaign status and requirements Communicate campaign updates, delivery pacing, and performance alerts to relevant stakeholders Coordinate with sales teams to resolve order discrepancies or address client requests Maintain clear documentation of all campaign communications and changes Reporting & Analysis Monitor campaign delivery and pacing to ensure performance goals are met Identify trends or issues in campaign performance and communicate findings Provide operational insights to support campaign optimization Invoicing & Financial Administration Prepare accurate invoices based on campaign delivery and contracted terms Reconcile campaign delivery with billing requirements to ensure accuracy Track invoice status and coordinate with accounting on payment processing You might be the right person for this role if: You have 1+ years of experience in digital advertising, digital media, or similar administrative role You’re tech-savvy and love learning about new technologies You’re extremely detail-oriented and thrive on quick turn arounds You're well-spoken and can clearly articulate technical information to technical and non-technical team members You’re comfortable with managing multiple concurrent projects with competing deadlines You’re comfortable managing workflows independently without constant oversight Required Qualifications · High School Degree or above Proficiency with Microsoft Excel/Google Sheets for data management and reporting Preferred Qualifications Strong understanding of common pitfalls in campaign setup (targeting errors, date discrepancies, creative spec issues, budget miscalculations) Experience with ad serving platforms (Google Ad Manager, DFP, or similar) Familiarity with creative specifications across multiple digital advertising formats Knowledge of digital advertising metrics and KPIs Experience with invoicing systems or billing software Background in client-facing or sales support roles Experience creating checklists, SOPs, or QA processes to prevent operational errors Job Type: Full-time Benefits: 401(k) 401(k) matching Dental insurance Employee discount Flexible schedule Health insurance Life insurance Paid time off Vision insurance Schedule: Monday to Friday Application Question(s): How many years do you have in administrative / sales support role? Have you worked a remote job before and do you have the ability to work from home minimum 8 AM - 5 PM? title: Marketing Creative Coordinator

This role blends graphic design, product photography, and light video editing to create compelling content that supports product launches, campaigns, events, and brand initiatives. You’ll play a critical role in shaping how our brand comes to life—from digital ads and social graphics to sales tools and trade show visuals. This role is ideal for a creative with a strong design foundation who enjoys both conceptual thinking and hands-on execution, and who is looking to grow within a fast-paced, brand-forward marketing team.Key Responsibilities Design on-brand graphics for digital and print use, including social media posts, email campaigns, web banners, event signage, and sales collateral Edit and retouch product images to ensure brand consistency and visual quality Edit short-form video content (e.g., product clips, social reels, event recaps) Support product launches and sales enablement with high-quality visual assets across, web, social, print, and presentations. Collaborate with the Content Strategist to translate messaging into engaging visuals Work closely with the Marketing Manager to ensure creative assets align with campaign timelines Maintain and evolve the brand library, ensuring assets are clearly organized, up to date, and easy for the team to access Uphold and contribute to the evolution of InVue’s refreshed visual identity What to Expect You’ll own the creation of visual content from start to finish—from ideation to delivery You’ll be part of a collaborative, and fast-moving team that values creativity, organization, and execution equally. You'll have direct impact on how our brand shows up in-market across multiple industries Occasional travel may be required for content shoots or events Qualifications 1–3 years of experience in graphic design, photography, content creation, or multimedia marketing Strong graphic design skills, with a solid understanding of typography, layout, and brand systems Proficiency with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign); Canva and Figma a plus Experience with studio-style product photography and image editing Comfort with basic video editing tools such as CapCut or Premiere Pro Strong attention to detail and ability to tell stories through visual design Ability to manage multiple projects and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment Strong communication and collaboration skills Preferred (Not Required) Experience working in B2B, retail, technology, or hardware-related industries Familiarity with print production, including signage, packaging inserts, and trade show materials Why Join InVue Be the in-house designer for a global brand undergoing a full brand refresh Work alongside a high-performing marketing team focused on storytelling, scale, and creativity Gain exposure to multiple areas of marketing including content, campaigns, product, and sales enablement Shape how InVue shows up across digital, physical, and partner channels InVue Security Products, Inc is an Equal Opportunity employer.
