title: Content and Communications Lead
description: Location : Remote, New York City, or Oakland Role Type : Full-time Compensation: For employees based in the Oakland, CA, and New York City, NY, metropolitan areas, the salary for this role is $111,500. For employees based elsewhere in the United States, the salary for this role is $102,000. Visa sponsorship: Not available Hires remotely in: United States Experience: 7+ Years Recidiviz is thrilled to be hiring a Content & Communications Lead to join our marketing team! We're a small, fast-moving group that creates moments and messages that drive our mission forward. That's where you come in. We're looking for a master storyteller with a strategist's eye for reputation. You will own our narrative across both owned and earned channels, building the content engine that shapes how the world perceives Recidiviz and our work. About us Recidiviz is creating safer, healthier communities by improving outcomes for people in the criminal justice system. We build technology to reduce the number of people in prison and to help criminal justice leaders embrace data-driven decision-making. Using software tools and thoughtful product design, we've been able to safely and permanently reduce incarceration and improve outcomes. We work side-by-side with leaders of the criminal justice system, people in prison and on supervision, and ecosystem partners to build a better path forward. In addition to the revenue we earn from state partners, some of the most respected philanthropists and foundations support our work. People who succeed at Recidiviz lead with kindness and humility, assume good intent, learn from failure, and fix problems when they see them. They are excited to work arm in arm with government leadership and staff to improve outcomes. They are great listeners. They build deep relationships with and empathy for the people we work with and the people who our mission serves, even when those two groups feel at odds. About the role As the Content & Communications Lead, your work will bridge the gap between content marketing and strategic communications, ensuring that every blog post, case study, and press pitch positions Recidiviz to drive greater impact with our government partners. You will not just be writing; you will be hunting for impact. You will interview pilot users to uncover success stories and translate them into assets that inspire adoption in other states. You will partner with our data analysts to understand and communicate industry-leading metrics and insights. Simultaneously, you will manage a highly targeted PR and speaking engagement strategy, securing high-value industry placements that validate our work while limiting our exposure to the risks of a highly polarized media environment. You will report to the CMO and work closely with the State Engagement and Business Development teams to ensure they have the narrative tools to build deep high trust relationships. This role requires occasional domestic travel (2-3x year) to collaborate with teammates in person, attend our annual Partner Summit, and support on-site content production with state partners. Core responsibilities The Perception Drumbeat: Own the editorial calendar and execute a content strategy designed to shift specific brand perceptions about Recidiviz and the challenges we are solving. Storytelling Engine: Build the internal infrastructure to regularly surface impact stories—from data wins to user quotes—and package them into case studies, blog posts, and whitepapers. Targeted Earned Media: Identify and evaluate press and award opportunities that reach specific audiences rather than chasing vanity metrics in broad outlets. You will manage our reputation by balancing visibility with thoughtful context. Roadmap Enablement: Partner with Product Marketing to build a library of state case studies that help our partnership teams show new agencies what is possible, and existing partners the case for more ambitious impact. Ghostwriting & Thought Leadership: Capture the voice of our executive team to ghostwrite Op-Eds, LinkedIn articles, and speeches that position Recidiviz as a leading govtech innovator. Wild success Here are a few things you might accomplish in your first year at Recidiviz that would demonstrate wild success in this role: Develop a New Thought Leadership Angle: Identify and build out a presentation topic for our annual Partner Summit, including pre and post-summit content. Unlock Support for our Partners: Corrections leaders often only make the news for crises. Collaborate with our state partners to drive awareness of alternative narratives about their work, unlocking support for ambitious and impactful reforms. Minimum qualifications Storytelling Expertise: 7+ years of experience in content marketing, communications, or journalism, with a portfolio of complex topics translated into compelling narratives. Navigating Complex Narratives: You have a finely tuned sense of "safe" vs. "risky" media. You understand how to steer in polarized environments and can judge which stories will build trust and support the goals of government leaders. Strategic Versatility: You are comfortable toggling between "Marketing Mode" (AEO, case studies) and "Comms Mode" (reputation, media relations). Project Management: Ability to manage an editorial calendar independently, coordinating with internal subject matter experts (SEMs) to extract insights without burdening them. Preferred qualifications Data/Technology Experience: Experience humanizing deeply technical or data-driven content for diverse audiences. Journalism Experience: A proven ability to interview subjects, find the "hook," and meet strict deadlines. Multimedia Experience: Producing content across video, podcasts, events, long and short-form writing, and interactive formats. Compensation Compensation is standardized based on roles and responsibilities. This ensures equitable compensation and responsible stewardship of our resources. As such, we do not negotiate compensation offers. For employees based in the Oakland, CA, and New York City, NY, metropolitan areas, the salary for this role is $111,500. For employees based elsewhere in the United States, the salary for this role is $102,000. What we offer Effective, extremely thoughtful colleagues, working together on a mission that matters 90% medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage for you and your dependents Flexible time off, including 20 days of PTO, 3 days of paid volunteering time, and 13 paid holidays per year 401(k) retirement plan with 5% company match and no vesting period 12 weeks of paid parental leave Complimentary One Medical membership (availability based on location) Partnership with Carrot to provide employees with inclusive fertility and family-forming benefits, as well as a small but growing number of hormonal health and gender-affirming care benefits Monthly ClassPass credit for personal wellness Free mental health support via Talkspace Well-located offices in Oakland and Manhattan, with the flexibility to work remotely or go into either office as you choose An annual company offsite and other ad-hoc gatherings All the exhilarating challenges and stretch opportunities of a tech startup, combined with the mission-driven heart of a nonprofit More about Recidiviz Recidiviz was an all-volunteer effort until early 2019, when Clementine, Andrew, and Joshua founded the organization. Since then, we've built an A-team of software engineers, designers, product managers and domain experts, from companies like Google, Apple, Dropbox, Opower, and Sidewalk Labs. Recidiviz was part of Y-Combinator's 2019 class and has received support from some of the most respected philanthropists and foundations, including Ford Foundation, Mackenzie Scott, Schmidt Futures, Arnold Ventures, Chan Zuckerberg Initiative, and the Mozilla Foundation. Today, Recidiviz works with (and earns revenue from) over 19 state partners - big and small, red and blue - and touches over 45% of the country's incarcerated population. We have helped to get hundreds of thousands of people out of the criminal justice system, safely and equitably. In addition to partnering with state Departments of Corrections, Recidiviz collaborates with and learns from partners – from organizations like the Correctional Leaders Association (CLA) to community based organizations and justice-impacted individuals, whose perspectives guide our work. To learn more about how we do what we do, check out our 2024 Year in Review, read some press coverage, or watch our TED Talk. And if you're really keen to see our work, you can dive into our Github :). An important note Lasting change is always built on diversity. Recidiviz recruits, employs, trains, compensates and promotes regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, disability, age, veteran status, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, gender identity and all protected status as required by applicable law. We consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. If you have a special need that requires accommodation, please let us know in your application. Even if you don't think you meet all the criteria above, drop your resume, and we'll take a look – you might be great for another role or another time!

title: Social Media Manager
description: WELCOME TO INGENICO Ingenico is the global leader in payments acceptance solutions. As the trusted technology partner for merchants, banks, acquirers, ISVs, payment aggregators and fintech customers our world-class terminals, solutions and services enable the global ecosystem of payments acceptance. With 40 years of experience, innovation is integral to Ingenico’s approach and culture, inspiring our large and diverse community of experts who anticipate and help shape the evolution of commerce worldwide. At Ingenico, trust and sustainability are at the heart of everything we do. About The Role & Team As a Social Media Manager you’ll join a highly collaborative, global marketing team that’s passionate about building a bold, modern brand and pushing the boundaries of how Ingenico shows up in the market. This role works closely with regional teams across EMEA, the Americas, and APAC, as well as Product, PR, and Communications, giving you real influence and visibility across the organization. It’s an exciting opportunity for a hands-on, strategic social media leader to own the channel end to end, shape the global narrative of a fast-evolving payments and technology company, and see their work drive meaningful brand impact and business results. What You’ll Do Strategy & Direction Define and execute Ingenico’s global social media strategy, aligned with brand vision, business objectives, and evolving platform trends. Establish clear platform-specific approaches for LinkedIn, YouTube, X, Instagram, and Facebook, ensuring consistency in voice, tone, and visual identity. Partner with global and regional marketing stakeholders in EMEA, Americas and APAC to ensure social media supports broader brand, demand generation, and communication goals. Act as the point of accountability for prioritization—focusing effort where it drives the greatest impact. Own social media as a business lever, translating Ingenico’s enterprise payments strategy into content that builds category leadership, influences buyer perception, and supports demand generation. Best Practices & Knowledge Sharing: Develop, document, and share best practices for audience segmentation, targeting, and campaign optimization across the organization. Performance Monitoring & Insights: Monitor campaign performance, analyze data, and generate actionable insights to inform future strategies and drive continuous improvement. Cross-Functional Partnership: Collaborate with LOB marketers, technology teams, and external vendors to ensure alignment and effective execution of audience management strategies. Manage off-shore execution team. Qualifications: We need a candidate who understands the "why" behind the data. The ideal profile has a blend of product marketing intuition and technical data comprehension. The Right Experience: 3+ years in digital audience strategy. We are looking for candidates who have worked in data profiling and product audiences, rather than those with a strictly DSP/media buying or ETL/technical engineering background. Technical Literacy: You don't need to be a coder, but you must understand how data flows through a MarTech stack (CDPs, DMPs) and be comfortable working in Jira and Confluence. We produce and distribute premier filmed entertainment and programming through our powerhouse film and television studios, including Universal Pictures, DreamWorks Animation, and Focus Features, and the four global television studios under the Universal Studio Group banner, and operate industry-leading theme parks and experiences around the world through Universal Destinations & Experiences, including Universal Orlando Resort, home to Universal Epic Universe, and Universal Studios Hollywood. NBCUniversal is a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation. Visit www.nbcuniversal.com for more information. Our impact is rooted in improving the communities where our employees, customers, and audiences live and work. We have a rich tradition of giving back and ensuring our employees have the opportunity to serve their communities. We champion an inclusive culture and strive to attract and develop a talented workforce to create and deliver a wide range of content reflecting our world. Job Description NBC4 and Telemundo 52 have an opening for a digital content producer to join our innovative multiplatform news team. We are looking for a self-starter who can get work done without extensive direction. Experience with Search Engine Optimization, social media video management, content management systems, covering both breaking news and feature stories. Using social media as both a reporting and engagement tool are important. Report and write original stories daily for our stations’ digital platforms, primarily nbclosangeles.com. Identify, report, write and edit high-value stories, ensuring the voice, tone and mix of content fits with the overall editorial strategy. Collaborate with TV reporters and producers; repurpose, enrich and publish broadcast video and scripts for digital formats. Curate content across all our digital properties and perform regular quality assurance checkups. Help manage and grow our video-based social media platforms YouTube, Instagram, TikTok and Facebook. Use data analytics to help guide content strategy in real-time and in planning stages. Help plan digital coverage of news events and station tentpoles. Work across departments, including marketing and community, to execute station initiatives. Other tasks as assigned. The shift for this role will be nights and weekends. Qualifications A minimum of an undergraduate degree, preferably in journalism, communications, political science or Spanish or combined college and work experience A minimum of 3 years of experience Experience as an editor, writer or digital news producer at a news organization that creates content under tight deadlines. Understanding of SEO and social media strategies. Eligibility Requirements: Interested candidates must submit a resume/cover letter through nbcunicareers.com to be considered Must be willing to work in Universal City, CA Must have unrestricted work authorization to work in the United States Must be willing and flexible to adapt to changes in responsibilities as dictated by newsroom needs. Must be willing and flexible to adapt to changing work schedules; must be willing to work outside of normal working hours, holidays, overtime and on weekends with short notice. Desired Characteristics Strong news judgment and the ability to identify and prioritize high-value stories of the day. Ability to problem solve and multi-task with unwavering attention to detail. Strong interpersonal skills to communicate at all levels. Excellent Spanish language skills, including speaking, reading, writing and copyediting. Experience working with TV newsroom software, newscast rundowns and scripts is a plus. Multiplatform video production skills, including editing in Adobe Premiere. Experience or familiarity working with live streams and technical troubleshooting. Basic understanding and demonstration of solid editorial judgment and journalistic ethics, including a current knowledge of laws of libel, slander and applicable FCC rules and regulations. Experience in the Los Angeles market and familiarity with Southern California audiences and communities. A deep understanding of internet culture is a plus. Fully bilingual (Spanish and English) is preferred. A deep understanding of local news and culture in the Los Angeles DMA is a plus. Additional Requirements: Required On-Site : This position is required to be performed full-time from an NBCUniversal-designated worksite. This position is eligible for company sponsored benefits, including medical, dental and vision insurance, 401(k), paid leave, tuition reimbursement, and a variety of other discounts and perks. Learn more about the benefits offered by NBCUniversal by visiting the Benefits page of the Careers website. title: Communications Content Specialist
description: Mary Bird Perkins Cancer Center is Louisiana’s leading cancer care organization, caring for more patients each year than any other facility in the region. And with strategic hospital and physician partnerships, we are delivering on our mission to improve survivorship and lessen the burden of cancer. Mary Bird Perkins and its partners work together to provide state-of-the-art treatments and unparalleled collaborative, comprehensive cancer services. This culture of innovation helps attract the best cancer minds in the country, from expert physicians and highly specialized scientists to forward-thinking leaders in supportive care and other disciplines. Together, with our hospital and physician partners, we are one-hundred percent focused on cancer care. Why Join Us? We are looking for talented and highly-motivated individuals who demonstrate a natural desire to support the meaningful work of community oncologists and the patients we serve. Job Description SCOPE: This position has corporate and cancer programs responsibilities. Responsible for the development and timely submission of engaging and relevant content for Mary Bird Perkins Cancer Center (MBPCC) to support the regional communications strategy. Responsible for executing special projects as needed to advance the mission, vision and goals of MBPCC. Functions Research, write and edit content for various communications channels and audiences as managed by the Marketing and Communications Department; coordinate with communications agency for project support. Responsible for providing public relations (PR) support as directed by the Sr. Director, as well as coordinating with external counsel. Responsible for implementing communications strategy tactics by developing presentations, writing key messages and assisting with nominations and other items. Responsible for executing special community relations and other projects as needed. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in English, communications, public relations, journalism or related field. Minimum of three years’ experience in communications or related field preferred, healthcare experience a plus. Specialized Knowledge Excellent written and verbal communication skills and knowledge of public relations skills. Technology savvy. Must be familiar with AP Style. Excellent ability to organize information and translate clinical concepts in to consumer-friendly copy. Proficiency using Microsoft Office, PowerPoint. Attention to detail in writing, editing and proof reading materials. Familiarity with digital photography and other technologies and platforms used for communication purposes.

title: Marketing Manager
description: Continental Western Corporation is a successful and stable packaging and supply company and attributes our success to providing quality products, outstanding customer service and having dedicated employees. We are proud that many of our employees have been with us for over 20 years. CWC is seeking an experienced marketing manager to develop, execute and manage Omni marketing strategies including web marketing, email, social media, trade shows, PR and other sources to drive traffic and generate sales at CWC. You will own the strategy and execution of online advertising, PPC strategy, SEO, and other online channels. Google Ads other AD campaigns for years, have a sharp mind for metrics, and a keen insight into customer conversion. You should be comfortable crafting campaigns from the high-level strategy down to detailed execution and be able to constantly come up with new ideas. You will take initiative, measure results and learn quickly, and thrive in a fast-paced environment. Specific Duties, Activities & Responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Works with stakeholders to develop and execute customer and market-facing plans, campaigns, materials, and events to support product and sales revenue goals, development milestones Develops and stewards CWC corporate and product brands and aligns stakeholders on branding, messaging, and content Plan, develop and manage online, event , and print promotional campaigns in line with marketing plans Brainstrorm new and creative digital and print campaigns that engage, inform and motivate. Responsibility for product brand management Collaborate and work directly with design agencies and other vendor partners Negotiating contracts and managing relationships with outside vendors Monitor and report on effectiveness of marketing communications Maintain effective internal communications to ensure that all relevant company functions are kept informed of marketing objectives Create and manage budgets associated with each of these activities Responsible for managing and optimizing all demand generating functions for new CWC products Responsible for managing and creating all visual branding Identify opportunities for organizational process improvements Collaborate with management and engineers to implement new product lines and product improvements based on market feedback Create content and collaborate with designers to support marketing efforts including press releases, white papers, brochures, email announcements and videos Measure and validate campaign effectiveness and ROI in addition to SEO and SEM results Qualifications: Bachelors Degree or higher 2+ years in marketing Excellent data analytics skills Passionate about consumer hardware Strong visual orientation and communication skills Background in B2B marketing Strong computer skills Some web design experience a plus Experience managing marketing campaigns across multiple mediums Adaptability to new challenges and environments Self-starter who can develop and enact effective processes to streamline tasks A curiosity for technology and learning new things Continental Western Corporation offers a competitive benefits package including 100% employer paid medical and dental insurance for employees, 401(k) with 3% employer match, profit sharing, paid vacation, paid sick time and more. Compensation and benefits are commensurate with skill & experience level. No phone calls, please. Job Type: Full-time Benefits: 401(k) Dental insurance Health insurance Paid time off Work Location: In person Job Type: Full-time Benefits: 401(k) 401(k) matching Dental insurance Health insurance Paid time off Work Location: In person

title: Social Strategy Manager
description: Job Overview: At Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), the consumer is at the core of everything we do. With a portfolio of more than 125 owned, licensed and partnered hot and cold beverage brands that meet virtually any consumer need, our key brands include Dr Pepper®, Canada Dry®, Snapple®, Keurig®, Mott's®, CORE Hydration®, 7UP®, Green Mountain Coffee Roasters® and RC Cola®. INTEGRATED SOCIAL AND COMMUNICATIONS TEAM The Integrated Social and Communications Content team is hiring for the role of Social Media Strategy Manager located in the Dallas, TX area. This role will report into the Director of Integrated Social and Communications. The Integrated Social and Communications team aims to grow our iconic brands with exceptional creative, content and communications strategies. This newly formed team is charged with championing our brands and growing brand love in the social space with fans across the US. This position is for a core member of our Social team responsible for leading content strategy for social media platforms including TikTok, Instagram, Pinterest, YouTube and Facebook. In this role, you’ll live at the intersection of communications strategy and internet culture to deliver creative content through to execution and measurement. The person who will be successful in this position is motivated to make an impact, an exceptional storyteller, curious and passionate about culture and chronically online. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: This position plays a vital role in how consumers discover and interact with our brands. In this role you will: Lead social content strategy for key platforms, including but not limited to TikTok, Instagram, Pinterest, YouTube and Facebook Manage an agile team of in-house content creators to deliver impactful social content that is on strategy and aligns to brand positioning Manage and ensure execution of social content calendars for multiple brands Identify and leverage trends in social media and culture to activate for key brands Steward the execution of social brand playbooks ensuring adherence to brand tone of voice, personality and equity Influence our approach to organic content creation so that we operate at the speed of culture Measure and optimize our creative assets and executions Analyze and report on content performance against social media KPIs Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Brand, Media, PR, Agencies) to ensure consistent messaging Support the broader Integrated Social and Communications team with ad-hoc projects on an as-needed basis Total Rewards: Salary Range: $96,800 - $160,000 Actual placement within the compensation range may vary depending on experience, skills, and other factors Benefits, subject to election and eligibility: Medical, Dental, Vision, Disability, Paid Time Off (including paid parental leave, vacation, and sick time), 401k with company match, Tuition Reimbursement, and Mileage Reimbursement Annual bonus based on performance and eligibility Requirements: Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. 3+ years of experience in sales and use tax and/or accounting. Experience reconciling financially based records and general ledger accounts. Strong analytical, critical‑thinking, and problem‑solving skills. Ability to effectively manage multiple priorities and meet internal and external deadlines. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Highly motivated self‑starter with the ability to work independently and exercise sound judgment. Preferred Qualifications Experience with SAP and indirect tax systems such as Vertex O and/or Vertex Returns. SAP experience related to customers, product materials, or condition records. Bottle deposit compliance experience. Company Overview: Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ: KDP) is a leading beverage company in North America, with a portfolio of more than 125 owned, licensed and partners brands and powerful distribution capabilities to provide a beverage for every need, anytime, anywhere. We operate with a differentiated business model and world-class brand portfolio, powered by a talented and engaged team that is anchored in our values. We work with big, exciting beverage brands and the #1 single-serve coffee brewing system in North America at KDP, and we have fun doing it! Together, we have built a leading beverage company in North America offering hot and cold beverages together at scale. Whatever your area of expertise, at KDP you can be a part of a team that’s proud of its brands, partnerships, innovation, and growth. Will you join us? We strive to be an employer of choice, providing a culture and opportunities that empower our team of ~29,000 employees to grow and develop. We offer robust benefits to support your health and wellness as well as your personal and financial well-being. We also provide employee programs designed to enhance your professional growth and development, while ensuring you feel valued, inspired and appreciated at work. Keurig Dr Pepper is an equal opportunity employer and recruits qualified applicants and advances in employment its employees without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, disability or association with a person with a disability, medical condition, genetic information, ethnic or national origin, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by law. title: Creative Lead, Copywriter, Superette – DoorDash's In-House Creative Studio
description: About the Team Superette is DoorDash's award-winning in-house creative studio. We create work that's sharp, memorable, and part of the conversation, blurring the lines between brand and culture, storytelling and utility, entertainment and effectiveness. We exist to build a brand people actually care about. One that shows up meaningfully across every touchpoint and earns its place in culture through creativity that hits hard and sticks. Our studio is stacked with some of the best talent in the game, and our ambition is simple: raise the bar with every brief. In 2024, we were named the #2 in-house studio by The One Show and The Drum. DoorDash landed on Fast Company's Brands That Matter list, US Weekly's Top 50 Hottest Brands, and was ranked one of the Top 10 Most Creative Brands in the World by Cannes Lions. And we're just getting started. About the Role We're looking for an ideas-first Creative Lead, Copywriter background. Someone who starts with a strong concept and takes full ownership of the work from start to finish. This is a hands-on senior creative role for someone who wants to be in the work, not managing from the sidelines. You'll be one of the senior creatives in the studio, shaping campaigns that move fast, stand out, and leave a mark on culture. You have a passion for storytelling. You know how to define the idea, tone, and voice of a campaign in a way that's both bold and cohesive. You're just as comfortable working on large-scale productions as you are creating something powerful on your own. Big budgets aren't a requirement for you to make something great; you're proactive, hands-on, and always pushing the work forward. You don't wait for the perfect brief; you understand the brand, spot the opportunity, and come up with smart, creative solutions that move the needle. You embrace AI as a creative tool that expands what's possible. You'll collaborate with writers, designers, producers, and teams across Brand and Social to craft work that drives real business results while pushing creative standards forward. If you bring fresh, original ideas, obsess over craft, and want to create work people actually talk about, this role is for you. You will report to our Creative Director of Co-Marketing and Ads, Chris Allick, within Superette. You're excited about this opportunity because you will… Work on projects that feel worthy of a top place in your portfolio. Have a lot of autonomy to run projects and grow as a leader. Develop campaigns with the best partners in the industry, who are all eager to work for DoorDash and help shape our brand. No pitches. No timesheets. Dive deep into the brand to create work that not only reaches the highest creative bar but also drives meaningful results. Experience a different approach to work compared to traditional advertising agencies. Go beyond, making ads to create experiences, content, product enhancements, and solutions that solve our audiences' real problems. Work at an intense pace during your day, but will have a good work/life balance Collaborate with a team of talented writers, art directors, designers, and social strategists who deeply understand our business and audiences. Fully-remote (within the US) We're excited about you because… You've got a portfolio full of smart, well-crafted, famous work that broke into culture. Proven track record in creating and leading high-impact, award-winning campaigns that drive brand recognition and love You've done TV/film that you're proud of, but you're mostly excited about big ideas regardless of the medium they live in – whether that is clever activations, social content, brand entertainment or anything else that earns free media. You're amazing at your craft—an exceptional story telling writer who can craft copy across all types of media. Ability to push creative boundaries while maintaining high production standards and a consistent brand aesthetic. You're an ad nerd. You're proactive by nature — you don't wait to be asked, you spot opportunities and make things happen. You embrace change. What worked yesterday might not work today — you stay curious, adapt fast, and keep up with new tools, platforms, and creative possibilities. You stay on top of cultural trends, always trying to find the sweet spot where a brand connects with people's lives and interests. You're passionate about sports, or entertainment, or pop culture, or all of them combined. You don't work in isolation—you love collaborating with people from all departments in the organization. Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience We expect this position to be filled by 3/31/2026. About DoorDash At DoorDash, our mission to empower local economies shapes how our team members move quickly, learn, and reiterate in order to make impactful decisions that display empathy for our range of users—from Dashers to merchant partners to consumers. We are a technology and logistics company that started with door-to-door delivery, and we are looking for team members who can help us go from a company that is known for delivering food to a company that people turn to for any and all goods. DoorDash is growing rapidly and changing constantly, which gives our team members the opportunity to share their unique perspectives, solve new challenges, and own their careers. We're committed to supporting employees' happiness, healthiness, and overall well-being by providing comprehensive benefits and perks including premium healthcare, wellness expense reimbursement, paid parental leave and more. Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion We're committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That's why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel. Statement of Non-Discrimination: In keeping with our beliefs and goals, no employee or applicant will face discrimination or harassment based on: race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, age, gender, marital/domestic partner status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability status, or veteran status. Above and beyond discrimination and harassment based on "protected categories," we also strive to prevent other subtler forms of inappropriate behavior (i.e., stereotyping) from ever gaining a foothold in our office. Whether blatant or hidden, barriers to success have no place at DoorDash. We value a diverse workforce – people who identify as women, non-binary or gender non-conforming, LGBTQIA+, American Indian or Native Alaskan, Black or African American, Hispanic or Latinx, Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander, differently-abled, caretakers and parents, and veterans are strongly encouraged to apply. Thank you to the Level Playing Field Institute for this statement of non-discrimination. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, and any other state or local hiring regulations, we will consider for employment any qualified applicant, including those with arrest and conviction records, in a manner consistent with the applicable regulation. If you need any accommodations, please inform your recruiting contact upon initial connection.

title: Social Media Manager
description: About the Role We're looking for a Brand Social Media Specialist who lives and breathes social platforms. This role is about crafting snappy, humorous, and insightful copy that captures attention, drives engagement, and truly reflects Ahrefs' distinct brand voice across all our social channels. If you instinctively know the right meme, caption, or trend to jump on, and can translate complex ideas into shareable, human-centric content, you'll thrive here. Responsibilities Own our social media presence, defining and executing the daily content strategy across key platforms like X/Twitter, LinkedIn, YouTube Community, Instagram, and Reddit. Translate our brand's unique voice into engaging, platform-specific copy that ranges from witty and humorous to insightful and straightforward. Stay relentlessly up-to-date with social media trends, platform changes, and emerging formats, ensuring Ahrefs' content is always relevant and ahead of the curve. Develop and curate a steady stream of original social content based on company news, product updates, industry insights, and broader cultural moments. Repurpose longer-form content (blog posts, videos) into concise, compelling social-first formats designed for maximum shareability and impact. Experiment with new content types, hooks, cadences, and storytelling approaches to continuously optimise engagement and reach. Collaborate with the broader marketing team, product, and content creators to ensure social messaging aligns with overall brand campaigns and objectives. Monitor social performance and engagement metrics to understand what resonates and adapt strategies accordingly. Requirements Must have: Proven experience managing social media for a brand, demonstrating a deep understanding of platform nuances and audience behaviours. Exceptional command of language with a portfolio showcasing your ability to write concise, engaging, and often humorous social copy. A natural instinct for current internet culture, trends, and the ability to leverage them authentically for brand communication. Strong editorial judgment and the ability to turn complex or technical topics into simple, compelling social content. Self-driven and autonomous: you can identify opportunities, execute ideas, and iterate without constant oversight. Nice to have: Experience working in the SaaS industry or with B2B tech brands. Familiarity with Ahrefs' tools and the SEO/marketing landscape. Skills in basic image/video creation or editing for social media. What Success Looks Like In 6 months: You'll have established a clear, consistent, and engaging voice for Ahrefs across our primary social channels. Our social engagement will show a noticeable uptick in likes, shares, comments, and conversation quality. You'll be actively contributing fresh, timely ideas to our content calendar, and your ability to spot and capitalise on relevant trends will be evident. In 1 year: Our social media presence will be a significant and growing channel for brand awareness and lead generation. You'll have demonstrably increased our social media exposure, engagement rates, and follower count across all key platforms, directly correlating with an increase in qualified leads and product market growth initiatives. You'll be seen as the go-to expert for all things social at Ahrefs, innovating and pushing our brand boundaries. Why Ahrefs? At Ahrefs, we believe in empowering people to do their best work without micromanagement. You'll have the autonomy to make smart decisions and truly own our social media voice, directly shaping how millions of marketers and businesses perceive our brand. We're a remote-first, async company that values honesty, results, and simple solutions over complex ones. Come join a smart, collaborative team where your words will make a real impact. Ahrefs does not engage with agencies or third party recruitment solutions for the roles we hire for. If at any point we need help, we'll let you know!

We explore experiences that push people to feel more, think more, and see more in others. We challenge the belief of a black-and-white world, and instead embrace the complexity and richness of our experiences. By magnifying these stories we can discover personal identities, shared truths, and how we fit in the overarching human story. title: Remote Marketing Coordinator
description: BioProtect is a medical device company dedicated to helping men with prostate cancer maintain a better quality of life after radiation therapy. Our flagship product, the BioProtect Balloon, was FDA approved in August 2023 and is designed to protect healthy tissue during treatment. Headquartered in Israel, our U.S. Commercial team of about 50 professionals across sales, clinical, marketing, training, and HR is passionate about bringing this innovation to patients and physicians nationwide. At BioProtect, our mission is to transform prostate cancer care through innovation and precision. We’ve developed the next-generation BioProtect Balloon Spacer, a groundbreaking technology designed to protect healthy tissue during prostate radiation therapy. By creating more space between the prostate and rectum, we help physicians deliver targeted, effective treatment while minimizing side effects, improving patient outcomes and quality of life. Every member of our team plays a role in bringing this life-changing solution to men and their families worldwide. We are currently looking for a Marketing Coordinator. Please note that this position is remote and can be based anywhere in the US with a preference to the East Coast. This person will be responsible for supporting the Marketing and Commercial teams with a strong priority on planning, managing, and executing logistics for regional and national conferences. This role requires organizational skills, strong communication, and the ability to manage multiple deadlines. In addition to event responsibilities, this position will assist with operational tasks including social media scheduling, asset organization, purchasing, CRM data support, reporting, and marketing fulfillment. Key Responsibilities Event & Conference Coordination Lead coordination of regional and national conferences, meetings, and tradeshows. Manage timelines, booth and material shipments, registration deadlines, vendor communication, and onsite support as needed. Prepare and maintain rooming lists for internal team members attending events. Communicate agendas and event details to internal team members in advance. Coordinate marketing materials, logistics, promotional items, and post-event reporting. Track key deliverables, budgets, and internal communications for each event. Social Media & Digital Communications Publish approved content across company social media channels. Monitor engagement and route relevant inquiries appropriately. Support deployment of marketing emails through Pardot including formatting, list setup, and scheduling. Lead Management & CRM Support Upload lead lists into CRM systems and maintain data cleanliness. Provide quarterly lead and performance tracking metrics. Marketing & Sales Operations Support Organize and maintain marketing files, tools, and documents within shared company platforms. Upload assets into the digital asset management system for commercial team use. Provide inventory updates to internal teams for brochures, promotional items, and collateral. Purchasing & Accounting Coordination Handle approved corporate card purchases and track receipts. Reconcile spend against accounting reporting requirements. Print, Fulfillment & Shipping Coordinate printing and shipping of promotional items and materials needed for commercial activities and conference events. Maintain organized records of shipments and inventory status. Reporting & Metrics Track and report quarterly marketing metrics, lead activity, and event performance. Support additional reporting needs as required. Requirements Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or equivalent experience. 1–3 years of experience in marketing coordination, event management, or related role. Demonstrated experience in event or conference coordination strongly preferred. Familiarity with CRM systems. Highly organized with excellent attention to detail. Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Strong communication skills and ability to work independently and collaboratively.

title: Marketing Specialist
description: Job Title: Marketing Specialist Department: Fund Development, Community Relations, and PR & Marketing Reports to: Manager of Marketing and Public Relations (PR) Status: Exempt Mission Support: Goodwill Houston (GWH) is transforming lives by connecting people to opportunities that advance the future of work. Through education, training, and employment, we create pathways to success for individuals facing employment barriers. We empower people, strengthen families, and impact communities by addressing both immediate and future workforce needs. With the generous support of our donors and retail customers, we are changing lives through the power of work. Job Summary: Supports and implements marketing initiatives that enhance brand awareness, increase engagement, and align with organizational goals. Plays a key role in campaign execution, content creation, digital marketing, and visual design. The ideal candidate is creative, detail-oriented, and proficient in graphic design, content development, and project management. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Marketing and Campaign Execution: Assist with the development, coordination, and execution of marketing campaigns across digital and traditional channels. Support campaign planning, timelines, deliverables, and performance tracking. Analyze campaign metrics and provide recommendations for improvement. Ensure brand consistency across all marketing and communication materials. Digital and Social Media Marketing: Assist with creating engaging social media content across platforms (Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, etc.). Support posting, scheduling, and monitoring community engagement. Track performance metrics and contribute to monthly social media reports. Email Marketing and Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Support: Assist in creating and sending email campaigns, newsletters, and automated workflows. Use CRM tools (HubSpot, Mailchimp, Salesforce, etc.) to track engagement and manage audience segmentation. Maintain accurate and organized marketing contact lists. Project and Administration Support: Coordinate marketing requests and ensure tasks and deliverables are completed on time. Maintain organized project documentation and assets. Assist with vendor coordination, print orders, and internal approvals. Other duties assigned by management. Content Creation and Management: Assist with clear, engaging copy for social media, email campaigns, blog posts, web pages, and marketing materials. Support the development and maintenance of content calendars. Collaborate with internal teams to gather information, stories, and visuals for content creation. Execute graphic design work to create visually compelling digital and print assets that align with brand standards and support marketing and communication objectives across multiple channels. Additional Responsibilities: Demonstrate professionalism, good judgment, and present a friendly, cooperative attitude. Ability to multitask, make quick decisions, adapt to change, and collaborate effectively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment. Adheres to all GWH Policies & Procedures, Loss Prevention & Safety Regulations, and GWH Guiding Principles. Must be punctual, dependable, and maintain a professional appearance. Maintain a high level of confidentiality. Must safely perform work; maintain good housekeeping standards and ensure all aisles and pathways to fire exits are clear and free of clutter. Adheres to local, state, and federal laws and regulations. Minimum Qualifications: A bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, graphic design, digital media, or a related field is preferred. Two (2) to five (5) years of experience in marketing, content creation, digital media, or a related field is required. Experience in non-profit is preferred. Excellent graphic design skills with proficiency in Canva and/or Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign). Good understanding of social media platforms and digital media best practices Exceptional organizational and project management skills. Experience with email marketing platforms (HubSpot, Mailchimp, Constant Contact, etc.). Certification(s) are preferred. Excellent computer skills (proficient in Microsoft Office). Strong writing, editing, proofreading, and storytelling abilities. Knowledge of video editing and motion graphics is preferred. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Key Performance Indicators (KPI): Campaign Execution: Delivers marketing campaigns, assets, and performance reports on time while maintaining brand consistency. Digital and Social Media: Keep a regular posting schedule, achieve engagement and growth goals, respond promptly to the community, and submit analytics reports monthly. Content Creation: Develop accurate, engaging content, deliver monthly calendars, and fulfill internal content promptly. Email and CRM Performance: Execute email campaigns that achieve high open and click-through rates, keep contact lists clean and organized, and ensure precise segmentation. Project and Administrative Support: Complete assigned tasks within the established timeline, maintain organized project files, and coordinate vendor and print needs effectively. Physical Requirements/Work Environment: Ability to sit, stand, bend, and reach. Ability to lift, push, and pull up to 25 lbs.

title: Digital Marketing Coordinator
description: Overview: The Digital Marketing Coordinator plans and executes email marketing campaigns, working collaboratively with both internal and external partners. Monitors results and recommends changes for optimization. Responsibilities: 1.Responsible for the execution of the e-mail marketing program. Includes actively planning, creating, and scheduling e-mail messages to guests while ensuring accuracy of all content and audiences. Coordinates with the creative team to request and schedule development of necessary creative assets and meeting all relevant deadlines. Partners with the Sr. Digital Marketing Manager to plan guest experiences for multiple e-mail campaigns, ensuring appropriate frequency across campaigns. Responsible for evaluating effectiveness of each e-mail campaign implemented and makes recommendations to optimize content, audience, or schedule. Responsible for the execution of digital display and re-targeting ads. Including working with multiple agency partners to effectively target ads to select audiences as well as monitoring and recommending changes based the ad effectiveness to those targets. Supports and monitors paid social marketing campaigns. Partners with the Sr. Digital Marketing Manager to guide audience targeting and implement messaging strategy for social marketing campaigns. Participates in search marketing (largely SEM) planning, evaluation, and continual improvement. Includes coordinating with agency partners to refine and implement search marketing programs, regular and frequent communication both internally and externally to react to changing circumstances and monitors performance to maximize the return. Performs other duties as assigned and needs. Qualifications: College degree and 2-4 years of relevant work experience. Email Service Provider (ESP) – prior experience with Responsys, Cheetah Mail/ Marigold, Constant Contact, MailChimp, or a similar system. Some previous technical experience specifically in HTML and CSS. Detail oriented - able to provide structure in fluid environments, ensure flawless execution, and keep multiple balls in the air at the same time. Change agent - not only reacts well to change but helps to lead, actively participate in, and communicate change. Excellent communication skills. Guest focused - always able to think from a guest's perspective and represent their needs internally and in all marketing communications. Collaborative work style - works well with others, builds consensus, likes to have fun. #LI-JW1

title: Creative Content Specialist - Video & Marketing Projects
description: At G.L. Hunt Foundation Repair and Service Squad Plumbing, we’ve built our reputation on solving homeowners’ toughest problems with expertise, care, and creativity. As we expand our brand presence, we’re investing in compelling video and social media content that tells our story and connects with our customers. We’re looking for a Creative Content Specialist to lead the charge on video production and creative storytelling, while also supporting our Marketing Director on special projects across campaigns, branding, and digital initiatives. Why Join Us? Be part of two fast-growing, well-respected home service brands with a combined $30M+ in annual revenue. Play a hands-on creative role with the freedom to bring ideas to life. Work in a collaborative, enterprising culture where your contributions directly impact growth. Opportunities for career growth as our marketing team continues to expand. Compensation: $65,000 - $85,000 yearly

title: Project Manager - Digital Marketing
description: We are seeking an experienced Program / Project Manager to lead and manage multiple technology and digital initiatives. The ideal candidate will bring strong program governance, stakeholder management, and cross-functional coordination experience within a digital or technology-driven environment. title: Senior Creative Strategist - Donut
description: WHY DONUT STUDIOS? At New Engen, we help brands grow, not just bigger, but smarter. We’re a digital marketing agency that drives meaningful impact across the entire customer journey by integrating media, creative, analytics, influencer, and retail strategies into performance-driven campaigns. Donut Studios is our in-house creative studio, purpose-built to craft high-performing, platform-native, and digital content for the world’s most ambitious brands. As a key part of New Engen’s suite of specialized solutions, alongside offerings like the Acorn Creator Suite - Donut Studios helps unlock breakthrough creative that drives results, fast. We’re a collaborative group of strategists, content creators, copywriters, editors, designers, account managers, and more who are all in on helping brands, and each other, thrive. We work hard, stay humble, and build with purpose. WHAT YOU’LL DO We’re looking for a Senior Creative Strategist to take point on our most impactful client accounts and help guide our growing team. You’ll lead creative direction, shape testing strategies, diagnose performance dips, and mentor junior strategists—all while collaborating closely with our Senior Creative Director to co-manage the team. If you’ve worked at a digital marketing agency or in-house at a DTC brand, and you know how to drive performance through smart, scroll-stopping creative, this role is for you. What you'll be diving into day-to-day: Strategic Ownership & Performance Lead creative strategy on multiple high-priority accounts with minimal oversight Develop and evolve creative strategy across testing, scaling, and evergreen phases Diagnose creative performance issues and implement effective solutions Improve ROAS and increase scale on underperforming accounts Maintain stable account performance for extended periods (6+ months) Launch high-volume, high-spend new clients and set them up for success Build and document strategy frameworks for handoff or ongoing growth Execution & Creative Direction Brief creative partners clearly and thoroughly, reducing the need for rewrites or clarification Review and quality check final exports to ensure they meet strategy and brand expectations Collaborate cross-functionally with design, production, and media teams to launch high-impact work Team Collaboration & Client Leadership Serve as the strategic lead in client meetings, presentations, and resets Co-manage the creative strategy team alongside the Senior Creative Director Mentor and support junior strategists, helping them grow their skills and confidence Develop and refine systems that make creative collaboration efficient and scalable WHAT YOU’LL NEED 6+ years of experience in paid social creative strategy, ideally at a digital marketing agency or in-house at a DTC brand Strong instincts for direct response creative and platform-specific storytelling Proven track record of leading client strategy and improving creative performance Deep understanding of Meta, TikTok, YouTube, and other digital channels Excellent communicator and collaborator who thrives in fast-paced, cross-functional environments Confident briefing partner and creative reviewer with high attention to detail Tools and nice to haves: Proficiency with Asana, Notion, Slack, and Google Suite (especially Sheets) Familiarity with Figma or Adobe Creative Suite is a plus Bonus points for experience with creative testing frameworks or building UGC programs SOME OF OUR PERKS Medical, Dental, Vision, Short/Long-Term Disability, Life/AD&D 401k - Employer match HSA - Employer contributes Flexible Vacation & PTO plan Paid Maternity / Paternity Paid Holidays Remote/Flex day schedule Laid back office environment The coolest team ;) Fun company events & off-sites Salary Range: $75,000 - $80,000 The range is applicable for the labor market where the role is intended to be hired. Final base salary is directly related to each candidates' qualifications and experience uniquely. New Engen is proud to have offices in Seattle, New York, and Northwest Arkansas, each one anchoring teams that drive creativity, collaboration, and culture. But great work isn’t limited to those zip codes. We also support a distributed team of remote employees across the country, giving people the flexibility to do their best work wherever they are. We currently hire full-time employees in the following states: Arkansas, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Michigan, Missouri, Nevada, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Oregon, Pennsylvania, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Virginia, Washington, and Wisconsin Let’s Build What’s Next Together - If you’re passionate about media and eager to make a meaningful impact with a team that’s all in, we’d love to hear from you. Important Notice About Job Openings: Please be aware of fraudulent job postings, consulting engagements, solicitations, or employment offers from suspicious sources. These attempts are often designed to steal your personal information or trick you into paying fees for recruitment or training services. New Engen does NOT charge any application, processing, or training fees at any stage of the recruitment or hiring process. All legitimate job openings will be posted directly on our careers page, and all communication about these roles will come exclusively from Recruitment outreach, conducted via email through our ATS, JazzHR, and never through unsolicited text messages or the @newengen.online domain. If you have any concerns about the authenticity of any message claiming to be from New Engen, please contact us immediately at peopleteam@newengen.com before taking any further action. We take your security seriously and are here to support you, ensuring your job search is a safe, positive experience. SzEQWjpVor

title: Digital Marketer

title: Senior Digital Marketing Coordinator - Website Product Owner
description: Note: If you are CURRENTLY employed at Children's and/or have an active badge or network access, STOP here. Submit your application via Workday using the Career App (Find Jobs). No matter the role, every member of our team is an essential part of our mission to make kids better today and healthier tomorrow. We’re committed to putting you first, and that commitment is at the heart of our company culture: People first. Children always. Find your next career opportunity and make a difference doing what you love at Children’s. Job Description The Senior Digital Marketing Coordinator leads the strategy and guides execution of digital initiatives across Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta’s websites and digital platforms. This role requires strong expertise in website user experience, SEO and project management, as well as a solid understanding of technical website applications, including basic HTML and website infrastructure. The individual works closely with our developer(s) to prioritize the backlog and assists in planning sprints that align with business and leader priorities. They exhibit ownership and accountability and have an innate drive to proactively identify opportunities, drive projects from initiation to completion, and ensure strategic alignment with business goals. This role is best suited for someone who combines strong technical knowledge with strategic thinking and can confidently collaborate across teams to deliver measurable results. Experience 3 years of experience in marketing, advertising, or communications Content management system (CMS) experience Experience in SEO, digital paid media acquisition or marketing automation Preferred Qualifications Proven experience managing large-scale digital projects and multiple priorities Strong content management system (CMS) experience, preferably Sitecore Experience with SEO (content and technical) Experience managing a backlog and working closely with developers to move tickets through internal development processes Certification and/or experience with Agile Development is a plus Education Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Computer Science, or related field Certification Summary No professional certifications required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Strong kn owledge of website UX and information architecture best practices (navigation, page structure, content hierarchy) ​ Technical SEO expertise (metadata, site structure, page performance, schema, etc.). Experience with content management systems and a fundamental understanding of how websites are built (to communicate effectively with developers). Understanding of Agile/product ownership concepts (backlogs, sprints, user stories, acceptance criteria). Solid project and stakeholder management skills, including scoping, timelines, and cross-functional coordination. Clear, persuasive communication skills for explaining digital/UX/SEO concepts to non-technical partners and leadership. Strategic, solutions -oriented mindset; able to view the website as a product serving users and business goals and to prioritize a backlog accordingly. creative operations; or production at a studio, network, agency, or digital publisher Working knowledge of major social platforms including TikTok, Instagram, X, YouTube, and emerging platforms, with an understanding of how content, timelines, and deliverables vary by platform Ability to quickly learn and adapt to new software, systems, and tools Passion for social media, digital storytelling, and Paramount brands Additional Qualifications: General understanding of social media content production workflows and campaign budgeting Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Experience with project management tools such as Airtable or similar platforms Familiarity with Jira, SAP, Slack, Microsoft Office 365, and Google Suite Strong attention to detail with proactive problem-solving and anticipatory skills Ability to assess, improve, and implement workflows and processes Comfortable working in a fast-paced, matrixed organization Ability to remain calm under pressure while managing shifting priorities Positive, collaborative, and solutions-oriented mindset Experience in social or entertainment marketing preferred Excellent written and verbal communication skills Paramount+, a direct-to-consumer digital subscription video on-demand and live streaming service from Paramount Global, combines live sports, breaking news, and a mountain of entertainment. title: Marketing Coordinator / Senior Marketing Coordinator
description: About Lightcurve Headquartered in Tacoma, WA, Lightcurve is a local leader in delivering high-speed internet and digital connectivity solutions to communities throughout Washington State. With a commitment to provide innovative and reliable broadband services delivered with a focus on excellent customer service, Lightcurve is dedicated to connecting people, supporting community growth, and building networks that fuel our local economies. In 2023 Palisade Infrastructure (“Palisade”) closed on a transaction to acquire Rainier Connect, forming Lightcurve. In 2024 Lightcurve closed on a transaction acquiring the Washington state operations of Consolidated Communications. Since then, Lightcurve has announced plans to deploy fiber optic broadband in several communities. These investments will allow Lightcurve to rapidly invest in modern fiber-optic broadband networks in both existing markets as well as in several expansion markets, bringing the latest in high-speed reliable broadband solutions to its communities. Company Culture and Values - At Lightcurve, we are driven by our core values. Integrity Upholding the highest standards of integrity at all times. Delighting Our Customers : Delivering exceptional experiences that exceed expectations. Collaboration : Working together to create successful outcomes for customers and employees alike. Position Summary As the Marketing Coordinator, you'll oversee the entire lifecycle of marketing campaigns, from conceptualization to execution, and post-analysis. This role requires a blend of strategic planning, content creation, and day-to-day management to ensure a cohesive brand message and increased customer engagement. Operating in a fast-paced environment, you'll need strong judgment, attention to detail, and adaptability. Collaboration with various teams is essential to ensure campaign execution aligns with business objectives. The ideal candidate should have a deep understanding of our products, competitors, and market trends, coupled with creativity and meticulousness in their marketing approach. Key Responsibilities: Campaign Coordination: Planning, execution, and tracking of marketing campaigns across multiple channels, including digital, social media, email, print, and events. Coordinate timelines, deliverables, and resources to ensure seamless campaign execution. Content Development: Collaborate with the creative agency to develop compelling marketing materials, including copywriting, graphics, videos, and other creative assets. Ensure that all content aligns with brand guidelines and resonates with target audiences. Marketing Collateral Management: Manage the production and distribution of marketing collateral, such as brochures, flyers, posters, and promotional materials. Coordinate printing, shipping, and inventory management to support marketing initiatives effectively. Digital Marketing Support: Assist in the management of digital marketing channels, including website updates, social media posting, email campaigns, and online advertising. Monitor campaign performance metrics and provide insights for optimization. Market Research and Analysis: Utilize market research and competitive analysis to identify trends, customer preferences, and opportunities for innovation. Use data-driven insights to inform marketing strategies and decision-making. Event Coordination: Support the planning and execution of marketing events, trade shows, and sponsorships. Coordinate logistics, booth setup, promotional materials, and post-event follow-up to maximize ROI and brand exposure. Collaboration and Communication: Effectively communicate campaign status, provide insights and collaborate cross-functionally to ensure alignment and integration of marketing efforts across various departments. Data Driven Decisions : Produce summaries of campaign performance, monitor marketing effectiveness, and conduct competitive analyses to identify areas for improvement and inform future strategies. Industry Trends: Stay informed about industry trends, competitor activities, and customer behavior to identify opportunities for innovation and improvement. Be a strong advocate for company purpose and values: By using the Lightcurve values as your guide to how you operate and engage with customers. Measuring Success Campaign Performance: The effectiveness of marketing campaigns is a key measure of success for a Marketing Coordinator. This includes metrics such as campaign reach, engagement, conversion rates, and return on investment (ROI). Successful campaigns should achieve predetermined goals and objectives, such as increasing brand awareness, generating leads, or driving sales. Brand Consistency: Maintaining consistency in brand messaging and visual identity across all marketing channels is essential for building brand recognition and trust. Success can be measured by the degree of alignment between marketing materials, adherence to brand guidelines, and consistency in brand communication. Timeliness and Efficiency: The ability to manage marketing initiatives within established timelines and budgets is important for success. Measures of success include meeting project deadlines, staying within budget constraints, and maximizing resource utilization to achieve optimal outcomes. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaboration with internal teams and external partners is critical for executing successful marketing campaigns. Success can be measured by the level of cooperation and alignment achieved across departments, as well as the effectiveness of communication and coordination in achieving shared objectives. Lead Generation and Conversion: Generating high-quality leads and converting them into customers is a primary objective of marketing activities. Success can be measured by metrics such as lead quantity, lead quality, conversion rates, and sales revenue attributed to marketing efforts. Experience and Qualifications · Bachelor's degree in marketing, communications, public relations, or a closely related field. · 1-3 years of marketing experience (Required); 6+ years of marketing experience for Senior Coordinator role. · Telecommunications experience a plus. · Strong understanding of marketing principles, strategies, and tactics. This includes knowledge of market research, consumer behavior, branding, advertising, digital marketing, and social media marketing. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills are essential. They should be able to effectively convey ideas, write compelling copy, and communicate with various stakeholders. · Strong organizational skills to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and ensure smooth project execution. · Proficiency in analyzing marketing data and metrics to evaluate campaign performance and make data-driven decisions. Familiarity with tools like Google Analytics, CRM systems, and marketing automation platforms is often required. · Ability to think creatively and come up with innovative marketing ideas and campaigns that resonate with the target audience. · Proficiency in digital marketing channels such as email marketing, social media, content marketing, SEO, and SEM. Experience with managing social media accounts, creating content calendars, and executing digital advertising campaigns is highly valued. · Ability to collaborate closely with other members of the marketing team as well as cross-functional teams such as sales, product development, and design. Being a team player and having strong interpersonal skills are important. · Basic project management skills to effectively plan, execute, and monitor marketing initiatives. This includes coordinating with internal teams, external vendors, and tracking project timelines and budgets. · The marketing landscape is constantly evolving with new technologies, trends, and consumer behaviors. A Marketing Coordinator should be adaptable and willing to learn new skills and tools to stay ahead in the field. · Experience with relevant software and tools used in marketing is a plus. · A scrappy, nimble, innovative, and hands-on contributor who is not afraid to roll up their sleeves to accomplish goals and has a passion for competing and winning against larger players in the market. Compensation: · This full-time salary position pays $66,000-$89,000 annually · Reimbursable mileage/travel as needed · Medical, Dental and Vision insurance · Optional supplemental insurance programs · 401K w/ Employer match · Access to Employee Assistance Programs · 10 Paid Holidays · 4 weeks per year Paid Time Off (prorated & accrued) Lightcurve is an Equal Opportunity Employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. Job Type: Full-time Pay: $66,000.00 - $89,500.00 per year Benefits: 401(k) 401(k) matching Dental insurance Employee assistance program Health insurance Life insurance Paid time off Tuition reimbursement Vision insurance Work Location: In person
