title: Paid Social Media Specialist (YouTube)
description: Are you the kind of person who watches an ad on YouTube and immediately starts analyzing the hook, the targeting, and the call to action? Do you love the balance of creative storytelling and performance optimization? Do you get excited about turning video content into measurable business results? If so, this role is built for you. Open Influence, a leader in influencer marketing, is looking for a Paid Media Specialist with deep expertise in YouTube advertising. In this remote, U.S.-based role, you will lead the execution and optimization of paid campaigns that amplify creator and influencer content through YouTube's ad ecosystem for globally recognized brands. IMPORTANT: To qualify, you must have hands-on experience running campaigns in Google Ads, specifically for YouTube. About Us Open Influence is a global, award-winning creator marketing company, providing premium, end-to-end creator marketing solutions. Since 2013, we have been pioneering the creator economy, executing social-first strategies for over 1,500 top brands—including Uber, Subaru, Coca-Cola, AT&T, and Disney. As a platform-agnostic and talent-agnostic partner, we connect brands with the right creators, on the right channels, to drive measurable impact and business growth. As a YouTube Paid Media Specialist, you will sit at the intersection of creator content and performance media, responsible for scaling video campaigns that deliver awareness, engagement, and conversion outcomes. What You'll Do Develop, execute, and manage YouTube ad campaigns across formats such as in-stream, in-feed, Shorts, and connected TV. Use Google Ads to set up campaign structures, audiences, bidding strategies, and conversion tracking. Optimize creative performance, including hook testing, audience segmentation, frequency management, and placement strategy. Analyze performance data to identify trends, improve efficiency, and scale what works. Translate brand and influencer content into paid media strategies designed for video-first performance. Manage budgets, pacing, and forecasting to ensure efficient media delivery. Partner with Account, Creative, and Strategy teams to align paid efforts with influencer and content programs. Serve as a client-facing expert, presenting insights, performance updates, and optimization plans. What We're Looking For 2+ years of hands-on experience running YouTube campaigns in Google Ads Experience activating paid media on other platforms such as Meta and TikTok is a plus, but not a requirement Strong understanding of video KPIs such as view rate, CPV, watch time, CTR, and conversion lift Experience with performance-focused campaigns (direct response, ecommerce, app, or lead gen) Familiarity with audience strategies including remarketing, custom intent, and affinity segments Comfort working with conversion tracking, tagging, and attribution frameworks Agency experience preferred Why Join Open Influence? Work with top-tier global brands and be at the forefront of influencer marketing and paid media. Be part of a high-caliber, fast-growing team in a rapidly evolving industry. Enjoy the flexibility of remote work while making a tangible impact. Collaborate with innovative minds and gain exposure to cutting-edge marketing strategies. If you're excited about the intersection of influencer marketing and paid social media, and you have the experience to back it up, we'd love to hear from you! The expected salary range for this role is $65,000–$100,000 USD, depending on experience.* Open Influence provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws. Highly organized and resourceful, thriving in a fast-moving, growth-oriented environment. Passionate about Vuori’s mission and aesthetic — living an active, mindful lifestyle and staying connected to culture and creativity. Experience managing multi-market or global social operations. Proven success integrating organic and paid social strategies. Experience managing agency partners and social budgets. Willingness to travel occasionally for shoots, events, and brand activations. title: Account Director
description: THE ROLE We are looking for an Account Director to join our team! This role will be responsible for overseeing a team who manages the day-to-day account initiatives for an assigned client. This role will involve the following areas: client relationship building and true partnership, execution of client campaigns or retainers, managing junior account team members, management of scopes and budgets, developing presentations and proposals, overseeing the internal team process and workflow, client problem solving, collaboration with internal stakeholders and planning/reporting. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in the aforementioned areas. WE ARE A creative agency powered by social connections. That means we thrive on what’s trending and creating campaigns that get people buzzing. We call it “Ideas Worth Talking About,” and we've proud to have played a part in some of the most culturally relevant campaigns of the decade for brands like McDonald’s, Adidas, Universal Studios, and YouTube. WHY YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE We like to laugh, we live for celeb gossip and we’re great on AUX. Our passport program lets you explore and work from one of our 18 other offices worldwide, including Paris, Singapore, Madrid, Dubai, London and more! You best believe we know how to celebrate our wins. And our losses (it’s how we learn). From carbon offsets to community kitchens, we’re passionate about making a difference. Did we say snacks? Because we have great snacks. (Shoutout to Sheila who keeps the kitchen stocked.) YOU ARE A person who thrives in a fast-paced environment, responding quickly and proactively Not interested in just following trends – you want to create them A self-starter, who’ll take ideas from concept to execution, navigating feedback like a pro Traditional agency experience is not required, but it’s a plus. What matters is your deep understanding of what resonates with social audiences. This role is energetic, innovative and forward-thinking. Alongside engaging with communities, you’ll guide brands by staying ahead of social trends and cultural moments. In return, you’ll join a global agency dedicated to generating buzzworthy ideas for some of the world’s leading brands. WHAT YOU’LL DO Serve as the primary point person in day to day client communication on projects. Manage clients and maintain friendly and productive lines of communication at all times. Deliver channel strategies and tactics for specific product launches Track work progress and deliverables and relay client feedback to team and senior leadership Provide strategic marketing guidance and thought leadership: Working to understand your clients’ businesses while simultaneously educating clients about how we can help. Become fluent in business objectives and organization goals Oversee client profitability/burn report, account budgeting/actualization and measurement metrics that demonstrate clear return to the client Work with internal team to develop, maintain, and adhere to schedules and plans as outlined by client Acquire proper internal and client approvals to ensure smooth and timely project execution Communicate and follow up with clients and internal departments regarding all projects to address any concerns Maintain working knowledge all aspects of production with vendors on behalf of the client Prepare and oversee the status of all client jobs and/or projects, confirm that all jobs are delivered on-time, and expedite operations that delay or alter schedules to meet unforeseen conditions. Build successful partnerships with client business leads and key decision makers to increase and expand revenue opportunities by identifying production solutions Develop staffing plans and proposals for new projects and briefs Maintain rigor and consistency in use of agency financial systems and templates. REQUIREMENTS FOR THIS ROLE Proven track record of at least 5 years in an account management role preferably within an advertising agency, with a specific emphasis on social media content strategy and execution. Bachelor's degree in marketing, advertising, communications, or a related field. Proven account management and client communication skills Experience with scoping, timeline creation, project management, margin, and revenue growth in close partnership with project management, production and finance teams Knowledge of brand experience, influencer/creator marketing, social-first creative and strategy playbooks. Curiosity about clients’ business and a passion for solving business problems Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to interface with senior level professionals. Financial literacy and management of multiple budgets Demonstrated expertise and experience delivering channel assets – from up front strategy and planning through activation and execution Leading New Business Pitches Highly proficient with Google Suite (Gmail, Doc, Slides, Sheets, Drive, Meet, Chat, Gemini) and Slack. Baseline knowledge of A.I. tools and functions. Added plus: Knowledge of Asana. JOIN US At We Are Social, we celebrate diverse perspectives and experiences. We welcome unique voices and talents, especially those not traditionally embraced by advertising. Discover more about our culture and our work. Let’s do cool stuff together. Apply now! title: Summer Intern, Promotions & Events Marketing - Part Time
description: Nashville, Tennessee Internships Intern (Fixed Term) Part-Time R-2026-01-99 Hybrid Who We Are: SiriusXM and its brands (Pandora, SXM Media, AdsWizz, Simplecast, and SiriusXM Connect) are leading a new era of audio entertainment and services by delivering the most compelling subscription and ad-supported audio entertainment experience for listeners - in the car, at home, and anywhere on the go with connected devices. Our vision is to shape the future of audio, where everyone can be effortlessly connected to the voices, stories and music they love wherever they are. This is the place where a diverse group of emerging talent and legends alike come to share authentic and purposeful songs, stories, sounds and insights through some of the best programming and technology in the world. Our critically-acclaimed, industry-leading audio entertainment encompasses music, sports, comedy, news, talk, live events, and podcasting. No matter their individual role, each of our employees plays a vital part in bringing SiriusXM’s vision to life every day. SiriusXM SiriusXM is the leading audio entertainment company in North America, and the premier programmer and platform for subscription and digital advertising-supported audio products. SiriusXM’s platforms collectively reach approximately 150 million listeners, the largest digital audio audience across paid and free tiers in North America, and deliver music, sports, talk, news, comedy, entertainment and podcasts. Pandora, a subsidiary of SiriusXM, is the largest ad-supported audio entertainment streaming service in the U.S. SiriusXM's subsidiaries Simplecast and AdsWizz make it a leader in podcast hosting, production, distribution, analytics and monetization. The Company’s advertising sales organization, which operates as SXM Media, leverages its scale, cross-platform sales organization and ad tech capabilities to deliver results for audio creators and advertisers. SiriusXM, through Sirius XM Canada Holdings, Inc., also offers satellite radio and audio entertainment in Canada. In addition to its audio entertainment businesses, SiriusXM offers connected vehicle services to automakers. How you’ll make an impact: SiriusXM is looking for an intern to work cross-functionally with our programming teams to provide content marketing support for our music, entertainment, and sports verticals. What you’ll do: Assist with sending out and gathering agenda for weekly team department meetings. Familiarize themselves with partner brand guidelines and content. Brainstorm creative concepts to help drive engagement across socials and marketing outlets. Draft marketing proposals to be shared with external partners. Monitor and track contractual obligations for all partners across music, talk, and sports. Generate powerpoint presentations recapping for numerous initiatives including show and channel launches, content campaigns, and events. Assist coordinators with circulating approved assets and programming details to other lines of business using Monday.com. Assist with administrative duties such as booking conference rooms, scheduling meetings, and submitting invoices. What you’ll need: Internships are open to current students enrolled at a university (those graduating by May 2027 are preferred) and recent graduates who graduated within the 12-month period prior to the start of the internship as well as broadcasting schools with one-year programs that provide certificates of completion. Thorough knowledge of MS-Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access). PowerPoint is a must. Basic photo editing experience (i.e. photoshop and canva) is a plus. Marketing, advertising, media or communications majors preferred. Passion for music, entertainment, and sports is a must. Good project management and planning skills. Interpersonal skills and ability to interact and work with staff at all levels. Excellent written and oral communication skills. Attention to detail skills. Ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. Willingness to take initiative and to follow through on projects. Must have legal right to work in the U.S. At SiriusXM, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining compensation, including your background and experience. These considerations can cause your compensation to vary. We expect the base salary for this position to be $18/hr and will depend on your skills, qualifications, and experience. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary short-term and long-term incentives. We encourage all interested candidates to apply. Our goal at SiriusXM is to provide and maintain a work environment that fosters mutual respect, professionalism and cooperation. title: Account Manager, Digital Content
description: Account Manager, Digital Content ON BOARD EXPERIENTIAL MARKETING On Board Experiential (OBE) is an award-winning, full-service, experience-led agency that creates meaningful brand experiences worldwide. We work out of our homes and physical offices in San Francisco, Los Angeles, and NYC, or wherever our adventures take us! Our clients include Nike, Meta, JPMorganChase, The Infatuation, Stripe, and OpenAI, to name a few. We know, "Cool, cool, but what's it like to work at OBE?" We thought you'd never ask. Event Marketer has recognized OBE as one of the best places to work in events and well, we couldn't agree more. The most important part of our business is our people. They are hardworking, brave and make magic happen on the regular. But under each majestic exterior is a human deserving of a healthy work-life balance. That's why we believe in recovery days, happy hours, friendly but competitive fitness challenges and company offsites. We conduct open meetings to facilitate discussions that heal, inspire, and bring us closer. We encourage each other to bring our whole selves to work. We want to hear about your lived experiences and unique perspectives. We don't just want you to be unapologetically you: it's part of the job description. SUMMARY OF POSITION Supports projects from planning through delivery. Liaises between project members, cross-functional teams, external vendors, and other stakeholders to ensure digital content deliverables are met. Utilizes appropriate tools to manage project timelines, tasks, milestones, and deadlines. Works with Digital Project Manager to communicate schedule and changes to all stakeholders. Prepares agendas, meeting notes, and project summaries. Works with Digital Project Manager to monitor task completion status to identify at risk project tasks and communicate at risk tasks to stakeholders. Digital Content Digital Content Management – lead on the curation and trafficking of digital content related to website and email content within OBE's scope Digital Liaison - Serve as the day-to-day Digital Liaison to client for select digital products, managing communications related to digital content deliverables Support Digital Project Manager in escalating relevant timeline, scope, and technical considerations to stakeholders Email Management Lead – Lead on all participant-facing emails through email content template creation, distribution list cultivation, data refinement, and scheduling send Digital Content Liaison – Represent the account team as the digital content liaison between project members, cross-functional teams, external vendors, and other stakeholders to ensure digital content deliverables are met. Support in translating client needs, content, and feedback into clear, actionable items for the Digital Project Manager. Website and Email Marketing – Support the Digital Project Manager in the creation of marketing templates for email marketing and project websites around approved strategy and content alignment. Social Marketing Support – Support the social marketing initiatives for projects, as assigned by project Client & Account Leadership Act as a primary point of contact for internal teams, building strong, trusted relationships. Partner with teams to understand business objectives and identify opportunities to deliver measurable results. Guide development of experiential tactical plans for IRL and/or virtual programs in collaboration with Strategy, Creative, Production, Tech, Digital, and Social teams. Manage day-to-day client communications and ensure timely, clear distribution of information across internal and external teams. Provide ongoing budget tracking, expense monitoring, reconciliations, and client approvals in partnership with Producers and Finance. Deliver performance feedback and post-program evaluations to clients and senior leadership. DESIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE EDUCATION Bachelor's Degree EXPERIENCE Bachelor's degree in marketing, Business Administration, Communications, Design, or related field. 3–5 years of experience in project management, account management, or experiential marketing. Experiential marketing agency experience strongly preferred. Minimum 2 years serving as a primary client contact or account lead. Experience managing IRL and/or virtual experiential activations. ESSENTIAL KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS + ABILITIES Exceptional organizational skills with strong attention to detail and accuracy. Proven ability to manage multiple priorities, deadlines, and stakeholders simultaneously. Excellent communication skills—written, verbal, and presentation. Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to identify issues and develop effective solutions. Comfortable working cross-functionally with creative, technical, and production teams. Ability to adapt quickly to new tools, platforms, and technologies. Understanding of digital product content and marketing strategies and governance. Experience with email marketing tools and web technologies. OTHER QUALITIES + SKILLS Enthusiasm for creating and presenting compelling design decks, pitches, and client-facing materials. Passion for experiential and spatial design, consumer journey, digital interactivity, and environmental storytelling. Creative thinker who understands time management and efficient use of resources. Professional, collaborative personality that works well across all levels of an agency. High energy, stamina, and flexibility to work non-traditional hours (nights/weekends as needed). Willingness to travel up to 50% and work a hybrid schedule (2 days minimum in office). Flexibility to collaborate with OBE offices and projects across multiple time zones, which may occasionally require work outside standard business hours. Ability to lift up to 25 pounds and remain on feet for extended periods during live events. Compensation Range: $80,000 - $95,000 annually. This is the pay range OBE believes it will pay for this position at the time of this posting. Consistent with applicable law, compensation will be determined based on level of experience, relevant skills, professional certifications, market pay, and demand for the role. OBE reserves the right to modify this pay range at any time. For this role, Benefits include: medical/dental/vision insurance, employer paid basic life and personal accident insurance. Also included: Annual Profit Sharing/Bonus Plan based on the company's performance and your individual performance, 401(k), Healthcare Flexible Spending Account, Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account, PTO, STD/LTD insurance benefits.

title: Growth Marketing Manager
description: Job Title: Manager, Growth Marketing Location: New York, New York Salary: $75,000 - $95,000 Who We Are GeistM is a global full-funnel performance marketing company revolutionizing customer acquisition for brands worldwide. We specialize in scaling profitable, content-driven advertising campaigns across leading platforms including Meta, Google, TikTok, X, Taboola, Pinterest, Reddit, and more. Our teams blend creativity, data, and experimentation to drive measurable growth for category-leading brands. About the Role We are seeking a Marketing Manager to join our Growth Team. In this role, you will own the strategy, execution, and performance of a portfolio of client accounts across paid social, native, and search channels. You’ll lead account-level decision-making, guide campaign execution, and manage junior team members while working closely with Creative and Content partners to drive scale. This role is ideal for a performance marketer who is highly organized, data-driven, and confident leading both client relationships and internal account teams. What You’ll Do Account Strategy & Performance Ownership Own end-to-end account strategy for assigned clients, including planning, execution, optimization, and scaling across paid social, native, and search channels. Guide account strategy and effectively delegate tasks across account teams to maximize net revenue and performance. Confidently pitch new networks, expansion initiatives, and test budgets to unlock incremental scale. Identify and act on opportunities to optimize all layers of the GeistM funnel, from creative and targeting to landing pages and conversion flow. Maintain accountability for campaign execution, including client approvals and timely delivery of action items. Budgeting, Forecasting & Reporting Own account budgets, pacing, and net revenue projections across all networks. Optimize ads efficiently and adjust budgets to achieve strong CPA, ROAS, and scale targets. Edit, approve, and deliver daily, weekly, and monthly client-facing reports and action plans. Determine deck structure, data narratives, and insights for Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) and lead QBR discussions with clients. Role Description The Social Media Manager is a strategic and creative powerhouse responsible for the digital voice of RTEAM and its diverse portfolio of festivals, venues, and partner projects. This role oversees the complete lifecycle of social media—from high-level content strategy and cross-brand ideation to daily implementation and community management. The Social Media Manager works closely with internal teams and external festival partners to ensure RTEAM’s brands are at the forefront of the music and events industry, driving ticket sales and vibrant online engagement. Strategy, Ideation & Collaboration Content Strategy: Develop and oversee a comprehensive multi-platform content strategy for RTEAM and its network of internal and external event, venue and festival accounts. Ideation & Brainstorming: Lead creative ideation sessions to develop viral-worthy campaigns, interactive content, and unique digital experiences that set RTEAM festivals apart. Partner Network Collaboration: Act as the primary point of contact for festival partners, sponsors, and artist teams to coordinate collaborative posts, takeovers, and cross-promotional strategies. Brand Voice Maintenance: Define and maintain a distinct, authentic voice for each unique brand under the RTEAM umbrella, ensuring consistency in tone and visual aesthetic. Content Planning & Implementation Master Calendar Management: Direct the master editorial calendar, ensuring a balanced flow of announcements, lifestyle content, sponsor highlights, and ticket-drive pushes. Strategy Implementation: Execute the end-to-end posting process, utilizing scheduling tools and native platform features to maximize reach (Instagram Reels, TikTok, X, Facebook, etc.). Performance Analytics: Monitor and report on key performance indicators (KPIs), using data to refine strategy and provide actionable insights to the leadership team. Community Engagement & Management Daily Interaction: Actively engage with the community by responding to comments, direct messages, and mentions, fostering a positive and interactive environment. Customer Success Liaison: Monitor social channels for customer service issues, providing real-time answers to general questions and escalating complex ticketing or production issues to internal teams. Reputation Management: Identify and mitigate potential social media crises by escalating sensitive issues to the Event Technology Manager or CEO immediately. Digital Outreach & Partnerships Influencer Strategy: Oversee the identification and management of digital influencers and brand ambassadors to amplify festival reach through coordinated campaigns and on-site activations. Trend Monitoring: Stay ahead of social media trends, platform algorithm changes, and industry news to keep RTEAM’s content relevant and competitive. Skills Advanced Social Strategy: Proven experience in building and scaling digital communities for festivals or entertainment brands. Creative Excellence: Expert eye for visual storytelling, video trends (Short-form video/Reels), and copywriting. Project Management: Ability to manage high-volume content flows for multiple accounts simultaneously under tight deadlines. Interpersonal Communication: Skilled at collaborating with high-level stakeholders, artist managers, and corporate sponsors. Technical Proficiency:** Mastery of social media management tools (e.g., Sprout Social, Later, Canva) and basic photo/video editing software (CapCut, Adobe Suite).

title: Senior Email Marketing Manager

title: Sr. Manager, Product Marketing , AWS Product Marketing
description: DESCRIPTION Are you an experienced technologist who loves helping bring product value propositions to life via great storytelling? Do you enjoy working closely with customers and engineering teams to make sure we’re building products that will delight users? Do you like leading teams and developing people? Yes? We might have just the right role for you. AWS is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. Product Marketing is a critical part of making sure that we are building the right products for our customers and creating crisp and differentiated value propositions that form the core of our storytelling efforts. Product Marketing managers work closely with our customers, product and field account teams, 3rd party analysts, press, and many others. We synthesize multiple diverse perspectives and “connect the dots” between customers and various different AWS capabilities as we build and launch compelling new services. In this role, you will leverage your own deep experience as a technologist as you lead a team of Product Marketing Managers responsible for AWS serverless, containers, and compute infrastructure. You will enable your team to work backwards and get the right customer insights, to stay sharp and deep on industry and product trends, and to synthesize all this data into meaningful information that informs our product roadmap and gets baked into the differentiated value propositions that your team creates. Your team will define the strategy for product launch and adoption and integrate efforts more broadly with other marketing and field teams across the company so that we can maximize customer awareness, adoption, and retention of products that will exceed the expectations of users. Your team will lead the creation of the most compelling assets that tell our story whether that’s presentations, demos, videos, and so on. Your team will also need to be able to “connect the dots” between various different AWS capabilities and services to make sure that we are deliberate in building and marketing the right portfolios of offerings for customers. AWS is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform, offering over 200 fully featured services from data centers globally. You’ll have the chance to work on various different sets of these services and capabilities (including Analytics, Developer Tools, Compute, Databases, Generative AI, Observability, Security) based on your experience, interest, and aptitude – as well as on new offerings while they are being developed. We are looking for builders who have experience working directly with customers, analyzing and synthesizing data from multiple sources, and who thrive both when working closely with engineering teams to influence product outcomes and building value propositions that will educate customers on the value that technology can bring to them. In particular, we are looking for individuals who can operate at both a broad and deep level; who can understand our technology with the same depth that our customers do, and who can think like a technologist and a business leader simultaneously. Key job responsibilities Partner with engineering and product management to develop portfolio-level strategy documents to guide prioritization of AWS offerings and capabilities we build Creation of positioning and messaging artifacts that will represent our offerings at a portfolio level Analysis and synthesis of 1st and 3rd party data (including driving direct data gathering from customers) to define opportunities for our offerings Acting as an advocate for customers within AWS (both based on direct customer work and your interactions with our customer-facing teams) during the product development process Involvement in the generation of content artifacts (presentations, demos, etc.) that can be leveraged broadly to educate customers on our offerings Working closely with other AWS teams to ensure these value propositions are articulated broadly and clearly (e.g. re:Invent keynote, AWS Summits, other AWS sales and marketing channels) About the team AWS Sales, Marketing, and Global Services (SMGS) is responsible for driving revenue, adoption, and growth from the largest and fastest growing small- and mid-market accounts to enterprise-level customers including public sector. The AWS Global Support team interacts with leading companies and believes that world-class support is critical to customer success. AWS Support also partners with a global list of customers that are building mission-critical applications on top of AWS services. Why AWS Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Diverse Experiences Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Work/Life Balance We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud. Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness. Mentorship and Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional BASIC QUALIFICATIONS Experience working with customers who are deploying cloud solutions 5+ years of managing teams experience BS degree in computer science, computer engineering, or related field, or 4+ years of technical work experience Travel up to 15% of the time 7+ years working knowledge cloud-based architectures in one or more technical domains (e.g. Analytics, Developer Tools, Compute, Databases, Generative AI, Observability, Security, etc.) PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Experience communicating across technical and non-technical audiences, including executive level stakeholders or clients AWS certification, such as, AWS Solutions Architect, or a similar cloud certification, or Associate's degree or above MBA, or Master's degree in management, business administration, economics, engineering, marketing Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Los Angeles County applicants: Job duties for this position include: work safely and cooperatively with other employees, supervisors, and staff; adhere to standards of excellence despite stressful conditions; communicate effectively and respectfully with employees, supervisors, and staff to ensure exceptional customer service; and follow all federal, state, and local laws and Company policies. Criminal history may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship with some of the material job duties of this position. These include the duties and responsibilities listed above, as well as the abilities to adhere to company policies, exercise sound judgment, effectively manage stress and work safely and respectfully with others, exhibit trustworthiness and professionalism, and safeguard business operations and the Company’s reputation. Pursuant to the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several US geographic markets. The base pay for this position ranges from $162,500/year in our lowest geographic market up to $350,000/year in our highest geographic market. Pay is based on a number of factors including market location and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Amazon is a total compensation company. Dependent on the position offered, equity, sign-on payments, and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits. For more information, please visit https://www.aboutamazon.com/workplace/employee-benefits. This position will remain posted until filled. Applicants should apply via our internal or external career site.

title: Content Marketing Manager
description: Content Marketing Manager Location: El Segundo, California, USA (HQ) Reports To: Director of Marketing Role Type: Full-Time, On-Site Compensation: $75,000-$90,000 annually About QuikStor QuikStor is the leading SaaS facility management platform for the self-storage industry, delivering a purpose-built, scalable system that serves as the foundation for intelligent automation and modern facility operations. We are in rapid growth mode, backed by significant capital from self-storage industry veterans who share our vision to build the most advanced and scalable operating system for storage operators worldwide. Role Overview As Content Marketing Manager, you will lead the development and execution of QuikStor's content strategy across all owned, earned, and paid channels. You'll create compelling content that educates self-storage operators, builds brand authority, drives organic and AI-driven search visibility, and supports the full sales funnel — from awareness through retention. This is a high-ownership role suited to a self-starter who can move fluidly between big-picture strategy and hands-on content creation, and who understands how to write for both humans and AI search engines.Key Responsibilities Content Strategy & Planning Develop and manage a comprehensive content calendar aligned to marketing goals, product releases, and industry events. Define QuikStor's content voice, tone, and style guidelines and ensure consistency across all channels. Conduct audience research, keyword analysis, and competitive content audits to identify opportunities. Build content frameworks optimized for traditional SEO and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) — structuring content for AI-driven search results, featured snippets, and generative engine visibility. Blog & Website Copy Write and publish high-quality blog posts, how-to guides, and thought leadership articles targeting self-storage operators and decision-makers. Own website copy — crafting and refreshing landing pages, product pages, and conversion-focused copy that reflects the QuikStor brand. Optimize all written content for search intent, AEO formatting (structured answers, FAQ schema, etc.), and readability. Video Content Develop scripts, storyboards, and creative briefs for product demos, customer testimonials, explainer videos, and social clips. Collaborate with internal teams or external vendors to manage video production from concept through delivery. Repurpose video content into supporting assets — transcripts, blog summaries, social snippets, and email features. White Papers & Long-Form Assets Research and write authoritative white papers, industry reports, and eBooks that position QuikStor as a self-storage technology leader. Partner with subject matter experts, product managers, and sales to ensure technical accuracy and strategic alignment. Package long-form assets as gated lead-generation tools and develop supporting promotional content. Social Media Manage QuikStor's social media presence across LinkedIn, Facebook, and other relevant platforms. Develop platform-native content that drives engagement, brand awareness, and community among self-storage professionals. Monitor trends, respond to comments, and track performance metrics to continuously refine strategy. Product Marketing Collateral Create sales enablement materials including one-pagers, product sheets, brochures, comparison battlecard guides, and presentation decks. Translate complex product features into clear, benefit-driven messaging that resonates with operators of all technical levels. Collaborate with the product and sales teams to support new feature launches and go-to-market campaigns. Email Marketing Write and manage promotional and nurture email campaigns targeting prospects, current customers, and re-engagement audiences. Develop segmented messaging strategies that speak to different operator personas — from single-location owners to multi-site management companies. Analyze open rates, CTRs, and conversion data to optimize subject lines, content, and send cadence. Performance & Reporting Track and report on content KPIs including organic traffic, engagement, lead generation, and pipeline influence. Use data to inform content priorities and demonstrate ROI to stakeholders. Qualifications Required 4-7 years of B2B SaaS content marketing experience Exceptional writing, editing, and storytelling skills across multiple formats. Demonstrated experience with SEO and AEO content strategies. Hands-on experience managing social media channels for a brand. Experience writing marketing emails and managing campaign performance. Ability to produce and manage multiple content types simultaneously. Strong project management skills and ability to meet deadlines independently. Preferred Experience in the self-storage, property management, or PropTech industry. Familiarity with CMS platforms (WordPress or similar), HubSpot, ClickUp and marketing automation tools. Experience briefing, directing, or producing video content. Comfort with analytics tools — Google Analytics, Search Console, or similar. Graphic design sensibility; experience with Canva, Figma, or Adobe Suite a plus. Understanding of AI search behavior and generative engine optimization (GEO). Who You Are A natural storyteller who can make technical software feel approachable and compelling. A strategic thinker who understands how content supports pipeline and revenue, not just page views. Deeply curious about AI, search, and how buyers discover and evaluate solutions. A collaborator who can work cross-functionally with Sales, Product, and Customer Success — but self-sufficient enough to execute independently. Organized, accountable, and comfortable managing multiple projects in a fast-moving environment. Location & Travel This role is based at QuikStor headquarters in El Segundo, California. Regular on-site presence is expected. Limited travel may be required for team offsites or industry events. Andasol Management Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Andasol Management Inc. does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion or religious creed, national origin or place of origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding or related medical conditions), physical or mental disability, age, protected military or veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, genetic information, legally protected medical condition, marital or domestic partner status, status as a victim of domestic violence (including sexual assault or stalking), or any other basis protected by applicable law. Qualified applicants with criminal histories will be considered for employment in a manner consistent with all federal state and local ordinances. title: Associate, Marketing
description: JOB OVERVIEW The Marketing Associate plays a key role in supporting the company’s growth and strategic initiatives. The primary focus of this role is to manage and contribute to the development of high-quality proposal responses for Requests for Proposals/Quotation/Information (RFP/Q/Is), ensuring alignment with company messaging and client requirements. In addition, the role supports competitive intelligence efforts and helps maintain and grow relationships with buyer cooperatives. JOB LOCATION AND REPORTING TO This position will be based in either our San Francisco or New York City office, or for the right candidate as a remote position. The Marketing Associate reports to the Senior Director, Marketing and works closely with Commercial team, Technical Development Team (TDM), and other various departments across the organization. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES RFP Response Coordination and Development Lead and coordinate the preparation of RFP responses, collaborating with internal stakeholders to gather necessary content and approvals. Draft, edit, and format proposal documents to ensure clarity, consistency, and compliance with submission requirements. Ensure certificates of insurance, contractor licenses, DIR registrations, and Mitsui financial info is all up to date on responses. Maintain and update a centralized repository of RFP content, templates, and past submissions. Track RFP opportunities and manage timelines to ensure timely and complete submissions. Serve as a point of contact for clients regarding RFP inquiries by attending Pre-bid meetings and submitting clarifying questions to the RFPs Competitive Intelligence & FOIA Work to find relevant and useful intel for the Commercial team to leverage in putting together more competitive proposals. Submit and manage Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests to obtain competitive data and insights. Organize, track, and analyze FOIA responses/research to identify trends, pricing benchmarks, and strategic opportunities. Share relevant findings with internal teams to inform business development and positioning strategies. Buyer-Cooperative Partnership Support Assist in managing relationships with key buyer cooperatives and purchasing consortiums. Request and manage the updated members' lists regularly Set up sales training sessions periodically with team Support the creation of marketing materials and presentations tailored to cooperative audiences and align co-marketing activities/events. Monitor cooperative engagement activities and help identify opportunities for deeper collaboration. Support contract finalization for cooperative programs. Forward any incoming inquiries to the appropriate sales managers and assist with initial conversations Operational & Misc. Manage subscriptions and platform services that support RFP Response (GovWin, BidPrime, Inventive.ai, etc.) Responsible for sharing information via SharePoint to the broader organization Misc. marketing team support, as needed QUALIFICATIONS We are looking for highly motivated individuals who want to be part of a fast-paced and fluid company poised for growth in one of the country’s most rapidly changing industries. Forward-thinking, innovative approaches and comfort with complexity are what define the ForeFront Power team. We are looking for individuals eager to run at the front with us. Strong written communication and editing skills. Excellent organizational and project management abilities. Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple deadlines. Familiarity with public procurement processes and FOIA practices. Collaborative mindset with the ability to work across departments. Proficiency in Microsoft Word and other Office applications is required, especially managing large documents with citations Proficiency or experience in Adobe InDesign is not required but may be a plus. Extremely proficient Excel, Word and PowerPoint skills. Desire to work in a growing company in the renewable energy field. PHYSICAL DEMANDS This position is primarily sedentary in nature and requires prolonged periods of sitting at a desk in front of a computer. There may also be opportunities to travel to support Commercial or Corporate events (~5%). The Marketing Associate should be comfortable traveling to various sites including tradeshows, or construction sites and being around heavy equipment. WORK ENVIRONMENT AND CULTURE The organization promotes an open and collaborative work atmosphere. Cross functional teams work closely together to solve problems and move the company forward. Our culture at ForeFront Power encourages employees to be authentic and to embrace our core values that celebrate passion for our work, a commitment to our planet, a winning attitude, an entrepreneurial spirit, and a community of respect for each other and our partners. BENEFITS This is a regular, full-time position eligible for benefits. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including competitive base salary and bonus potential; health, vision, and dental coverage; commute assistance; professional development; Paid Time Off; and 401(k) with employer match. ABOUT FOREFRONT POWER** ForeFront Power is a leading developer of commercial and industrial-scale (C&I) solar energy and battery storage projects in the U.S. and Mexico, also offering vehicle fleet electrification services. Over 15 years of working together, the ForeFront Power team has developed more than 1,900 behind-the-meter and community solar projects, totaling more than 1.6 gigawatt-DC of renewable electricity. ForeFront Power serves business, government, education, healthcare and community solar customers with a broad array of development, asset management and advisory services from its San Francisco headquarters and via teams based in New York, Mexico City, and across the U.S. through a hybrid work model. A wholly owned subsidiary of global energy infrastructure and investment leader Mitsui & Co. Ltd., ForeFront Power operates under Mitsui’s North American investment arm, MyPower Corp. Mitsui holds a robust balance sheet and an “A” credit rating from Standard & Poor’s. ForeFront Power is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity of thought, gender, and race. We believe our continued success depends upon the equal treatment of all employees and applicants for employment without discrimination on the basis of race, religion, creed, color, national origin, sex, gender, age, mental or physical disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship status, military or veteran status, pregnancy, or any other classification protected by country, federal, state, or local law.

title: Manager, Creator Marketing
description: We are in relentless pursuit of an equitable and inspiring workplace that is respectful of all, reflects and represents the world in which we live, and fosters trust, collaboration and belonging. Consistent with this approach, we hire the best qualified candidates for all positions. We’re looking for a Manager , Creator Marketing to execute best-in-class creator campaigns across a range of clients and industries. This role sits within our Creator Marketing team and is ideal for someone who thrives on owning creator partnerships from start to finish — from strategy and outreach to contracting, briefing, and performance analysis. You’ll be at the intersection of brand partnerships, digital culture, and content strategy, developing campaigns that are both impactful and culturally relevant. Whether it’s a seasonal product moment or an always-on brand storytelling effort, you’ll play a key role in crafting campaigns that break through with authenticity and creativity. This role is perfect for someone who balances operational precision with creative instincts — someone who’s just as comfortable managing contracts and timelines as they are coming up with unexpected creator moments that spark conversation. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: Own end-to-end execution of creator marketing campaigns, from initial outreach and vetting through contracting, briefing, and performance analysis with little oversight. Communicate with creators, talent managers, and agents to ensure timely deliverables and alignment with brand goals. Co-develop and implement strategies that align with client KPIs, target audience insights, and platform trends. Create detailed creator briefs and host creator briefing calls, ensuring all campaign deliverables, timelines, and messaging guidelines are communicated clearly with little oversight. Negotiate competitive rates and contract terms with talent, balancing value with client budgets and ROI goals with the assistance of business affairs. Collaborate cross-functionally with brand strategy, paid media, and creative teams to integrate creators seamlessly into broader marketing initiatives. Stay ahead of creator trends, cultural moments, and platform innovations to identify new partnership opportunities. Develop and maintain strong relationships with creators, managers, and talent agents across all tiers. Mentor junior team members, providing oversight and training on best-in-class influencer marketing execution. Preferred Knowledge/Skills: Proven track record managing large creator campaigns end-to-end, including sourcing, negotiating, contracting, and performance reporting. Knowledge of the creator landscape across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and emerging platforms. Strong negotiation skills and experience working directly with creators, agents, and managers. Exceptional project management abilities with the capacity to juggle multiple campaigns and stakeholders simultaneously. Understanding of FTC guidelines and legal considerations for paid partnerships. Passion for pop culture, digital trends, and emerging voices in the creator economy. Experience ideating and executing creator-led events is a plus Highly organized and excel at multitasking in a fast-paced environment Experience using creator data platforms such as Captiv8, CreatorIQ , etc. Experience leveraging data to optimize content, inform strategic pivots and drive measurable results. While experience with AI tools like ChatGPT and Copilot isn't a pre-requisite, a desire to create efficiencies through AI integration into workflows is. 3+ years of experience in influencer/creator marketing, talent management, or branded content — preferably within an agency or brand environment. $60,000 - $80,000 per year #LI-IR1 An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, any contractual agreements, and business or organizational needs. The range listed is just one component of DJEH’s total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include annual bonuses, a Paid Time Off policy, and region-specific benefits. DJEH offers a wide range of benefits: medical and dental insurance, vision, 401K, life insurance, disability insurance, paid time off, travel assistance and wellness programing. DJEH is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and believes in diversity, equity, and inclusion. We seek applications from all qualified candidates without regard to race, color, gender, sex, age, religion, physical or mental disability, military and veteran status, or any other basis protected by federal, state or local law. If you require a reasonable accommodation in any part of the employment process, please let us know.

title: Director, Content Strategy & Operations (Remote)
description: We believe in the power and joy of learning At Cengage Group, our employees have a direct impact in helping students around the world discover the power and joy of learning. To learn more, please see https://www.cengagegroup.com/about/inclusion-and-belonging/ . At Milady, we’re redefining trades education for the next generation of professionals. As the Director, Content Strategy & Operations, you’ll be the function leader in charge of evolving our learning experiences into intuitive, impactful digital experiences that support students, educators, and schools nationwide while innovating content production processes that introduce efficiency and scale. This role requires a dynamic leader with a deep understanding of digital metrics, audience behavior, and content strategy. What you'll do here: Leadership & Team Management Lead the content and operations function within Milady, through Portfolio, Content & Learning and Production teams. Set the strategic vision for subject area portfolios, content development and operational execution, aligned to company goals and market needs. Partner with cross-functional Directors of Product, Marketing and Sales to build a comprehensive content and production strategy that enhances customer engagement and drives digital user growth. Drive innovation in content strategy and operations, increasing engagement and annual number of learners reached. Regularly analyze product and portfolio plans to align forecasting and measurement of progress towards goals; deliver cases for strategic investment. Content Operations Orchestrate multi-channel content operations—from planning, production, distribution, and measurement to optimization Identify new ways to use technology to decrease content creation timelines, ensuring learners always have access to relevant learning experiences Design regional and global content strategies that drive engagement and loyalty Deliver content and learning experiences that drive positive outcomes for learners Voice of Customer Lead your teams to apply customer centric methodology to identify customer problems and validate potential solutions Create customer driven, monetizable content opportunities Culture of Excellence Ensure high standards and integrity across all content, fostering a culture of accuracy, relevancy and speed. Ensure relevance by staying informed of regulatory landscape, industry trends and technology evolution Skills you will need here: 10+ years leading content strategy and operations teams, including significant experience in AI enabled content production. Outcomes: Shown success in using content strategies to deliver revenue growth Analytical Skills: Proficiency in digital analytics tools and a strong knowledge of digital KPIs in the learning environment Technical Proficiency: Experience with LMS platforms, content management systems, and content production departments. Experience using AI for content development a plus. Leadership: Strong editorial judgment, leadership, and communication skills with the ability to inspire and lead a complex team. Adaptability: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, evolving environment and adapt to changing technologies and audience behaviors Innovation: Passion for innovation, experimentation, and continuous improvement in news delivery B.S. or proven experience Expected travel ~15% Cengage Group is committed to working with broad talent pools to attract and hire strong and most qualified individuals. If you are an applicant with a disability and require reasonable accommodation in our job application process, please contact us at accommodations.ta@cengage.com . About Cengage Group Cengage Group, a global education technology company serving millions of learners, provides affordable, quality digital products and services that equip students with the skills and competencies needed to be job ready. For more than 100 years, we have enabled the power and joy of learning with trusted, engaging content, and now, integrated digital platforms. We serve the higher education, workforce skills, secondary education, English language teaching and research markets worldwide. Through our scalable technology, including MindTap and Cengage Unlimited, we support all learners who seek to improve their lives and achieve their dreams through education. title: Product Marketing Manager, Google Cloud, Developer Marketing
description: Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. In accordance with Washington state law, we are highlighting our comprehensive benefits package, which is available to all eligible US based employees. Benefits for this role include: Health, dental, vision, life, disability insurance Retirement Benefits: 401(k) with company match Paid Time Off: 20 days of vacation per year, accruing at a rate of 6.15 hours per pay period for the first five years of employment Sick Time: 40 hours/year (statutory, where applicable); 5 days/event (discretionary) Maternity Leave (Short-Term Disability + Baby Bonding): 28-30 weeks Baby Bonding Leave: 18 weeks Holidays: 13 paid days per year Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: New York, NY, USA; Kirkland, WA, USA; Reston, VA, USA; Seattle, WA, USA; Sunnyvale, CA, USA; San Francisco, CA, USA . Minimum qualifications: Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. 6 years of experience in product marketing for a technical product, with a focus on marketing software to developers. Experience in the developer ecosystem. Experience creating product narratives for a technical audience. Preferred qualifications: Experience in technology and innovation, with a focus on empowering developers to build the future. Knowledge of AI and ML, with a potential focus on generative AI, along with an understanding of the unique needs of the developer audience. Ability to operate in a rapidly changing industry and solve problems. Ability to leverage technical and business acumen to interface with teams at all levels, including engineering, product, and marketing. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, prioritize, and meet critical deadlines. Excellent written and verbal communication, organizational, leadership, and problem-solving skills. About the job As a Product Marketing Manager on the Developer Marketing team for Google Cloud, you will be at the forefront of the dramatic shift in how applications are developed, published, and distributed. You will drive awareness, adoption, and usage of Google Cloud products among developers. You will be responsible for defining the marketing strategy, crafting compelling messaging, and creating content that resonates with developers and helps them succeed with certain Google Cloud products. This is a highly collaborative position that requires an understanding of the developer audience and the ability to translate technical features into clear, benefit-driven value propositions. You will have project management skills that are needed to orchestrate marketing execution across campaigns, social media, customer programs, events, and more.Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google's products solve the world's problems--from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And we approach marketing in a way that only Google can--changing the game, redefining the medium, making the user the priority, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself. The US base salary range for this full-time position is $137,000-$201,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google . Responsibilities Define and execute go-to-market strategies to increase adoption of Google Cloud products among developers, including positioning, messaging, and most effective channels for reaching developers. Create marketing campaigns to drive awareness and product usage; build assets for social, blogs, events, etc. that resonate with developers. Work cross-functionally to build launch strategies for new products, execute these strategies, and ensure products are positioned effectively. Build narratives and content for key events, such as Cloud Next and Google I/O, working with other Google developer marketing teams. Understand the full scope of developers’ needs, market trends, and the engaged landscape. Become a domain expert, with in-depth knowledge of how developers use certain products. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. title: Partner Marketing Manager
description: WHAT YOU'LL DO At Seakeeper, marketing isn’t an afterthought but a strategic player in what we do and how we do it. As our Partner Marketing Manager, you’ll lead the strategy and execution of marketing initiatives that support our OEMs, dealers, brokers, and marketing partners across the Seakeeper and Seakeeper Ride brands. This role is built for a relationship-driven marketer who thrives on collaboration, creativity, and strategic coordination—turning partnerships into measurable growth opportunities. You’ll manage co-branded marketing campaigns, develop engaging partner content, and amplify partner success stories through digital channels, newsletters, and events. You’ll also oversee influencer relationships, ensuring all partnership activity aligns with brand strategy and drives real business impact. In this role, you’ll: Lead the development and execution of partner-driven marketing campaigns, aligning with both OEM and dealer objectives Support partner launch initiatives and product integrations through co-branded content, digital campaigns, and promotional toolkits Manage co-op programs for OEM and dealer partners, ensuring brand consistency and marketing impact, tracking shared costs and resources across initiatives and advertising efforts Develop and manage new partner spotlight features and success stories for web, social, and email Coordinate and assist in content creation for the Dealer Newsletter (Ride) and Broker Newsletter (Gyro) to share updates, opportunities, and insights Collaborate cross-functionally to maintain website updates for dealer onboarding, partner center access, and database listings Manage Seakeeper Ride’s influencer program and creator partnerships, ensuring streamlined processes are followed for product compatibility checks and install coordination, content scheduling, and overall execution Identify new partnership opportunities to expand Seakeeper and Seakeeper Ride’s reach within the marine, outdoor, and lifestyle categories Act as a direct contact and campaign manager for Sales & Marketing initiatives and requests directed at partner audiences Partner with and support the Aftermarket Sales Reps and OEM Account Managers to ensure Sales & Marketing efforts align with B2B channel priorities and generate measurable results Track campaign performance, partner engagement, and ROI to inform future strategy Collaborate closely with the rest of the Growth Marketing, Brand & Creative, and Events teams WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED You’re a connector, a collaborator, and a campaign builder, who loves turning partnerships into performance. You bring the mindset of a strategist and the hands-on drive of a marketer. You understand that the best partnerships are built on trust and shared success, and you have the organization, creativity, and initiative to bring ideas to life across multiple brands and partners. Here's a few other things you'll need to succeed. MUST-HAVES 2-4 years of marketing experience, with at least 3 years focused on partnership, B2B, or campaign marketing Strong, proactive project management skills with experience juggling multiple campaigns, priorities, and stakeholders—confident working across teams and driving ideas from strategy to execution Experience working with influencers, content creators, ambassadors, and brand partners A collaborative, adaptable, energized, self-starter and entrepreneurial mindset, passionate about partnership-driven growth and ready to engage and present to a wide range of internal and external stakeholders Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, advertising, or a related field NICE-TO-HAVES Experience in the marine industry and/or an interest in boating Background in supporting events, experiential activations, or co-branded initiatives Familiarity with co-op marketing programs MORE DETAILS YOU'LL WANT TO KNOW You’ll be part of a fast-paced, high-performing Growth Marketing team that covers Digital Marketing, Social Media, and Channel Marketing You’ll report to the Director of Growth Marketing Travel may be required up to ~30% for industry events, team gatherings, and campaign activations This is an onsite role based in Ft. Myers, FL or Leesport, PA WHY YOU’LL LOVE IT HERE It’s true that we make extraordinary products, but our favorite part about Seakeeper is our people! We love the culture we have built and are aggressively protective of our team atmosphere. This is why we value your cultural fit as equally as we value your technical contributions. With a palpable passion for what we do, we work hard, move fast and are constantly pushing (or should we say crushing) boundaries. That means there is no room for jerks! We operate with the nimbleness and growth mindset of a scrappy startup. Throughout the organization, we take ownership and accountability for our ultimate team success by openly and actively seeking out constructive feedback on how we can improve. We believe great ideas can strike at any moment, and when you have one, you’re empowered to speak up! Fast-paced and hands-on don’t even begin to describe what you’ll experience here. That means you have the freedom to make a difference and contribute to the larger goal, regardless of your position. The only constant at Seakeeper is change, and we thrive on it! WHO WE ARE 71% of our Earth is covered by water and we want everyone to make the most of it. That’s why we are on a mission to create products that transform the boating experience with an organization that employees want to work for, customers want to buy from, and vendors want to partner with. Founded in 2008, we have grown from a start-up operation to the worldwide leader in marine motion control. The journey began with our gyrostabilization technology, which eliminates up to 90% of boat roll and creates a land-like experience on the water. This technology went from an unknown commodity to a must-have boating requirement in the first decade. In 2022, we added another jaw-dropping technology to our portfolio with the launch of Seakeeper Ride, a first-of-its-kind Vessel Attitude Control System which eliminates underway pitch and roll, making time on the water safer and more comfortable for everyone onboard. In summary, we are in the business of creating gravity-defying products that completely change the way people spend their time on the water…and we are just getting started! We have long runways with both of our technologies with new addressable markets on the horizon and drive to push the boundaries of what our technologies can do. With dedicated focus to each segment, in addition to shared leadership of each vertical, we can balance the different demands of each segment while providing a consist overall vision and direction of the company. Ready for a new challenge in a fast-paced environment? Want to help us disrupt an industry? Come on and apply, we are ready for you! Sign up to receive email updates about Seakeeper’s current open job opportunities: www.seakeeper.com/job-sign-up Seakeeper is personally committed to building an inclusive and diverse workforce. We are an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer/Affirmative Action Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status, genetic information, or any other protected characteristic under applicable law. All employment is decided on the basis of job requirements, individual qualifications, and business need.

title: Creative Strategist
description: About Fay At Fay, our mission is to make nutrition a foundational pillar of preventative care, improving outcomes, accessibility, and affordability at scale. We're building AI-powered infrastructure to transform preventative healthcare. Think that's bold? We're just getting started. Today, Fay is a three-sided marketplace connecting providers, patients, and payers to revolutionize preventative care delivery. Our b2b2c business-in-a-box solution empowers dietitians to deliver high-quality, affordable nutrition care, helps patients take control of their health, and enables payers to build healthier member populations. Nutrition is our starting point, not the finish line. The platform we're building will expand into new areas of preventative health and wellness as we grow. We're proud to be one of the fastest-growing companies in healthtech, backed by category-defining investors like General Catalyst, Forerunner, 1984, and Goldman Sachs. Ready to leverage AI-driven technology to transform preventative care and make a real-world impact on health? Let's talk. About this Role We are looking for a performance-loving Creative Strategist to join our team, serving as the creative lead for our advertising and broader marketing efforts. This individual will collaborate closely with internal teams, external partners, video editors, and graphic designers to ideate and bring creative ideas to life. You will run countless experiments, and develop a creative playbook to help scale our Meta, TikTok and other paid advertising channels. The ideal candidate has experience developing innovative marketing concepts that drive incremental and scalable performance on key digital ad platforms. This individual is an imaginative thinker, with a desire to integrate creativity and strategy together to drive measurable results. A successful candidate will be on a mission to understand all things about consumer behavior and emerging trends, analyze that data and leverage it to bring a valuable perspective to the table. Beyond technical prowess, an ideal candidate will possess exceptional communication skills, effortlessly articulating complex ideas proactively and consistently. What you'll be doing Serve as the creative leader at the crossroads of performance marketing and creative strategy Lead the conception process to devise innovative campaign concepts and strategies. Transform ideas into detailed creative briefs for our internal/external creative teams to articulate creative visions effectively for ad execution Develop and own a comprehensive testing roadmap for new creative concepts, ensuring a continuous cycle of innovation Collaborate with the performance and creative team to test, iterate, optimize, and scale winning campaign creatives and messaging Provide clear direction for copy, wireframing landing pages, working with creator/UGC talent, and managing video editors Monitor and analyze ad platform data, market insights, overall performance of creative content, and the competitor landscape to derive insights and guide the development of new briefs and angles for each product Keep abreast of industry trends, social media platform updates, and best practices and identify core opportunities for future performance marketing campaigns Play a quarterback role in project managing and collaborating with cross-functional teams. Work closely with brand, design, and media planning teams to ensure timely and seamless campaign execution. Qualifications 5+ years of experience working in a creative strategy or performance marketing role Strong idea generation and creative problem-solving skills. Strong knowledge of the creative production process, briefing, and ad creation Strong copywriter and can write effective, consumer-centric copy. Strong analytical skills, with familiarity of performance metrics and performance funnels across platforms Applicants should have a drive to understand 'What makes a winning ad?' The best companies are made of the best people. There's no shortage of work ahead, but we stay balanced and look forward to celebrating our wins as a team. See our careers page here to learn more about working on our team. Fay is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
