title: Pre-Sale Content Strategist Director
description: About the Team: The Trust is the award-winning brand marketing and content studio of Dow Jones, publisher of The Wall Street Journal , Barron's and MarketWatch . We create credible, insight-driven storytelling for the world's most influential brands—grounded in journalistic integrity, powered by data intelligence, and distributed across Dow Jones's global platforms. About the Role: We are seeking an Editorial Pre-Sale Content Strategist — a senior storyteller with newsroom experience who can translate complex ideas into editorially sharp, commercially effective narratives. This is a role for a former journalist or editor who understands how to craft stories with authority, precision, and reader insight, then adapt them to serve brand objectives without losing credibility. You will act as the editorial conscience of the pre-sale process: shaping the narratives, tone, and storytelling frameworks that define The Trust's campaigns across sectors and formats. At The Trust , we believe the best brand stories start with editorial thinking. As a Pre-Sale Content Strategist, you will ensure every idea we take to market carries the weight, clarity, and authority that only great journalism can bring — helping global brands communicate with intelligence, empathy, and impact. You Will: Lead the editorial development of pre-sale content strategies, ensuring every proposal reflects the rigour, intelligence, and clarity associated with The Wall Street Journal . Distill client objectives into editorially led ideas that align with our audience's interests and the cultural moment. Partner closely with Client Partners, Integrated Marketing, and Insights teams to identify storylines that feel both commercially resonant and newsroom-credible. Craft editorial frameworks, narratives, and headlines that set the tone for multi-platform campaigns — from feature-style content and data storytelling to video scripts and live event narratives. Act as editor-in-chief of the pre-sale phase: reviewing language, structure, and creative positioning for coherence and authenticity. Work across verticals — from business, finance, and technology to sustainability, luxury, and lifestyle — connecting global themes and industry insights into compelling content platforms. Present ideas to clients with authority and narrative clarity, translating newsroom concepts into brand opportunities. Mentor junior writers and content producers, raising editorial standards and helping embed newsroom sensibility across the commercial organisation. You Have: You have 12+ years of experience in journalism, branded content, or editorial strategy within a major newsroom, publisher, or high-end content environment. You have held roles such as senior editor, bureau chief, feature writer, or editorial strategist, and are fluent in both narrative craft and commercial context. You are an exceptional writer and conceptual thinker who can make complex ideas simple, relevant, and persuasive. You bring an instinct for credibility and tone, ensuring all work feels authentic to The Wall Street Journal 's editorial voice and standards. You are commercially curious, audience-driven, and understand how brand narratives can be built without compromising journalistic integrity. You thrive in a collaborative, fast-moving, global environment — bridging editorial and marketing worlds with ease. Our Benefits: Comprehensive Healthcare Plans Paid Time Off Retirement Plans Comprehensive Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance Plans Education Benefits Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave Family Care Benefits Commuter Transit Program Subscription Discounts Employee Referral Program Reasonable accommodation: Dow Jones, Making Careers Newsworthy - We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. EEO/Disabled/Vets. Dow Jones is committed to providing reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities, in our job application and/or interview process. If you need assistance or accommodation in completing your application, due to a disability, email us at talentresourceteam@dowjones.com. Please put "Reasonable Accommodation" in the subject line and provide a brief description of the type of assistance you need. This inbox will not be monitored for application status updates. Business Area: Dow Jones - Media Sales Job Category: Editorial/Journalism Union Status: Non-Union role Pay Range: $160,000 - $200,000 We recognize that attracting the best talent is key to our strategy and success as a company.As a result, we aim for flexibility in structuring competitive compensation offers to ensure we are able to attract the best candidates.The quoted salary range represents our good faith estimate as to what our ideal candidates are likely to expect, and we tailor our offers within the range based on the selected candidate's experience, industry knowledge, location, technical and communication skills, and other factors that may prove relevant during the interview process. Pay-for-performance is a key element in our strategy to attract, engage, and motivate talented people to do their best work. Similarly to salary, for bonus eligible roles, targets are set based on a variety of factors including competitive market practice. For benefits eligible roles, in addition to cash compensation, the company provides a comprehensive and highly competitive benefits package, with a variety of physical health, retirement and savings, caregiving, emotional wellbeing, transportation, and other benefits, including "elective" benefits employees may select to best fit the needs and personal situations of our diverse workforce.. Since 1882, Dow Jones has been finding new ways to bring information to the world’s top business entities. Beginning as a niche news agency in an obscure Wall Street basement, Dow Jones has grown to be a worldwide news and information powerhouse, with prestigious brands including The Wall Street Journal, Dow Jones Newswires, Factiva, Barron’s, MarketWatch and Financial News. This longevity and success is due to a relentless pursuit of accuracy, depth and innovation, enhanced by the wisdom of past experience and a solid grasp on the future ahead. More than its individual brands, Dow Jones is a modern gateway to intelligence, with innovative technology, advanced data feeds, integrated solutions, expert research, award-winning journalism and customizable apps and delivery systems to bring the information that matters most to customers, when and where they need it, every day. title: Brand Marketing Manager
description: Company Description Blue Standard Inc. is an innovative leader in sustainable material science and eco-friendly manufacturing, with close to three decades of expertise in creating scalable, renewable solutions. The company is committed to reducing waste and CO2 emissions through the use of recycled and renewable resources, while designing high-quality products aimed at disrupting market norms and driving positive environmental impact. Guided by circular economy principles and verified through third-party life cycle analysis, Blue Standard ensures transparency and accountability in its sustainable practices. Additionally, the company emphasizes ethical business practices, compliance with strict labor regulations, and global reforestation efforts. Blue Standard Inc. is dedicated to delivering sustainable products and creating a healthier, safer, and more sustainable future. Brand Marketing Manager (CPG, B2B + Global) Location: New York City (Hybrid) About the Role We are seeking a Brand Marketing Manager to lead brand marketing across Blue Standard Inc. and BetterBone , a consumer brand developed from Blue Standard’s proprietary material science and manufacturing platform . Blue Standard is a sustainability-driven material science and manufacturing company that develops innovative materials and scalable manufacturing solutions, which power consumer and B2B brands—including BetterBone. This role is responsible for connecting the platform, the materials, and the brands into clear, compelling stories that resonate across consumer, retail, wholesale, and B2B audiences. You will work closely with teams in North America and Europe to ensure consistent brand expression and effective go-to-market execution across global sales channels. This is a hands-on role ideal for a marketer who enjoys brand ownership, storytelling, and execution across both consumer brands and B2B platforms . title: Content Design Producer - Apple Services
description: Summary Apple, new ideas have a way of becoming extraordinary products, services, and customer experiences very quickly. You bring passion and dedication to your job and there's no telling what you could accomplish. We are seeking a Content Design Producer for Apple Services. Do you excel at orchestrating complex content lifecycles, and are you passionate about bringing exceptional digital experiences to life? As a Content Design Producer, you will be a pivotal force within Apple Services, guiding innovative content from conception to launch for applications used by millions. This role invites you to lead and collaborate across diverse teams, defining and standardizing the processes that ensure seamless delivery and unparalleled quality. If you thrive in a dynamic, high-impact environment and are driven by the opportunity to shape the future of content production at Apple, we invite you to explore this truly impactful role. Description As a Content Design Producer, your days will be dynamic, deeply impactful, and centered on the seamless delivery of world-class content. Your role is a blend of strategic planning, hands-on execution, and collaborative leadership. Responsibilities Orchestrating Content Lifecycles: You will own and standardize the end-to-end app release lifecycles, from initial planning and initiation to execution, launch, and post-release retrospectives. This means you'll define project scopes, set timelines, and ensure all stakeholders are aligned. Leading and Collaborating: You’ll be instrumental in training and guiding other producers, sharing best practices, and fostering a culture of excellence. Expect to develop and deliver workshops or documentation that elevate the team's capabilities. Forging Cross-Functional Partnerships: A significant part of your day will involve collaborating closely with Design, Engineering, QA, UI, and Product Marketing teams. You’ll act as a crucial bridge, translating technical requirements into creative needs and vice versa, ensuring smooth communication and execution. Driving System Innovation: You'll leverage data and cross-functional input to continuously develop and refine scalable content management systems (CMS) and delivery pipelines. This might involve defining new tooling requirements, optimizing existing workflows, or proposing new technical solutions to enhance efficiency without sacrificing quality. Navigating Complexity: You will regularly tackle ambiguous, complex challenges using a proactive, empathetic, and solution-oriented approach. This means leading discussions, identifying roadblocks, and charting clear paths forward with positive leadership. Ensuring Quality and Timeliness: Your ultimate deliverable is the timely, high-quality release of content across various applications, ensuring it meets Apple's rigorous standards and delights our users. You'll be responsible for the health and efficiency of these content pipelines. Minimum Qualifications 5+ years of experience in technical project/program management or production/operations management within large-scale media, design, or software industries, with a focus on scaling content delivery for products serving millions of users. Extensive experience working with large asset pipelines, complex data sets, and robust Content Management Systems (CMS). Experience defining tasks, planning sprints, and providing training to scale production efforts. Strong cross-functional collaboration experience, partnering effectively with Engineering, Design, QA, and Product Marketing teams. Experience working with professional creative software suites used for media design, art direction, and audio/video/image/FX generation and editing. Experience introducing or transitioning teams to Agile and continuous development frameworks, utilizing a variety of project management tools and software. Experience with project/feature roadmapping and quarterly planning. Experience with Apple Creative Studio (e.g., Final Cut Pro, Logic Pro, Keynote, Pages, Numbers) Preferred Qualifications Experience supporting Creative Design and Engineering teams, with the ability to translate technical and creative requirements bidirectionally. Highly organized with a proven ability to manage multiple concurrent projects and maintain meticulous attention to detail amidst complex data. Decisiveness to drive toward well-reasoned operating assumptions, coupled with the adaptability to regularly assess new information for course correction and optimal outcomes. Self-motivated and proactive, consistently seeking new ways to experiment, improve processes, and deliver exceptional value. Deep understanding of technical systems and tooling related to content production and integration, enabling expert guidance and strategic problem-solving. Proven ability to navigate ambiguous and complex challenges effectively, demonstrating strong problem-solving skills and a solution-oriented mindset. A strong desire to continuously learn, grow, and evolve, actively seeking new methodologies and improvements to enhance workflows and outcomes. Pay & Benefits** At Apple, base pay is one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This provides the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role. The base pay range for this role is between $161,300 and $242,500, and your base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Apple employees also have the opportunity to become an Apple shareholder through participation in Apple’s discretionary employee stock programs. Apple employees are eligible for discretionary restricted stock unit awards, and can purchase Apple stock at a discount if voluntarily participating in Apple’s Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You’ll also receive benefits including: Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits, a range of discounted products and free services, and for formal education related to advancing your career at Apple, reimbursement for certain educational expenses — including tuition. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary bonuses or commission payments as well as relocation. Learn more about Apple Benefits. Note: Apple benefit, compensation and employee stock programs are subject to eligibility requirements and other terms of the applicable plan or program. Apple is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity. We seek to promote equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics. Learn more about your EEO rights as an applicant. Apple accepts applications to this posting on an ongoing basis.

title: Senior Manager, Marketing
description: Winning the race to bring new healthcare products safely to a waiting world drives Rapid Micro Biosystems (RMB) to combine today’s innovative technologies as never before. Your career at RMB puts you at the center of diverse global teams that span robotics, AI, Machine Learning, imaging, microbiology, and more, re-shaping how urgently needed pharmaceuticals are made, tested, and released for decades to come. The sky’s the limit. Careers at RMB are fast-moving, with the high growth you’d expect from a world leader in microbiology automation. Advancement at RMB affords an opportunity to achieve your personal goals and develop your passions, in an inclusive environment where every employee has the resources and opportunities to hone their skills. You’ll do more, learn more, and have the ability to make a profound impact on our business. The Senior Marketing Manager is a strategic, data‑driven leader responsible for developing and executing high‑impact marketing strategies that accelerate brand growth, customer acquisition, and revenue. This role requires a forward‑thinking marketer who is fluent in AI‑powered marketing technologies and experienced in building Account‑Based Marketing (ABM) programs that drive measurable pipeline influence. The ideal candidate blends creativity with analytical rigor and thrives in a fast‑paced, cross‑functional environment. This role is based in Lexington, MA US and reports to the Director of Marketing. ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS Leverage AI‑driven insights, predictive analytics, and automation to enhance targeting, personalization, and campaign performance. Identify market opportunities, customer insights, and competitive trends to guide decision‑making. Build annual and quarterly marketing plans with clear KPIs and performance targets. Lead the strategy and execution of ABM programs across key segments and target accounts. Partner with Sales to define account tiers and personalized engagement strategies. Develop multi‑channel ABM campaigns that increase account penetration, accelerate deal cycles, and expand customer value. Measure ABM effectiveness through account engagement, pipeline influence, and revenue impact. Lead multi‑channel campaigns across digital, content, events, and paid media. Use AI tools to optimize audience segmentation, creative testing, and real‑time performance adjustments. Ensure consistent brand messaging across all touchpoints. Use data and AI‑powered analytics platforms to evaluate campaign effectiveness and inform future strategy. Own marketing dashboards, reporting, and forecasting. Optimize spend across channels to maximize ROI. Oversee relationships with agencies, freelancers, and technology partners, including AI and ABM platform vendors. Coordinate the design of promotional material and distribute through online and offline channels. Network with industry experts and potential customers to drive brand awareness. Gather customer feedback to inform sales and product teams. Support team needs for internal meetings and deliverables. Skilled in managing a modern marketing technology stack PREPARATION, KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & ABILITIES Proven work experience as a Senior Marketing Manager or similar role Familiarity with B2B and B2C advertising campaigns Experience with CRM, ABM platforms, and modern marketing tech stacks Demonstrated expertise in Account‑Based Marketing strategy and execution Hands‑on experience with AI‑powered marketing tools (e.g., predictive analytics, personalization engines, automation platforms) Proficient in HubSpot and Pardot Knowledge of SEO and Google Analytics Strong analytical skills with experience using marketing analytics tools Experience managing global or multi‑market campaigns Excellent communication, leadership, and project‑management abilities. Highly organized and driven Eager to continuously learn and grow Strong writing skills to translate complicated technical jargon into customer-facing materials Minimum BSc in Marketing, Communications, Public Relations or relevant field 5+ years of progressive marketing experience WORKING CONDITIONS/PHYSICAL DEMANDS Flexible to travel less than 20% Experience working with international businesses Rapid Micro Biosystems is proud to offer a competitive total compensation package designed to attract, inspire and retain the best talent in our industry. In addition to base pay, our full-time regular employees are eligible for an attractive benefits package, cash incentive opportunities, and an equity grant. The base annual salary hiring range for this position is $155,000-$170,000 Starting salaries are determined based on skills, experience, budget and other job-related factors. More information about our total compensation package will be shared with candidates during the recruitment process. ABOUT RAPID MICRO BIOSYSTEMS: Rapid Micro Biosystems creates, sells, validates, and services innovative products for fast, accurate, and efficient detection of microbial contamination in the manufacture of pharmaceuticals, biologics, biotechnology products, medical devices, and personal care products. The company’s Growth Direct™—the first and only growth-based system to automate rapid compendial QC Micro testing—ensures data integrity, compliance, and operational efficiencies driven by rapid methods and automation. Rapid Micro Biosystems is dedicated to providing groundbreaking technology and products to support companies in their journey to achieve greater reliability, efficiency, and better predictability, ultimately providing higher quality products for improved patient outcomes. Rapid Micro Biosystems is headquartered in Lexington, Massachusetts. Our research & development, and manufacturing operations are located in Lowell, Massachusetts. Additionally, we maintain field offices in Freising, Germany; Switzerland, and Singapore. Equal Opportunity: Rapid Micro Biosystems is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity. Applicants for employment and employees are reviewed on their individual qualifications for a position. Under no circumstances will Rapid Micro Biosystems discriminate against qualified persons on the basis of race, color, religious creed, retaliation, national origin, ancestry, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, mental illness, genetics, choice of health insurance, marital status, age, veteran status, or any other basis prohibited under applicable law. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. title: Client Growth Partner, (Digital Media & CTV Sales)
description: AdOmni is transforming how brands buy digital advertising on real-world and in-home screens. Our platform connects brands to high-impact video placements across DOOH and CTV environments, driving outcomes from brand awareness to performance marketing KPIs. The Role We are hiring a revenue-driven, hunter-minded sales professional who understands the media and agency landscape and can convert relationships into revenue. This is a quota-carrying individual contributor role focused on net-new business development. You will be responsible for building your own book of business from scratch: prospecting, developing your pipeline, managing the full sales cycle, negotiating, and closing. You should be comfortable speaking with both agency investment teams and brand-side decision makers. You know how to sell intangible value: strategy, outcomes, media innovation, not just inventory. If you are motivated by making money, thrive in competitive environments, and want to be paid in direct proportion to what you produce, this role is built for you. What You’ll Own Revenue generation is the job. Everything else supports it. Build and manage a robust self-sourced pipeline across agencies and brand-direct accounts Prospect daily through outreach, networking, industry events, referrals, and market mapping Sell programmatic, social-to-DOOH, video, and CTV solutions into agencies and enterprise brands Translate complex ad tech solutions into business impact for clients Lead discovery conversations with agency planners, buyers, and senior marketing decision-makers Negotiate and close deals independently Forecast accurately and manage pipeline health Work cross-functionally with strategy, product, and client success to deliver strong campaigns Stay ahead of market trends across programmatic, CTV, retail media, and social ecosystems You will not inherit a book of business. You will build one. Who You Are You are commercially sharp. You understand how agencies operate, from investment teams to activation, and you can navigate holding companies and independent shops alike. You likely have 4-8 years of media sales experience and a track record of exceeding quota. More importantly, you have proof that you can generate new revenue without relying on inbound leads. You are comfortable selling ideas that require education and buy-in. You can articulate value beyond CPMs. You can speak about business outcomes. You are resilient. Rejection does not deter you. It motivates you. You live in or near a major metro market (preferably NYC) because proximity to agencies and brands matters. You know that face time closes deals. What We’re Looking For 3-8 years of media, digital, programmatic, CTV, or advertising sales experience Proven history of exceeding quota in a net-new business environment Experience selling to agencies and/or brand-direct clients Deep understanding of media buying workflows and decision-making structures Strong prospecting discipline and pipeline creation skills Confidence in presenting to senior decision-makers Highly motivated by commission and performance-based compensation

title: Social Media Specialist
description: Do you believe in the power of human connection across cultures? Are you passionate about storytelling, community building, and creating digital experiences that inspire trust, emotion, and action? Join us and help tell the story of Cultural Care to thousands of families across the U.S. and around the world. We’re looking for a hands-on, strategy-minded Social Media Specialist to lead and elevate our brand’s social presence. In this role, you’ll oversee our core brand accounts, drive innovative campaigns, and guide regional teams on best practices. You’ll have the freedom to create, experiment, and measure what resonates—all while shaping how we connect with our host family audience. Sound like you? Read on! What You'll Do As Social Media Specialist, you'll be the voice of our brand across platforms. You’ll own the strategy, content, and execution of a social presence that celebrates our mission and builds community among host families, au pairs, and future program participants. Lead audience engagement and reach across our brand accounts (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, Pinterest, LinkedIn and emerging platforms) Develop, launch, and optimize social media strategy that drives awareness, engagement, and lead generation Analyze performance and create insightful reports that tell the story of our social presence, and inform strategy and future initiatives Plan, write and produce compelling content – stories, videos, graphics, and reels that bring our brand to life. Showcase authentic voices from our community: real families, au pairs, staff and LCCs. Collaborate closely with content, design, video and marketing teams to develop campaigns and integrate social into larger digital and traditional marketing campaigns Partner with our Content Manager to integrate larger company goals on social media and surface authentic, user generated stories that inspire our audience and drive conversions Develop and lead a host family content and influencer strategy to position Cultural Care as a trusted leader in the childcare and cultural exchange space. Train and inspire our Local Childcare Counselors on how to use social media to promote themselves, CCAP, and generate interest in the program in their markets. Lead community management—monitoring comments/DMs, fostering conversation, listening to our community on socials, and protecting brand voice Stay ahead of trends by actively researching new tools, channels, and competitor approaches Who You Are 3-5 years of professional social media marketing experience (brand side or agency) Proven track record of building and executing campaigns from concept to reporting You are obsessed with keeping up with the latest trends and industry standards even as platform best practices quickly change and constantly evolve Comfortable with analytics tools and reporting, you use data to iterate and improve Skilled at hands-on daily execution of campaigns that ladder up to big picture organizational goals Smart, social-first writer Experienced in on-camera content creation, native platform tools, and using Canva for graphics and light design Thrive in a cross functional, multicultural team and enjoy sharing best practices Collaborative, curious, and energized by connecting people to a mission driven brand You are a team player who understands the importance of teamwork, collaboration, and open communication across a global organization Familiar with Sprout Social, Asana, and other content management platforms Why you’ll love working here: Perks, Benefits, and more! This is the most fun, high caliber place you’ll ever work. Ask any employee why they love EF (whether they’ve been here 10 minutes or 10 years) and they’ll probably tell you the same thing: it’s the people. When you work at EF, you join a purpose-driven, international and energetic community that thrives on continuous learning, fearless innovation and mutual support. In Addition, You Can Expect Commitment to professional growth: robust monthly calendar of trainings and workshops Four weeks paid vacation your first year, ten paid holidays, and two floating holidays Exciting business travel opportunities 25% company match on your 401(k) contributions Market-leading medical, dental and vision coverage, along with options for life and disability insurance, accident and hospital insurance, legal and pet insurance Dependent care, healthcare and commuter Flex Spending Accounts (FSAs) Access to fertility care and family-building support Wellness benefits including a yearly fitness reimbursement Frequent social and learning events, including access to our employee-run resource groups Robust Employee Assistance Program Tenure-based sabbatical eligibility EF Product Discounts (discounts on travel, international language schools, Au Pair program and more) Discounts at local venues and businesses Amazing offices designed to match the caliber of the people who work there, plus the flexibility of working from home one day a week. Compensation range for this role is between $65,000-$80,000 based on experience. About Cultural Care Au Pair At Cultural Care Au Pair, we believe that cultural exchange has the power to make the world a more welcoming, understanding, and open place. As the largest and most experienced au pair organization in the world, we build global families by bringing together international visitors with American hosts—providing meaningful childcare for families, an unforgettable year in America for au pairs, and a culturally enriching experience for all. If you’re ready to expand your own horizons we invite you to be a part of our global team of problem-solvers, relationship-builders, and innovators. Join us as we continue to bring the world a little bit closer together, one family at a time. Cultural Care Au Pair is associated with EF Education First, the world leader in international education. At EF we believe that the world is better when people try to understand one another. Since 1965, we have brought millions of people to see new places, experience new cultures, and learn new things about the world and themselves. Our culturally immersive education programs—focused on language, travel, cultural exchange, and academics—create students of the world, and turn dreams into international opportunities. An equal opportunity employer, Cultural Care Au Pair is committed to inclusion and belonging across race, ethnicity, gender identity/expression, sexual orientation, age, religion, ability, parental status, experience and everything else that makes you, well…you.*

title: Product Line Manager II, Streetwear Mens Apparel, Global Jordan
description: Who You’ll Work With You will support the product creation process through close partnership with Product Management, Design, Materials, Tech Design, Merchandising, Sourcing/Costing, Demand Planning, and Operations. You will maintain and foster strong relationships between Global Apparel, Geo, and X-Category Product Management teams to ensure alignment, collaboration, and integration for key milestones. Who We Are Looking For An ideal PLM II is a collaborative teammate with deep streetwear experience, strong problem‑solving skills, and exceptional attention to detail. This person brings strong organizational skills, a passion for building best‑in‑class product, and the ability to learn, adapt, and lead quickly. A dedication to serving the consumer is essential as the team builds the future of Streetwear apparel. This role requires a high capacity for workload management and resourcefulness—no two days are ever the same. What You Bring Bachelor's degree in Business, Product Management, Product Merchandising, Product Marketing, Retail Merchandising or related field. Will accept any suitable combination of education, experience and training 2‑4 years’ experience in an apparel or footwear product creation role with a deep understanding of the product creation process Demonstrated excellent and creative consumer, streetwear, and marketplace understanding Experience, knowledge, and passion for men’s streetwear and the culture of sport Demonstrated leadership capabilities, including the ability to motivate, lead, negotiate, and influence cross‑functional partners Strong business management experience, analytical proficiency, and understanding of retail math Proficiency with Excel, Keynote, Miro, and MMX Ability to travel domestically and internationally up to 10% What You’ll Work On You will be responsible for supporting the holistic Men’s Streetwear Apparel team by: Obsessing over the consumer’s culture of influence and creating insights that fuel product lines and new opportunities. Creating Jordan Apparel for the global marketplace. Analyzing streetwear and sport trends across global markets and translating those insights into authentic Jordan Brand opportunities. Applying a strong understanding of product creation, delivery, and pricing needs of consumers and key markets to make informed, thoughtful decisions. Helping craft and communicate a cohesive product vision through end‑in‑mind storytelling and consumer‑right product strategies. Serving as the primary systems expert in partnership with key cross‑functional teammates, ensuring systems integrity and daily accuracy. Managing and regularly updating collection visual line plans (VLPs) and other tools that support product creation. Managing team samples through strong organization and clear communication. title: Marketing & Sales Intern
description: Start Date: June 1, 2026 Why an Internship with the Hilb Group? An internship with the Hilb Group offers students the opportunity to explore multiple career paths within a growing insurance brokerage. As a rapidly expanding organization, we provide hands-on learning experiences and exposure to real-world projects that support both personal and professional development. Interns work closely with experienced team members, gain insight into our business operations, and contribute to meaningful marketing, sales, and operational initiatives. You will also have the opportunity to travel to our corporate office in Richmond, VA, where you’ll connect with fellow interns, network with company leaders, and participate in a volunteer event. Develop marketing materials for producers, ensuring alignment with their needs after meetings and discussions, to support Hilb Group’s marketing and sales strategies. Coordinate with the Marketing and Sales Enablement Specialist to brainstorm ideas and review ongoing projects. Contribute to diverse projects, including the development of a webinar series, data appending, and social media/digital marketing initiatives. Utilize tools such as Canva and Adobe and apply strong professional written communication skills to create impactful content. Develop a foundational understanding of the insurance industry and agency operations. Collaborate and network with associates across the organization to support marketing efforts. Engage in a dynamic, team-oriented environment that values creativity. Qualifications: Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate degree program Preferred majors include, Risk Management & Insurance, Business Management, Marketing, Communications, or related fields Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities Excellent written and verbal communication skills Curious, motivated, and eager to learn about the insurance industry and grow professional The Hilb Group is an equal opportunity employer, and we actively support and comply with all applicable federal, state, and local laws prohibiting all forms of discrimination in employment. Additionally, we have a zero-tolerance policy for all forms of harassment in violation of federal, state, and local laws.

title: Internship - Marketing and Communication Specialist
description: Department Information This internship is a temporary part-time (minimum 10 hours/week) , hybrid Internship within Colorado and will exist for 5 months at a minimum with potential to be longer. Applicants must be located in Colorado. This internship is unpaid. This is a non-employee internship position. No benefits are available. This position is not covered by the Partnership Agreement between the State of Colorado and Colorado Workers for Innovative and New Solutions (COWins) (This posting may be used to fill multiple vacancies and may be extended.) Make a real difference . . . Do you have a passion and drive to apply your talent to create a better Colorado? Are you looking for an internship that provides you with opportunities to learn how to improve the world around you? The Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment (CDPHE) is seeking diverse individuals from all backgrounds to apply for a fantastic unpaid internship position that provides experiential learning about improving the lives of Colorado citizens. Our mission is to protect and improve the health of Colorado's people and the quality of its environment. If your goal is to pursue a career upon graduation that makes a difference, consider an internship with the dedicated people of the Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment. Our tobacco-free campus offers free parking and is conveniently located near RTD bus lines, the Cherry Creek Shopping Center, Glendale City Set, and the Cherry Creek bike path. We are committed to increasing the diversity of our staff and providing culturally responsive programs and services. Therefore, we encourage responses from people of diverse backgrounds and abilities. Description Of Job Tentative Start Date: Spring to Summer 2026 Internship Desired start date is 4/6/2026 to 9/4/2026 About The Work Unit The Office of STI/HIV/VH (OSHV) leads the public health practice for the control of sexually transmitted infections (STI), human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), and viral hepatitis (VH) in Colorado. The OSHV works to reduce disease transmission and to ensure that all people in Colorado access quality and medically accurate STI/HIV/VH related prevention, care, and treatment services. The OSVH fulfills multiple functions to serve the public, including: Public education and health promotion Training for workforce competencies Epidemiologic surveillance of STI/HIV/VH Follow-up and guidance to people newly diagnosed with a STI/HIV to ensure adequate treatment Referrals and partner notification Access to treatment and care as appropriate Partnerships with local communities across the state to implement comprehensive prevention interventions Evaluation of STI/HIV/VH epidemic in Colorado including the direct prevention and care activities The Public Health Education and Promotion Services is a section of Capacity Building and Training Program (CBT) within OSHV. Internship Description Project Title: Marketing and Communication Specialist Internship Salary: This internship is unpaid. Project Description This internship will participate in the OSHV social media/marketing efforts to support communication with minority youth and all young people ages 12-22 years old in the state. They will assist with tapping into existing online youth communities to increase visibility to the educational campaigns promoted by the OSHV. The position will assist with collecting feedback from target audiences to inform future media efforts and campaign themes, as well as channel feedback from focus groups to create educational messages and topics each month. Objectives By the end of the internship, the student will help develop, refine and post informational and "trendy" Instagram content to the CDPHE OSHV account. The student will assist with synthesizing content performance, focus group feedback, and leadership feedback to continue to improve STI/HIV/VH education to Colorado youth. Deliverables Or Outcomes A survey to collect the thoughts of youth and young adults in CO about existing Instagram content as displayed on the CDPHE_OSHV account. A detailed analysis of the results. Presentation for leadership with a proposal for future material. Strategic plan for how to move forward based on the results collected. Which Public Health Core Competencies skills does the project develop? Data Analytics and Assessment Skills; Evidence informed planning and service improvement; Communication skills; Understanding the role of mass media in shaping public education, normative health practices and influencing behavior. Minimum Qualifications Minimum Qualifications, Substitutions, Conditions of Employment & Appeal Rights Qualified Candidates Must Be Either Bachelors or Masters student enrolled in an accredited college or university s eeking a degree program in Health Education and Promotion, students must have a focus on health communication or digital health or health marketing. OR A recent bachelors or masters graduate with a completed degree in Health Education and Promotion, with a focus on health communication or digital health or health marketing. Recently graduated means the individual should have attended an accredited college or u niversity and have graduated with a bachelors or masters degree in the listed degree within three calendar years (2024-2026) (include transcripts). College Transcripts You must be using education to qualify, a legible copy of your official or unofficial college transcript(s) or a foreign credential evaluation report must be submitted with your application. Transcripts must show the name of the school and that a degree was conferred. Transcripts from colleges or universities outside of the United States must be assessed for U.S. equivalency by a NACES educational credential evaluation service. Failure to provide a transcript or foreign credential evaluation report may result in your application being rejected and you will not be able to continue in the selection process for this position. Preferred Qualifications Public Health: Students should have a understanding of sexual health education and a background in sexual health and public health principles is preferred. Social Media Analytics: Understanding how to measure the reach and impact of social media campaigns. Students who have experience using Meta and google sheets to track basic trends and campaign performance are preferred. Content Creation for Social Media: Designing engaging and informative content for Instagram via Canva and Instagram Reels. Community Engagement Strategies: Building and interacting with online communities related to health topics. Health Communication Theory: Applying communication principles to effectively convey health information in different languages including English, Spanish and to young audiences ages 12-24 years old. Digital Marketing Techniques: Utilizing social media advertising and targeting options to reach specific audiences. Conditions Of Employment Pursuant to CDPHE policy, a background check is required for all positions at CDPHE. Signed Non-Employee Confidentiality Agreement is required If you will be using this internship for academic credit, a Signed Affiliation Agreement is required between CDPHE and your University. Supplemental Information Supplemental Information A complete application packet must include: A completed State of Colorado application. A resume. If you are using education to qualify, a copy of your official or unofficial transcript showing that your degree has been conferred and the name of your school, or your NACES accreditation. Please submit this as an attachment to your online application. The Selection Process All applications that are received by the closing of this announcement will be reviewed. Candidates who are selected for further consideration will proceed to the next step and all applicants will be notified by email of their status at each step of the process. ADAAA Accommodations** The State of Colorado believes that equity, diversity, and inclusion drive our success, and we encourage candidates from all identities, backgrounds, and abilities to apply. The State of Colorado is an equal opportunity employer committed to building inclusive, innovative work environments with employees who reflect our communities and enthusiastically serve them. Therefore, in all aspects of the employment process, we provide employment opportunities to all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, medical condition related to pregnancy, creed, ancestry, national origin, marital status, genetic information, or military status (with preference given to military veterans), or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law. The Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment is committed to the full inclusion of all qualified individuals. As part of this commitment, our agency will assist individuals who have a disability with any reasonable accommodation requests related to employment, including completing the application process, interviewing, completing any pre-employment testing, participating in the employee selection process, and/or to perform essential job functions where the requested accommodation does not impose an undue hardship. If you have a disability and require reasonable accommodation to ensure you have a positive experience applying or interviewing for this position, please direct your inquiries to our ADAAA Coordinator, Dan McKenna, at daniel.mckenna@state.co.us or call 303-692-2060, option 5.
