title: Social Media Intern
description: Sprinklr is the definitive, AI-native platform for Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM), empowering brands to deliver extraordinary experiences at scale — across every customer touchpoint. By combining human instinct with the speed and efficiency of AI, Sprinklr helps brands earn trust and loyalty through personalized, seamless, and efficient customer interactions. Sprinklr’s unified platform provides powerful solutions for every customer-facing team — spanning social media management, marketing, advertising, customer feedback, and omnichannel contact center management — enabling enterprises to unify data, break down silos, and act on real-time insights. Today, 1,900+ enterprises and 60% of the Fortune 100 rely on Sprinklr to help them deliver consistent, trusted customer experiences worldwide. Job Description About the Role As our Social Media Intern, you’ll gain hands‑on experience building content, shaping channel strategies, listening to real audiences, and responding in real time. You’ll learn how social media supports advocacy, brand trust, employee storytelling, and moments that matter—both planned and unexpected. This is a paid 12‑week full‑time summer internship designed for rising seniors who want real experience using enterprise‑level tools, collaborating cross‑functionally, and building confidence in their voice. You’ll leave with real, publishable work and examples you can confidently include in a portfolio. What You’ll Work On You’ll contribute to meaningful social media initiatives, including: Supporting social channel strategy development—learning how goals, audiences, and platforms work together Writing, editing, and creating content that aligns with brand voice, channel norms, and timely moments Monitoring conversations, moderating engagement, and practicing social listening using Sprinklr Supporting customer, employee, and executive advocacy initiatives, including elements of an associate advocacy program Assisting with insights, reporting, and presentations that explain what’s working, what’s not, and why Learning how brands prepare for sensitive moments and apply sound judgment during crises or high‑visibility situations What You’ll Learn This internship is designed to build both hard and soft skills. By the end of the program, you’ll have experience with: Social channel planning and strategy development Copywriting and content creation for multiple platforms Social listening, moderation, and community engagement best practices Using Sprinklr for publishing, advocacy, and insights Understanding brand guardrails, decision‑making, and risk awareness Tools & Platforms You’ll Use Sprinklr (publishing, listening, advocacy, and insights) Microsoft Suite, including Copilot Native analytics tools across supported social platforms Collaboration tools to support remote, cross‑functional work Who This Is For Rising seniors pursuing communications, marketing, journalism, or a related field Strong interest in social media, storytelling, and brand building Curious thinkers who want to understand the why behind social decisions Confident writers with an eye for creativity and detail Organized & proactive in hybrid/remote work environments We focus on our mission : Sprinklr was founded in 2009 to solve a big problem: growing enterprise complexity that separated brands from their customers. Our vision was clear: to unify fragmented teams, tools and data — helping large organizations build deeper, more meaningful connections with the people they serve. Today, Sprinklr has a unified, AI-native platform for four product suites: Sprinklr Service, Sprinklr Social, Sprinklr Marketing, and Sprinklr Insights. Sprinklr is here to do three things: Lead a new category of enterprise software that we call Unified-CXM. Empower companies to deliver next generation, unified engagement journeys that reimagine the customer experience. Create a culture of customer obsession, with trust, teamwork, and accountability. We believe in our product : Customers who value exceptional customer experiences have what they need on our single unified platform, built with an operating system approach on a single codebase. That means that everything — and everyone — can work together to service, respond, sell, and market to customers on the channels they prefer. While Unified Customer Experience Management (Unified-CXM) as a category is just getting started, we are well on our way to creating a no-compromise, unified approach to better customer experiences for the world’s leading enterprise brands. We invest in our people : We offer a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to help each member of our team thrive. Sprinklr believes that you should be able to get the type of care you need for your personal well-being when you need it. We offer you and your family voluntary healthcare coverage in countries where applicable. We believe it is important to take time off – it is essential for your mental and physical wellbeing. We provide Sprinklrites with paid time off to recharge and spend time with loved ones. We want to grow our talent with purpose. Our open Mentoring Program is designed to create meaningful connections that support growth and amplify our focus. To learn more about employee benefits by region, click here. To learn more about all-things-Sprinklr, visit our candidate resource hub here. EEO - Our philosophy : Our goal is to ensure every employee feels like they belong and are operating in a collaborative environment. We fervently believe every employee matters and should be respected and heard. We believe we are stronger when we belong because collectively, we’re more innovative, creative, and successful. Sprinklr is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, religion, creed, national origin or ancestry, ethnicity, sex (including gender, pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), age, physical or mental disability, citizenship, past, current, or prospective service in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable law. Reasonable accommodations are available upon request during the interview process. To request an accommodation, please work directly with your recruitment coordinator or recruiter. Job Req Compensation Range $39,000 - $65,000 The base salary range for this role is shown above. At Sprinklr, base pay depends on multiple individualized factors, including experience, qualifications, job-related knowledge and skills, and geographic location. Base pay is only one part of our competitive Total Rewards package: the successful candidate may also be eligible to participate in Sprinklr’s discretionary bonus plan, commission plan, and/or equity plan, depending on role. US-based Sprinklr employees are eligible for a highly competitive benefits package as well, which demonstrates our commitment to our employees’ health, well-being, and financial protection. The US-based benefits include a 401k plan with 100% vested company contributions, flexible paid time off, holidays, generous caregiver and parental leaves, life and disability insurance, and health benefits including medical, dental, vision, and prescription drug coverage. Warning about Recruiting Scams** : Please be vigilant for recruiting scams impersonating Sprinklr. Sprinklr will never ask you for money, to pay for equipment, or for unnecessary personal information during the interview process. Sprinklr will also never pay in Bitcoin or send email communications from our executives. Please review the Federal Trade Commission's advice to avoid these types of scams. If you are contacted by someone whom you suspect may not be appropriately representing Sprinklr, please do not engage and block their email or phone number immediately.

title: Marketing Manager
description: The Marketing Manager is responsible for devising and executing innovative strategies to amplify targeted traffic and rental income for the communities and company. This entails a multifaceted approach encompassing digital marketing, website optimization, social media engagement, bolstering Google presence, and reputation management. In close collaboration with the Director, Marketing & Strategy, this role assumes responsibility for upholding DLP Capital's brand guidelines and standards across all assets. Serving as the linchpin in driving local traffic to the communities, this position serves as the resident expert in leveraging cutting-edge digital marketing practices to propel revenue generation, ensuring that budgeted income across all communities is met and exceeded. As the brand's voice, the Marketing Manager interfaces closely with the communities to orchestrate the seamless execution of effective and coherent messaging, marketing, and branding strategies. The ultimate aim is to maximize leasing potential, cultivate brand awareness and enrich the lives of our residents. Through a strategic blend of creativity, market insight, and tactical execution, this role is pivotal in shaping the communities' perception and positioning them for sustained success in a competitive landscape. What you have already achieved: (Requirements) Proficiency with digital marketing platforms, automation tools, and analytics for date-driven decision making. 3+ years of multifamily housing marketing experience with knowledge of market trends and renter demographics. Strong attention to detail and ability to analyze marketing performance data for insights and accuracy. Bachelor's degree in marketing, business administration, or related field. Creative and adaptable, with the ability to innovate marketing strategies in a shifting industry landscape. What you are great at: (Responsibilities) Develop, implement and monitor marketing strategies and campaigns, using analytics to attract prospective tenants to optimize performance. Oversee digital performance marketing (SEO, SEM, paid social, Google Performance Max) and manage budgets to maximize ROI. Coordinate production of marketing collateral and ensure all materials align with brand standards and guidelines. Draft, present, and maintain an Annual Business Marketing Plan for each assigned community, outlining key initiatives, seasonal campaigns, digital advertising strategy, outreach efforts, and event programming for the year ahead. The plan must align with the company's overall marketing objectives, reflect the community's positioning within its market, and include measurable performance indicators. Manage and optimize AppFolio CRM workflows, ensuring effective lead management, campaign integration, and data-driven insights to improve conversions. Partner with onsite leasing teams to develop community-level campaigns, events, and outreach initiatives. Oversee social media strategies and reputation management platforms (including JTurner) to drive engagement, surveys, and brand trust. Develop and maintain relationships with external vendors such as ILS advertisers, prop tech partners, designers, and printers. Plan and execute resident and community engagement initiatives, including events, referral programs, and partnerships with local businesses and organizations to enhance brand visibility. Stay up-to-date on industry trends, best practices, and emerging technologies in apartment marketing. Provide training and support to leasing teams on marketing initiatives and strategies. Report on marketing performance, including key metrics and insights, to asset and regional managers. What you're motivated by: Making an impact. Whether it's the residents who call our Thriving Communities home, the investors who trust us to be stewards of their capital, or the sponsors we work with to 10x their business, everything we do is about making an impact. Challenges. You see roadblocks as opportunities and are proactive in finding solutions. Growth. We've been named to the Inc. 5000 as one of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in America for thirteen consecutive years. Who We Are What We Do In One Sentence: We finance the building of Thriving Communities. Our Mission: We are changing the nation through the building of Thriving Communities. We are the leader in funding the building of Thriving Communities centered in safe, attainable housing. Our Purpose: To passionately make an extraordinary impact by transforming lives and building Thriving Communities. Our Big, Hairy, Audacious Goal (BHAG): To positively impact and transform 10 million lives by funding the building of 5,000 Thriving Communities, becoming one of America's 100 largest private companies. Our Culture: We work hard, marching towards ambitious goals driven by a purpose that's bigger than ourselves. We are impact-driven, seeking to make a difference not just on the affordable housing crisis but also in the lives of our employees, investors, and partners. We are disciplined, following our exclusive Elite Execution System to develop personally and professionally. We strive to Live Fully, finding fulfillment in all areas of our lives. Our Core Values: Driven For Greatness : We are driven by the pursuit of knowledge and the paramount importance of a growth mindset, cultivating greatness both personally and professionally. Living Fully : We prioritize living fully with a dedication to achieving optimal health in all aspects of life: faith, family, friends, freedom, fun , fulfillment, fitness, and finance. We are intentional with our time, integrating the 8fs of our life to achieve our goals & God's goals. Grit : We have the endurance and unshakable commitment necessary to achieve our visionary long-term goals. Community : We elevate the greater good over individual interests, magnifying our impact and cultivating thriving relationships with investors, sponsors, residents, and our colleagues. Authenticity : We are true to ourselves and others, steadfast in our beliefs and values. We hold ourselves and others accountable, fostering open dialogue to propel growth. Prepared to Win : We apply discipline to our thoughts and our actions, which leads us to put forth consistent effort and commitment doing the important proactive work despite the urgent priorities we face each day. We plan to win, and are prepared for volatility and challenges. Kingdom Impact : We share God's love and transformative power through our labor, serving everyone in God's likeness. The world will know God more through our work and how we do it. Twenty-Mile March : We march towards clear, well-defined goals and objectives with unwavering focus, constantly evaluating our performance and course correcting as needed. Stewardship : We are guided by our commitments to those who trust us with their capital, their communities, and their careers. Servant Leadership : We lead with high standards & deep devotion. We display as humble confidence as we build deep relationships and trust. We show the way through the forest, empowering growth, accountability, and a founder's mentality, with team members regularly asking "What else can I do to achieve the desired results?" Benefits and Perks: Unlimited PTO, health/dental/vision/life insurance, 401(k) matching, parental leave, adoption services reimbursement/learning/educational incentives, Volunteer Time Off, well-being programs, company events, self improvement/productivity tools, & the incredible feeling of making a difference. Equal Opportunity Employer: DLP is a committed Equal Opportunity Employer. If you are a qualified individual with a disability and require assistance in searching for a position or submitting an application, please contact HR at hr@dlpcapital.com . Offers of employment are conditional upon satisfactory completion of pre-employment background verification (including E-Verify ). California applicants can view our California Data Privacy Policy here . #LI-CL1 #LI-Onsite

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: Seer Interactive is a digital marketing consultancy that harnesses compassion, data, technology, and AI to make a mark on our communities—our coworkers, our clients, our industry, and our neighbors. We relentlessly pursue (and share) the truth, bringing together millions of data points from quantitative and qualitative sources, enhanced by responsible, human-centered AI. Our purpose is to unlock potential across these communities, envisioning a world where doing the right thing is intuitive and informed. At Seer, we see AI as a catalyst for innovation, collaboration, and creativity. Regardless of your division or discipline, leveraging AI is a core part of how we deliver smarter, faster, and more strategic outcomes for our clients. Our AI Council drives company-wide integration by focusing on education, purposeful implementation, and continuous learning — ensuring that AI is embedded in how we think, operate, and grow. If you’re ready to uplift others, challenge your ego, commit to the truth, and be better than yesterday—keep reading. Seer is a remote-first agency and a Certified B-Corp, with team members across the country. If an office environment is more your style, this position can also sit in our HQ office in Philadelphia. We can only consider candidates based in the US who can work continental US-based hours. We’re looking for a junior-level contractor or intern to join our marketing team on a flexible, part-time basis. This is a hands-on coordination role — perfect for someone early in their career who is eager to learn, hungry for experience, and excited to learn more about the future of AI + marketing. You don’t need to know all the tools today. What matters most is curiosity and the drive to learn and grow. This is an opportunity to work closely with Wil Reynolds (CEO & founder), who’s been at the forefront of SEO, and now AI and digital marketing, as well as a marketing team focused on growth and innovation. You’ll be supporting both Wil and the broader team across public speaking, content production, experimentation, and executive marketing initiatives. This position is a hybrid role out of our Philadelphia, PA office . Candidates must be available to come into the office on a regular basis. What You'll Do: Executive Marketing Production & Thought Leadership Help prepare presentations and webinars Edit video clips for social and YouTube using Descript or equivalent Use AI tools to analyze content performance and identify experimentation opportunities Content, Creative & Publishing Use Canva, Figma, or similar tools to create and edit graphics for blogs, social media, and events Format, schedule, and publish blog posts in line with brand standards Refresh and update website elements (bios, CTAs, event landing pages) on a regular cadence Support email nurture execution Design and support slides for speaking sessions, webinars, and client-facing presentations Events & Campaign Support Build landing pages and creative assets for webinars and events Support workflows and distribution for campaigns and post-event materials Help run social media campaigns (posting, scheduling, testing) Support marketing tests and experiments What We’re Looking For A desire to learn and grow in marketing and AI Strong organizational skills and attention to detail Ability to juggle multiple projects and follow through on deadlines Comfort diving into new tools (we’ll help!) A collaborative mindset and willingness to pitch in where needed Position Logistics This role is flexible with scheduling and will require ~30 hours per week Hybrid role in Philadelphia with flexibility to come into our office on a weekly basis This is a contract / intern opportunity that will give you exposure to the breadth of modern marketing execution. You’ll work closely with a senior marketing team, learn how strategies translate into execution, and build real-world skills across creative, publishing, events, and campaign operations. We don’t expect you to walk into this role knowing how to do it all, but we do expect you to walk in hungry and eager to learn. Ready to apply? Ditch the cover letter — we don’t need it. We’d rather see your AI chops. Use our Marketing Career AI Self-Assessment GPT to find out where you land on the AI marketing maturity curve and share your results with us. Our team is excited to read your application responses and will be using them to help determine who we’d like to meet with! Have questions about recruitment at Seer? Check out our Custom GPT: Guide to Talent Acquisition at Seer to drop in your questions! You can ask it anything about our hiring process, and how to best prepare for an interview with us. As a B-Corp Certified organization, we are committed to creating an inclusive environment and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. Not ready to apply but want to keep in touch? Stay connected via our monthly Career Update newsletter! We'll share curated content on Seer, open roles, industry insights, career advice, events you don't want to miss, and more (so you never miss a beat). #LI-Remote We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Field Marketing & Multimedia Specialist
description: About the Role MPower is an innovative new clinic specializing in orthopedic care and men's health. Our mission is to provide top-notch, patient-centered care with a focus on cutting-edge treatments and personalized solutions. We are seeking a dynamic and results- oriented Field Marketing and Multimedia Specialist to drive growth and expansion. Responsibilities Building Relationships (40%): Build relationships with physician offices and referral sources. Understand the specific needs, challenges, and priorities of the physician offices and referral sources. This would involve learning about their patient demographics, common conditions treated, and any unique challenges they face. Tailor your approach to address their specific needs and demonstrate how our services can help. Maintain consistent and open lines of communication through meetings, calls, and emails. This helps in staying top-of-mind and ensures any concerns or updates are promptly addressed. Clearly articulate the benefits and value of our services. This includes how they can improve patient outcomes, streamline processes, or provide additional support. Offer educational materials or training sessions to keep them informed about new treatments, technologies, or services that might benefit their practice or patients. Provide excellent customer service and support. Address any issues or concerns promptly and effectively. Participate in industry events, conferences, or local meetings where you can interact with physicians and referral sources in person. Look for opportunities to collaborate on community health initiatives, research, or other projects that benefit both parties. Recognize and thank them for their referrals or any support they provide. Small gestures like thank-you notes or acknowledging milestones. Share success stories or positive outcomes that resulted from their referrals or collaboration. Ensure that your goals align with theirs. Understanding their objectives helps in creating a partnership that is beneficial for both parties. Work together to set mutual goals and develop strategies to achieve them. Adhere to industry regulations and ethical guidelines to maintain a professional reputation. Creative Designer (20%): Work in collaboration with the Practice Manager and other members of the team to create visually pleasing designs for print and digital media, including brochures, flyers, banners, signage, social media graphics, website assets, and email templates, for our audiences. Develop animated and other digital graphics and content for digital communications, marketing, and outreach efforts. Develop/manage the concept and framework for videos, podcasts, and audio recordings, including creating storyboards, scripts, and producing a final product. Provides support for design of marketing materials for internal and external audiences in various creative categories, to support the delivery of custom materials Executes creative design details including layout, writes copy, edits, color selection and/or illustration for marketing collateral and product design concepts. Coordinate print and fulfilment jobs with external print vendors. Web Content and Social Media (20%): Maintain and update website content for public facing website Support creation, development and approval for social media content and calendar, in collaboration with the Practice Manager. Create a monthly report including: digital tracking, conversion activity, traffic volume, engagement trends, and recommendations and analysis based on best practices. Brand Management (10%): Functions as a brand steward to ensure brand standards and guidelines are maintained internally and with our cross-functional and external partners Captures high-quality images for marketing materials, website, and social media. Maintain a library of design assets, templates, and style guidelines for consistent branding across various platforms. Collaborate with the Practice Manager and other members of the team to design customized multi-media and PowerPoint presentations, slide templates and to create master slides. Project Management (10%): Coordinate and implement design, production, and distribution of print materials for all marketing projects; including planning one’s own workload to achieve and ensure the project is completed on time and within budget. Independently troubleshoot solutions should a project fall off plan, demonstrating a solution-oriented approach to problem solving. Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Healthcare Management, Marketing, or a related field preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in business development or marketing, preferably in the healthcare sector. HubSpot CRM and Canva experience desired. Skills: Strong analytical skills, excellent communication and interpersonal skills, and the ability to build and maintain relationships. Knowledge: Familiarity with the orthopedic and men’s health sectors is highly desirable. Attributes: Self-motivated, results-driven, and able to work independently as well as part of a team. Job Type: Full-time Pay: $20,000.00 - $50,000.00 per year Benefits: 401(k) Flexible schedule Paid time off Experience: Healthcare business development: 1 year (Required) Ability to Commute: Richland Hills, TX 76118 (Required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Richland Hills, TX 76118

title: VIDEOGRAPHER, CONTENT CREATOR
description: Job Overview The Videographer focuses on capturing and editing uplifting and inspiring videos of Oaks Church events and ministries and its congregants as well as leading the development of a highly capable videography team. This is a full-time, hourly position, scheduled for Sunday through Thursday. Reports to Creative Director . Responsibilities + Duties: Always be innovative, self-starting, and value team-based ministry bringing a positive upbeat spirit to the team Responsible for High tier, high performance Events and shoots Works with Creative Director to plan, produce, film, and edit videos throughout the year Scheduling teams of video volunteers for Sunday, Youth services, Kids services, Wednesday Prayer Meetings and including but not limited to special events, ministry, and styled shoots when needed Recruitment of volunteers and giving them assignments in project management systems Leading videography Sunday service, Youth services, Kids services, Wednesday Prayer Meetings and including but not limited to special events, Pastoral headshots, ministry and styled shoots Planning, prepping, recording, editing, and distribution of Video Announcements Capture and organize b-roll on Sundays Bring creative ideas and creative direction when needed Initiate the rentals of equipment for special projects and return shipment/rentals Cleaning and organizing parts, cards and up-keep, repairs, and utility of all video equipment Training Creative interns from Oaks College and beyond Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or above is encouraged Experience in leading teams and developing people is required Must be highly knowledgeable to work with Canon and Sony cameras and their accessories High Proficiency in Premiere is required Experience in After Effects is required Skills in videography, lighting composition, digital framing. The ability to handle each event with grace and leadership. Ability to recruit and lead a team of volunteers Must be able to multitask Must be able to work with a team Ability to proactively respond to work situations and challenges Organized and self-motivated Excellent in organization and project management methodologies Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail. Excellent time management skills with a proven ability to meet deadlines. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong supervisory and leadership skills. Ability to prioritize tasks and to delegate them when appropriate. Ability to function well in a high-paced and at times stressful environment. Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite or related software and Asana Project Management Software General Expectations Commitment to continual growth in personal spiritual life and relationship with Christ Attend Tuesday Staff Prayer Meetings, All Staff Meetings, “All Hands On Deck” Events “Serve One and Attend One” at weekly Oaks Church Sunday services. Support the vision, values and ministry of Oaks Church through tithing, serving, etc. Proactive thinking and willing to learn and train others Model a Godly life for volunteers Be teachable with a humble and submissive attitude Maintain all information confidential (about staff, projects, property, members, etc.)

title: Sr Field Marketing Manager, Amazon Business
description: DESCRIPTION Come be a part of a rapidly expanding $45 billion dollar global business. At Amazon Business, a fast-growing startup passionate about building solutions, we set out every day to innovate and disrupt the status quo. We stand at the intersection of tech & retail in the B2B space developing innovative purchasing and procurement solutions to help businesses and organizations thrive. At Amazon Business, we strive to be the most recognized and preferred strategic partner for smart business buying. Bring your insight, imagination and a healthy disregard for the impossible. Join us in building and celebrating the value of Amazon Business to buyers and sellers of all sizes and industries. Unlock your career potential. As the Sr. Field Marketing Manager, you will be responsible for increasing consideration, acquisition, and adoption of Amazon Business within Enterprise accounts. You will partner closely with Sales and cross-functional Marketing teams to concept and deliver integrated marketing programs including demand generation campaigns, executive events, and other field marketing campaigns such as tradeshows, content and sales enablement. To be successful in this role, this individual will work seamlessly with senior stakeholders in Sales and Marketing at Amazon Business and pan-Amazon teams such as AWS to align and implement strategies. We are looking for an individual with broad experience in field marketing and integrated marketing campaign leadership. Field marketing includes annual strategic planning, event management, webinar creation, and executive engagement programs. Candidate should be capable of being strategic and tactical. This role requires skills in strategic thinking, relationship building, and problem solving, as well as a willingness to be hands-on with execution. The individual will thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment, have impeccable attention to detail, and will have a track record of on-time delivery of projects. If you’re looking to join a dynamic, growing team in a high-impact role, this is a great fit for you. Key job responsibilities Develop the marketing strategy to reach and engage C-suite and decision makers, across enterprise accounts, through bespoke, 1-to-1, and 1-to-few Account Based Marketing campaigns and initiatives Collaboratively create Executive Engagement initiatives such as Customer Advisory Boards, Executive Briefings, Executive Dinners Own planning and execution of bespoke marketing channels like Webinars, Direct Mail, Sponsorships across client, trade and association publications, etc. Manage relationships with internal Marketing, Sales, Sales Enablement, and Product stakeholders, as well as external media agencies and partners Drive robust and detailed measurement and reporting processes A day in the life The Sr Field Marketing Manager is the conduit between Sales and broader Marketing teams. They will dive deep with Sales to understand key business priorities, and work hand-in-hand with extended marketing teams such as digital, content, and channel marketing teams to create and then execute an integrated marketing plan that supports those priorities. They will act as the single point of contact for all of Marketing, driving visibility of upcoming initiatives to Sales, and reporting back campaign impact. About the team You will join the field marketing team. Field marketing coordinates all marketing activities across partner marketing, sales, and product teams. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS 6+ years of professional non-internship marketing experience Experience using data and metrics to drive improvements Experience with Excel or Tableau (data manipulation, macros, charts and pivot tables) Experience building, executing and scaling cross-functional marketing programs Experience communicating results to senior leadership Bachelors degree or equivalent PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Experience creating and executing integrated marketing lead generation campaigns using tactics that include live or virtual events, webinars, email, lead nurture programs, and CRM/sales insights Experience with Salesforce and Tableau Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several US geographic markets. The base pay for this position ranges from $112,800/year in our lowest geographic market up to $186,500/year in our highest geographic market. Pay is based on a number of factors including market location and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Amazon is a total compensation company. Dependent on the position offered, equity, sign-on payments, and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits. For more information, please visit https://www.aboutamazon.com/workplace/employee-benefits. This position will remain posted until filled. Applicants should apply via our internal or external career site.

title: Social Media Specialist
description: Company Description Fortune Brands Innovations, Inc. is an industry-leading home, security and digital products company. We’re focused on exciting opportunities within the home, security and commercial building markets. Our driving purpose is to elevate every life by transforming spaces into havens. We believe our work and our brands can have incredibly positive impacts for not just our business and shareholders, but for people and the planet, too. At Fortune Brands, we’re building something big. We’re advancing exciting innovations in all of our products and processes. We’re delivering trust, dependability, sustainability, and style. To make it all happen, we’ve transformed our workplace into an environment where smart, ambitious people have the support to reach their fullest potential. When you join Fortune Brands, you become part of a high-performing team empowered to think big, learn fast and make bold decisions. We support an inclusive culture where everyone is encouraged be their authentic selves, and where our differences and unique perspectives are a key strength. Explore life at Fortune Brands here. Job Description We are seeking a creative, detail-oriented Social Media Manager to support the execution and day-to-day management of our organic social media presence. This role will play a critical part in bringing brand strategies to life across social platforms, publishing compelling content, engaging with our communities, and ensuring operational excellence across channels. Reporting to the Senior Brand Marketing Manager, Social Media & Influencer, you will be responsible for hands-on content execution, community management, platform optimization, and performance tracking across Moen and House of Rohl’s social channels. The ideal candidate is highly organized, culturally fluent in social media, and equally comfortable writing a caption, scheduling a Reel, and pulling post-level performance analytics and insights. Location: This role is eligible for a hybrid schedule based at our Deerfield, IL campus Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays with remote flexility Mondays and Fridays. What you will be doing: Social Media Execution & Publishing Execute the day-to-day organic social media presence across platforms including Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Pinterest, LinkedIn, YouTube, and emerging platforms. Manage content scheduling and publishing, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and alignment with brand voice and campaign priorities for both Moen and House of Rohl. Support the development and maintenance of the social content calendar in partnership with the Senior Marketing Manager and creative teams. Write engaging, platform-native captions and post copy that align with brand guidelines while reflecting current social trends. Content & Community Management Proactively monitor and manage social communities, including comment moderation, community engagement, and escalation of customer issues in partnership with customer care teams. Identify opportunities to engage with followers, creators, and brand partners to foster community growth and brand affinity. Track and surface UGC opportunities, flagging high-performing content for potential amplification or reuse. Stay current on social trends, formats, and cultural moments, proactively sharing ideas and recommendations with the broader social team. Performance Tracking & Optimization Track post-level and channel-level organic performance, including engagement, reach, follower growth, and content trends. Assist in compiling regular reporting and insights for internal stakeholders. Identify learnings and optimization opportunities based on content performance, platform trends, and audience behavior. Cross-Functional Collaboration Partner closely with creative, brand, PR, media, and e-commerce teams to ensure social content supports broader marketing initiatives. Work collaboratively with agency partners to ensure smooth execution and alignment on timelines and deliverables. Support internal education around social media best practices and platform updates as needed. Qualifications 3+ years of experience managing brand social media channels, preferably for consumer, lifestyle, or home-related brands Deep familiarity with major social platforms (e.g. Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Pinterest, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.) and social publishing tools (e.g. Hootsuite, Sprinklr, Sprout, etc.) Strong writing skills with an ability to adapt tone and voice across two distinct brands Highly organized with excellent attention to detail and project management skills Nice to Have: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or similar Comfortable working in a fast-paced, highly collaborative environment Passion for social media, content creation, and digital culture Experience with social analytics tools and reporting dashboards Additional Information Fortune Brands believes in fair and equitable pay. A reasonable estimate of the base salary range for this role is Hiring Pay Range: $60,000 USD - $99,000 USD. Please note that actual salaries may vary within the range, or be above or below the range, based on factors including, but not limited to, education, training, experience, professional achievement, business need, and location. In addition to base salary, employees will participate in either an annual bonus plan based on company and individual performance, or a role-based sales incentive plan. At Fortune Brands, we support the overall health and wellness of our associates by offering comprehensive, competitive benefits that prioritize all aspects of wellbeing and provide flexibility for our teammates’ unique needs. This includes robust health plans, a market-leading 401(k) program with a company contribution, product discounts, flexible time off benefits, adoption benefits, and more. We offer numerous ERGs (Employee Resource Groups) to foster a sense of belonging for all associates. Fortune Brands is built on industry-leading brands and innovation within the high-growth categories of water, outdoors and security. The Company makes innovative products for residential and commercial environments, with a growing focus on digital solutions and products that add luxury, contribute to safety and enhance sustainability. To learn more, visit our website at fbin.com. Equal Employment Opportunity Fortune Brands is an equal opportunity employer. Fortune Brands evaluates qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity or expression, national origin, ancestry, age, disability/handicap status, marital status, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, genetic history or information, or any other legally protected characteristic. Reasonable Accommodations Fortune Brands is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application or interview process, please contact us at fbinleave.fbhs@fbin.com and let us know the nature of your request along with your contact information. Important Notice: Protect Yourself from Fraudulent Job Postings To protect yourself from fraudulent job postings or recruitment scams, please note that Fortune Brands job postings are exclusively hosted on our website at fbin.com/careers via our SmartRecruiters platform. Fortune Brands will never request banking information or sensitive personal details until an offer of employment has been accepted and the onboarding process begins.

title: Marketing Strategist
description: Department: Marketing Reports To: Senior Marketing Manager Job Type: Full-time Location: Remote Position Summary: The Marketing Strategist will develop integrated marketing strategies for donor fundraising and stewardship purposes. The position will be responsible for ensuring the timely execution of these campaigns, the market research, marketing KPIs and reporting. The Marketing Strategist reports directly to the Senior Marketing Manager and will be based in our corporate office located in Pompano Beach, FL or work remotely. Duties and Responsibilities: Work closely with internal stakeholders to develop integrated marketing campaigns that include, but are not limited to, advertising, content marketing, email marketing, earned and paid media, and direct mail. Brief and oversee the work of internal and external agencies who will execute the campaign activity. Edit and proofread copy for all relevant marketing materials. Ensure that Cross Catholic Outreach’s brand and identity are adhered to in campaigns and in all communication channels. Manage the implementation, tracking and measurement of marketing campaigns. Deliver regular reports of campaign results, including web analysis and evaluation of KPIs to the marketing and internal stakeholders. Collect and use data to construct new campaigns. Establish and maintain campaign budgets. Collaborate with the internal marketing teams on the creation of all marketing assets, collateral, and mailings. Knowledge, Skills & Abilities Require/Preferred: Is a consistent witness for Jesus Christ; maintains a courteous, Christ-like attitude in dealing with people within and outside of the ministry. Feels a calling to serve God through serving Cross Catholic Outreach in this capacity. Believes in and supports the mission and ministry of Cross Catholic Outreach, and agrees with the conduct policies and conduct expectations of Cross Catholic Outreach. Excellent oral and written communication skills including editing and proofreading. Ability to manage complex projects simultaneously under tight deadlines. Meticulous attention to detail and accuracy. Ability to prioritize based on the overarching needs of the organization. Excellent interpersonal skills. Ability to work collaboratively with internal and external stakeholders Marketing research, automation, and digital marketing experience required. Integrity, credibility, and commitment to the organization’s mission. Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint required. Experience Requirements: Minimum 5 years’ experience in communications, marketing or related field, preferably in nonprofit development. Donor acquisition and stewardship marketing experience Education or Certification Requirements: A bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, public relations, or business is preferred. Marketing Strategist Department: Marketing Location: Remote APPLY NOW Application Process If you wish to apply for an open position, please complete the online application and upload your resume to our career portal. We look forward to hearing from you!

title: MarTech Architect (Hybrid)
description: We have many opportunities available on our other career site pages. Click here to link to our careers page! Signet Jewelers is the world's largest retailer of diamond jewelry, operating more than 2,800 stores worldwide under the iconic brands: Kay Jewelers, Zales, Jared, H.Samuel, Ernest Jones, Peoples, Banter by Piercing Pagoda, Rocksbox, JamesAllen.com and Diamonds Direct. We are a people-first company and this core value is at the heart of everything we do, from empowering our valued team members, to collaborating with our customers, to fostering the communities in which we live and serve. People – and the love their actions inspire – are what drive us. We’re not only proud of the love we inspire outside our walls, we’re especially proud of the diversity, inclusion and equity we’re inspiring inside. There are dynamic career paths awaiting you – rewarding opportunities to impact the lives of others and inspire love. Join us! MarTech Architect Locations: Dallas, TX or Akron, OH (Hybrid) POSITION SUMMARY: We are seeking a MarTech Architect with strong Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) and Adobe Journey Optimizer (AJO) experience to lead customer data and MarTech architecture. This role will design, govern, and evolve enterprise-scale customer data flows, enable real-time personalization, and integrate CDP with the broader MarTech ecosystem (CRM, Analytics, and Marketing Channels). The MarTech Architect will also define platform standards, governance, monitoring, and long-term strategies to ensure data quality, identity stability, security, and AI/ML readiness across the customer profile ecosystem. RESPONSIBILITIES: Architect and own Adobe AEP–based CDP implementation across Dev, Stage, and Prod Design and maintain XDM schemas, identity namespaces, identity rules, and profile stitching strategies Define and enforce CDP data governance standards, including event and attribute taxonomy Enable real-time customer profiles, segmentation, and audience activation Design and govern Adobe Journey Optimizer (AJO) journeys (event-based and audience-based) Integrate CDP with the MarTech stack including CRM, analytics, email, SMS, paid media, web, and mobile platforms Define monitoring, observability, and alerting standards for AEP pipelines, identity stitching, segmentation, and activation flows Establish platform guardrails, environment strategy, and best practices for deployment, versioning, and security changes Define the long-term strategy to fix and prevent identity issues across the customer profile ecosystem Define and maintain security models, access control, and compliance configurations within AEP Support AI/ML readiness initiatives including predictive segmentation, enriched attributes, and decisioning integration Influence the multi-year CDP and MarTech roadmap, evaluate vendor capabilities, and guide platform evolution Ensure data quality, governance, privacy, and consent management Partner with marketing, analytics, engineering, security, and vendors on MarTech enablement POSITION QUALIFICATIONS: 7+ years of experience in CDP, MarTech, or Data Architecture roles Hands-on experience with Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) in enterprise environments Strong expertise in XDM data modeling, event and attribute design, and schema governance Experience with identity resolution, identity rules, and customer profile stitching strategies Experience working with Real-Time Customer Profile, segmentation, and audience activation Strong experience with Adobe Journey Optimizer (AJO) for journey design and orchestration Experience with batch and streaming data ingestion patterns within AEP Solid understanding of enterprise MarTech architecture and system integrations Experience integrating CDP with CRM systems, analytics platforms, and marketing activation tools Knowledge of data governance, privacy, consent management, and compliance requirements Experience defining or supporting monitoring, observability, or data quality practices Ability to work effectively with both technical teams and marketing stakeholders Preferred / Nice to Have: Experience with Adobe Customer Journey Analytics (CJA) Adobe AEP / AJO certifications Experience with data governance, privacy regulations (GDPR / CCPA) Exposure to CI/CD, automation, or DevOps for MarTech platforms Retail, eCommerce, or large-enterprise MarTech experience Ideal Candidate Profile Deep Adobe CDP and MarTech expertise (not generic data engineering) Comfortable owning platform standards, governance, and best practices Able to translate business use cases into scalable CDP and journey designs BENEFITS & PERKS: Competitive healthcare, dental & vision insurance 401(k) matching after one year of employment Generous time off + company holidays Merchandise discount Learning & Development programs Much more!

title: Associate Editor, Digital Content
description: WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times. Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally. WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH Reporting to the Practice Editor, Digital Content, the Associate Editor creates, posts, maintains, updates, and curates Bain’s digital content in various formats and on various online channels. They have a good understanding of digital presentation and visual formats (e.g., infographics, snap charts) and collaborate with the Editorial Data Visualization Team, Brand Design, and Digital COEs to develop such content. To learn more about the broader Marketing team at Bain, visit our careers site: Marketing. WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM This role requires an understanding of content strategy, the ability to learn and use data visualization tools effectively, the ability to effectively communicate with a non-technical audience, and comfort with using AI as an assistant in editorial tasks. The Associate Editor consistently meets deadlines, manages their time effectively, and proactively identifies and resolves conflicts or roadblocks. WHAT YOU’LL DO Producing high-quality online content that reflects Bain's industry or capability points of view in multiple formats, especially shorter, visual formats; building content from the ground up, repurposing existing content; managing content development and production for practice priority publishing projects, maximizing presentation and online value of all content, securing appropriate rights for external digital content, adjusting the mix of content based on analytics, and understanding the performance of Bain online content. (~75%) Managing daily and weekly postings and other activities: making routine site improvements to Bain.com using the Episerver content management system, overseeing the work of interns preparing final HTML, assuming primary responsibility for regular curation and maintenance of microsites, curating the Insights home page and pages for Bain’s practices, and ensuring that all projects are properly tracked in Kapost. (25%) ABOUT YOU This role follows a hybrid model, requiring in-office presence at least 2 days per week. Required High school degree Experience working in an online publishing environment, with direct experience working with online content management systems Experience with Episerver a plus, but not required Should have experience and feel comfortable meeting deadlines and working in cross-functional teams Strong writing skills Fluency in online content Strong organizational and multitasking skills Online project management skills Strong knowledge of MS Office tools, i.e., Word, PowerPoint, and Excel Familiarity with generative AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, etc.) Working knowledge or experience with online and creative publishing platforms, e.g., content management systems, WordPress, Adobe Suite (including Photoshop), HTMP, etc. Ability to work collaboratively within and across teams and influence others Ability to communicate with key stakeholders effectively in an open, honest and direct fashion Ability to generate solutions, not just identify problems Demonstrate a high commitment to customer service Maintain a high degree of team spirit, pitching in where needed and respecting all contributing members of the team Ability to interact with staff at all levels in a fast paced environment; to work under pressure; and to remain flexible, proactive, resourceful and efficient, demonstrating the highest level of professionalism Ability to effectively collaborate globally through virtual media Self-starter with proven ability to work independently, yet demonstrates team-oriented approach and understanding Proactive thinker and doer on process improvement Preferred BA-level degree in journalism, English, communications, or marketing or related field Professional journalism or writing experience with top-tier organizations, with a focus on business content; background in general business press Experience with OptiServer (formerly EpiServer) content management platform Experience with data visualization tools including Flourish, Infogram, Tableau Familiarity with content workflow management systems (e.g., Kapost, Opti, SmartSheet, Trello, etc.) U.S. Compensation Information Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below). Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range to be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows: In Atlanta, GA, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $58,250-$70,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level (AIS may eliminate this piece due to no range) In Chicago, IL,** the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $64,250-$77,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level (AIS may eliminate this piece due to no range) Annual discretionary performance bonus This role may also be eligible for other elements of discretionary compensation 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, which increases after 3 years of service and is 100% vested upon start date Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family. Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays Fully vested 401(k) company contribution Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance Annual fitness reimbursements

title: Senior Content Manager (Audiobooks)
description: We’re looking for a Senior Content Manager (Audiobooks) to help us bring the best possible content to our customers! About Yoto Yoto is a screen-free interactive audio platform for kids. We make carefully connected audio players that kids control, with no microphones, cameras or ads. We have a catalogue of audio that inspires creative play and learning with 1,000+ titles in our card store from top creators, publishers and labels - think Disney, Marvel, Roald Dahl and Universal Music. We also create Yoto Originals like BrainBots. Voted the 14th fastest-growing private software technology company in Britain in The Sunday Times 100 Tech 2025; and named the 94th fastest-growing company in Europe in the prestigious FT1000. Ranked 9th in Fast Company's Most Innovative Companies in 2024; and featured in the Sifted 100 fastest-growing startups across the UK&I by revenue growth. Featured in Bloomberg’s 25 UK startups to watch in 2023; and ranked second fastest growing tech company in the UK in the Deloitte Fast50. Named one of TIME Magazine’s Top 100 Inventions of 2020. We’re over 200 employees spread across the world.. and growing! We’re on a mission to be the soundtrack of childhood, and help families on their own unique adventures. By creating products that inspire independent play, we help families discover and grow with an inspiring world of audio - and we would like you to help us achieve it! Yoto’s Content Team Content is at the heart of our business, and we have a dedicated team of children’s media specialists – with experience across publishing, music, education, rights, and audio production – who carefully consider every title that makes it into our library. We license existing audio from some of the biggest names in the world of kids’ entertainment (Paramount, Penguin Random House, Hasbro, Macmillan and more), and also develop and produce our own content as part of our Yoto Originals range. What you’ll be doing : Reporting to the Head of Content in North America, you will be responsible for maintaining and developing partnerships with publishers in North America. No two days are the same, but your role will involve a lot of the following: Nurturing partnerships with our current Audiobook publishers. This includes but is not limited to: Taking quarterly meetings with existing partners to discuss performance of their titles, upcoming releases, and future opportunities, Liaising with publisher’s marketing teams to brainstorm opportunities for upcoming releases and backlist titles, Submitting new initiatives for partners’ approval, Keeping partners informed about Yoto’s catalog strategy, and gathering and sharing data about trends and needs in the kids’ audiobook space, Acting as an ambassador for the Yoto brand in industry conferences, Sourcing potential audiobook partners that will help diversify our North American portfolio. Audiobook acquisition: Making strategic decisions about new additions and title renewals, with the goal of making Yoto the best audio platform for children, Creating financial forecasts (P&Ls) for key acquisitions, and keeping track of sales numbers and other available data to report on the performance of existing catalog and identify gaps, Negotiating all deal terms with partners, for new acquisitions as well as renewals, Partnering with our legal team to ensure timely execution of contracts, Making data-based and strategic decisions about which properties to pursue. Working closely with Sr. Manager of Licensed Content and Head of Production to propose quarterly publication schedules a year in advance. Working closely with the Content Coordinator to ensure timely delivery of all assets. Approving all publisher royalty reports and managing stock reordering process for your titles. Coordinating internal processes with different departments. Including but not limited to: Working with the accounting team to ensure timely payment of invoices Proofreading for upcoming products General support for marketing initiatives Providing feedback for retail ranges when needed Creating presentations for the wider Yoto team about licensed audiobooks Liaising with Yoto’s Marketing and Commercial teams to ensure alignment on release schedule. What you’ll bring: Significant experience in children’s editorial or media acquisition Ease converting leads into deals and handling rights negotiations Familiarity with contracts and legal publishing language A demonstrable passion for audiobooks and a finger on the pulse of the current children's audiobook landscape Exceptional English language communication skills Extraordinarily organized, and able to juggle multiple projects at a time Ability to take full ownership of your projects, and great at staying on top of your workload Ability to work independently and also a great team player Comfortable working across multiple software platforms such as Office Suite and Google Suite, Dropbox, Box, Slack, Monday.com, to name a few Working in a fast-paced, constantly-changing environment makes you feel energized. Desired qualifications / experience: LatAm Spanish language skills would be highly desirable but not mandatory Experience managing a small team Familiarity with the educational market Knowledge and working relationships with publishers across Canada Salary: $110,000 - $120,000 based on experience What you can expect from us: Flexibility: Hybrid working with a choice based approach - we would love you to come into the Brooklyn office twice a week, if not more. Take advantage of our ‘work from anywhere’ policy to work from a different location for up to 6 weeks every year. Summer Hours policy to use between the months of June and August. Financial: Receive Noncontributory Long Term Disability Insurance and Life Assurance / AD&D insurance that covers your from day one of service. 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan where Yoto matches up to 4% of your contributions, from day one of service. Generous employee referral bonus scheme. Health & Wellness: Medical, Vision and dental insurance where 85% of costs for you and your family paid for by Yoto; and a Wellbeing Allowance to be used for memberships, subscriptions, treatment or therapy. Access to 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) through Health Advocate. Time Off: 31 PTO days per year, which include 20 vacation days, plus 11 holidays. On top of this, you also receive your birthday off, plus 1 extra day of holiday for every year worked after you hit your 2 year anniversary, up to 5 extra days. After 4 years of service, you are also eligible for our sabbatical policy offering up to 3 months' of unpaid sabbatical. Helping Families: Variety of family-friendly leave, including enhanced maternity, paternity, adoption, fertility, pregnancy loss and carers leave. Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account (FSA) which allows you to use pre-tax dollars to pay for out-of-pocket dependent care expenses. People & Planet: Up to 2 volunteer days per year. A Book Allowance of up to $50 a year to contribute to your professional development. Fun Perks: You receive your very own Yoto player and starter pack of cards! Regular company socials and celebrations, including our annual YotoFest! Equity and Diversity At Yoto, we love creating a world where childhood is rich with discovery and joy. We’re also passionate about building a workplace where everyone can thrive - both professionally and personally. We value flexibility, giving our team the freedom to balance life and work in ways that suit them. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable environment where everyone feels valued, respected, and supported. Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion group - sponsored by a member of our Executive Team - meets regularly to amplify diverse voices and perspectives, ensuring that all employees have a space to be heard. We don’t just embrace differences - we celebrate them. From curating inclusive audio content for families (check out our DEI selection) to building a workforce that reflects the diversity of our community, we believe that diverse perspectives drive innovation and make us stronger. At Yoto, hiring decisions are based on skills, experience, and passion. We work continuously to improve representation, remove bias from our processes, and ensure fair evaluation for all candidates. We invite individuals from all backgrounds to explore opportunities with us and be part of a company that champions flexibility, equity, and inclusion. Learn more about our commitment to People and Planet on our website. Please only apply or engage with Yoto jobs posted on our careers page (careers.yotoplay.com or careers.yotoplay.com/en). All correspondence from our Talent team will only come through Teamtailor, our application tracking system.

title: SEO Strategist
description: About the Role We’re looking for a curious, collaborative SEO strategist who enjoys solving problems, experimenting with new tools, and helping websites grow organically. In this role, you’ll work with a variety of client accounts, using both proven SEO techniques and new approaches involving automation and AI. You don’t need to be an AI expert or a developer, just someone who’s excited about where search is going and eager to learn and adapt. This is a hands-on role with a lot of opportunity to make an impact. What You’ll Do Lead and Manage SEO Campaigns Plan and implement SEO strategies across different client websites. Monitor key performance metrics like traffic, rankings, and leads. Work on both technical SEO and content-driven improvements. Explore New Tools and Tactics Try out AI tools and automation platforms to improve SEO efficiency. Experiment with prompt testing, featured snippets, and new types of search results. Look for creative ways to gain visibility beyond traditional search engines. Share Insights and Collaborate Keep up with SEO trends, algorithm changes, and AI developments. Share updates with the team and clients in clear, actionable ways. Help contribute SEO knowledge to reports, internal resources, and training. Tackle Technical SEO Challenges Perform audits and suggest improvements for things like site speed, structure, and schema. Collaborate with developers and designers to ensure clean technical setups. Support AI and SERP visibility through smart tagging and data structure. Support Growth and Team Learning Help build efficient processes, templates, and documentation. Contribute to training, knowledge-sharing, and quality checks. Provide input on internal site improvements, blog content, and reporting. What You’ll Gain A forward-looking role at the intersection of SEO, content, and AI. Opportunities to explore new technologies and experiment with fresh ideas. A supportive team that values learning, creativity, and collaboration. Room to grow into leadership or deeper technical expertise. About FatCat Strategies FatCat Strategies is a full-service marketing agency based in Raleigh, NC, with a focus on home improvement companies. We’ve been around for over 20 years, and many of our clients stick with us for the long haul, which means you’ll get to see the lasting results of your work. We’re a small, supportive team that cares about helping each other grow, not just checking boxes. Our culture is casual, friendly, and focused on doing great work together. Interested? If you’re excited about where SEO is headed and enjoy testing new ideas, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now and help us explore the future of search! Requirements What We’re Looking For You don’t need to check every box to apply, if you’re experienced in SEO, open to learning, and excited about the direction search is heading, we’d love to hear from you. Here’s what will help you succeed in this role: 3–5 years of hands-on SEO experience, including technical, content, and strategic work. Comfortable using SEO tools like Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, Google Search Console, Looker Studio, and Google Analytics. Technical SEO skills, especially within WordPress environments (site structure, speed, tags, indexing, etc.). Working knowledge of structured data and schema markup or a willingness to learn and implement them with guidance. Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, you’ll need to explain SEO ideas clearly to teammates and clients. A self-directed and curious mindset, you enjoy building systems, testing ideas, and improving processes. Bonus Points (Not Required) If you have experience or interest in these areas, great! If not, we’re happy to support your learning: Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) or AI-enhanced search behavior. Prompt testing, embedding concepts, or GPT-based tools and APIs. Using automation tools (like Make.com or Zapier) to improve workflows. Work Location: Hybrid in Raleigh, NC (must be able to work on-site multiple days per week) This role will be strictly in person only for the beginning of your employment until we're both comfortable with being virtual part-time. Schedule: Monday - Friday, hours flexible between 8:00am - 6:00pm Benefits** Compensation will be determined based on your experience, skill set, and the impact you’re positioned to make within our team.

title: Multimedia Specialist
description: We are looking for a Multimedia Specialist! Have you worked as part of a Marketing Team? Have you created video, audio, graphic design, and multimedia content? Do you like to collaborate on new creative ideas? If so, we would like to invite you to consider a career at American 1 Credit Union! Below are more details before you decide to apply: Position: Multimedia Specialist Location: Primarily on-site in Jackson, MI, with possible remote work flexibility after training. Salary: From $55,000 and varies based on qualifications and experience. title: Lifecycle Marketing Manager
description: Who is Credible? We are a marketplace where users can compare personalized, prequalified rates and quotes from multiple lenders and carriers, for student loans, mortgages, personal loans, and insurance. We're challenging the status quo by giving power to the consumer. We believe in a world where 'ethical', 'lending', and 'insurance' can coexist, so we set out to build innovative platforms that actually work for customers. Our mission is to help people find the best loan or insurance policy possible. We believe researching and buying loans or insurance shouldn't be confusing or complex, so we've focused on simplicity. About the Role: Credible is seeking a data-driven, results-oriented Lifecycle Marketing Manager to join our Lifecycle Marketing team. Lifecycle marketing plays a critical role in driving user engagement, conversion, and revenue across our core verticals (Student Loans, Personal Loans) and ancillary products (e.g., credit cards, high-yield savings, HELOC, insurance), with strong executive visibility and measurable impact on company performance. You'll help drive campaign strategy and execution, focusing primarily on email to engage users and move them through the funnel. This is a full-time, individual contributor role reporting into Lifecycle Marketing leadership. The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment, loves working with data, and enjoys cross-functional collaboration to build impactful campaigns. This role offers the opportunity to influence lifecycle strategy and performance across multiple high-impact initiatives. You will: Drive the planning, execution, optimization, and reporting of lifecycle email campaigns Develop and execute automated lifecycle campaigns, including user-triggered drip series, recurring scheduled sends, transactional communications, and one-time campaigns. Develop data-driven audience strategies, including targeting frameworks and eligibility logic to improve campaign accuracy and performance. Write and optimize clear, persuasive email copy tailored to different audience segments, incorporating dynamic content and personalization best practices. Partner with our Marketing Automation Team for technical execution, including template production, journey builds, audience segmentation, and QA. Lead A/B and holdout testing across lifecycle campaigns. Analyze performance to identify key drivers of engagement and revenue, translate insights into actionable recommendations, and communicate results to team and leadership stakeholders. Contribute to recurring lifecycle planning cycles, develop lifecycle strategies (e.g., new communication flows or journey structures), and support the creation of business cases. Collaborate with Business Intelligence, Product, Engineering, and Legal to align on data needs, content, compliance, and timing. Research lifecycle strategies and competitor tactics to identify areas for innovation. Support lifecycle marketing for additional programs, channels, or cross-sell efforts as team needs evolve. Requirements: 5–9 years of experience in lifecycle or email marketing in a B2C environment. Hands-on experience with enterprise marketing automation platforms (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud [preferred], Braze, Iterable, or comparable tools) and working with large databases. Experience modifying basic HTML and using dynamic content. Strong understanding of segmentation, dynamic content, and email personalization strategies. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret campaign performance and apply insights. Knowledgeable of best email practices, including brand consistency, CAN-SPAM, and deliverability. Excellent written and verbal communication skills; strong cross-functional collaborator. Highly detail-oriented and organized; able to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Economics, or related field—or equivalent experience. Nice to have: Experience with financial products such as loans or insurance, or working in a FinTech or startup environment. Familiarity with tools like Looker or Tableau for data visualization. Basic understanding of SQL. Exposure to multi-product or multi-vertical lifecycle strategy. A creative mindset with a test-and-learn approach. Credible is open to hiring candidates in the following locations: California, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Kansas, Kentucky, Maine, Massachusetts, Michigan, Missouri, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oregon, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington Why work at Credible? We combine the intelligence, expertise, and confidence of a financial advisor with the approachability and honesty of a friend. In other words, we're the friend you always wish you had in finance. We are optimistic, challengers, trustworthy, clever, and smart. We are open and transparent. We strive to act as advisors by being friendly, objective, and open in our communication. We use language that is intelligent yet approachable. When appropriate, we'll drop in a bit of wit to position ourselves as a fresh, reliable voice in the financial world. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, gender identity, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories consistent with applicable law.

title: SEO Manager, Organic & AI Search Optimization
description: Summary Of Responsibilities The Manager, Organic & AI Search Optimization role will be responsible for driving FirstKey Homes’ digital visibility across both traditional search engines and emerging AI-powered platforms. The position will lead the development and execution of a unified Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) strategy, combining technical SEO expertise with innovative approaches to AI-driven discovery systems. By positioning FirstKey Homes as a trusted authority in the residential rental market, this role will oversee initiatives that optimize content performance, adapt to evolving algorithms, and ensure the brand remains discoverable and competitive across all digital touchpoints. Essential Duties Lead the strategy and implementation of AEO/GEO initiatives to maximize organic visibility across traditional and generative search environments. Apply deep Technical SEO expertise, including site architecture, structured data, schema markup, page speed optimization, and crawl/index management to ensure site health and discoverability.
