Demonstrated success optimizing content for traditional search engines (Google, Bing) as well as modern AI platforms (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview, Voice Assistants). Strong understanding of how search engines and generative AI systems surface and prioritize content. Experience working in or with enterprise-level websites, preferably in real estate, property management, B2C service industries, or omnichannel with a local search focus. Proficiency with SEO and analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, GSC, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog). Proven ability to translate complex digital concepts into actionable strategies for cross-functional teams. Strong communication, leadership, and project management skills. Experience with large datasets, recommending actions and synthetizing insights. Proven ability to manage data and extract exceptions for targeted performance management. Highly process oriented with passion to investigate root causes, driving process improvement, and enabling change management. Experience working within a multi-disciplined, cross-functional technical organization. Exceptional verbal and written communication skills with proven ability to effectively influence staff at all levels. Required Skills Critical Thinking — Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions or approaches to problems. Complex Problem Solving — Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions. Mathematics — Using mathematics to solve problems. Active Listening — Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times. Monitoring — Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action. Preferred Education And Experience Exposure to AI/LLM-driven search environments and how generative models influence discoverability Experience in highly regulated or location-based industries such as property management, housing, or financial services, where compliance and accuracy are critical Familiarity with voice search optimization and conversational AI trends, including emerging platforms and assistant technologies Knowledge of local search dynamics and strategies for scaling visibility across multi-market footprints WORK STYLES & BEHAVIORS Leadership — Job requires a willingness to lead, take charge, and offer opinions and direction. title: Brand Manager, NY
description: Sluggers, Brand Manager New York Sluggers Hit is taking over the New York market. We don’t just open doors; we own them. We are looking for a high-octane Marketing Manager to lead our sell-through strategy across the state. This isn't a "behind a desk" job—it’s a boots-on-the-ground, hustle-heavy role focused on making sure Sluggers is the most visible, best-trained, and fastest-moving brand in every dispensary we enter. The Mission Your goal is simple: Maximize sell-through. You will own the brand’s presence at the retail level. Once our production partner and their sales team get us on the shelf, it’s your job to ensure we stay at the top of the menu and the front of the budtender’s mind. Key Responsibilities Retail Domination: Own the "look and feel" of Sluggers in-store. This includes high-impact merchandising, securing premium shelf placement, and ensuring all physical and digital menus are accurate, updated, and visually on-point. Product Evangelism: Conduct high-energy, "Sluggers-style" product trainings for budtenders. You aren't just teaching specs; you’re building a fan base and creating excitement for the brand. Digital & In-Store Real Estate: Negotiate and execute online marketing banners on store websites and take full advantage of internal retail marketing programs (loyalty apps, newsletters, etc.). Strategic Partnerships: Build bulletproof relationships with store managers, buyers, and marketing directors. You are the face of the brand to our retail partners. Sales/Production Synergy: Work hand-in-hand with our New York production partner and their sales team to ensure a seamless handoff from "opening the door" to "owning the shelf." Team Leadership: As the market grows, you will be responsible for hiring, training, and managing a team of brand ambassadors who share your level of intensity and attention to detail. Who You Are The Sluggers Mentality: You are tough, you hustle, and you take it personally if a competitor has better placement than us. You represent the brand as if it’s your own. Culture-First: You are deeply in tune with the cannabis culture and understand the nuances of the NY dispensary landscape. The "Closer" of Sell-Through: You have a proven track record of moving products at the retail level through creative promotions and relentless follow-up. A Natural Leader: You can command a room during a training session and mentor a team to live up to your high standards. Organized & Aggressive: You can manage multiple store accounts and digital menus while never losing your "street-level" edge. Requirements Based in or around the NYC/New York market. 3+ years of experience in cannabis marketing, retail management, or high-volume brand activation. Strong existing relationships within the NY cannabis retail community are a major plus. Ability to travel frequently to dispensaries across the state. Valid driver’s license and a "no excuses" attitude. Managerial Responsibility This position does have managerial responsibilities and will oversee a team. Travel Requirements Light travel is expected for this position to attend marketing events and to visit corporate on occasion. Physical Requirements While performing the duties of this job employees will be required to walk, stand for sustained periods, and lift and carry at least 50 pounds. Employees will be required to use hands, fingers and arms to grasp, pull, twist, reach, and push as needed to operate basic tools such as wrenches, screwdrivers, torque wrenches, etc. Employees must be able to push, pull, and stop carts and trolleys. Employees must be able to see, hear, and communicate basic operational and safety instructions. Employees may be required to lay on the floor or climb stairs. Employees may be required to wear hearing, eye, respiratory, hot/cold, or fall protection. This position is regularly exposed to raw cannabis flower and other cannabis derived materials and products. Other Duties Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice. Work Authorization/Security Clearance There is no visa or H-1B sponsorship. Natura embraces diverse perspectives, and we value unique human experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer that celebrates the diversity of the communities where we live and do business.

title: Director Brand Strategy & Marketing
description: About Formhouse: Formhouse is a brand incubator that transforms cultural influence into enduring consumer businesses. We partner with leading creators and celebrities to launch original apparel and lifestyle brands built on clear identity and real consumer value. Founded by brand builders who have scaled and exited highly visible tech and consumer companies, including one of the most successful entrepreneurs of this generation, we build brands with culture at the center. Our approach blends strong design, fast development, deep collaboration, and a focused point of view on product and storytelling. About the Role: We’re hiring a Brand & Growth Marketing Lead to build and launch new direct-to-consumer apparel brands from the ground up. This is a project-based role with the opportunity to grow into a full-time Head of Brand position based on launch results. This is for an entrepreneurial operator who thrives in early-stage environments and knows how to take a brand from concept to real revenue. Build and lead influencer, ambassador, and cultural partnership programs that generate credibility, demand, and early brand heat. Develop community loops that convert early customers into long-term advocates. Measure, Optimize, and Scale Performance Define launch KPIs across awareness, engagement, conversion, retention, and sell-through. Establish testing frameworks across messaging, creative, and drop strategy to improve performance. Analyze traffic, engagement, and revenue data to inform iteration and scale. Own post-launch reporting and optimization across future collections. Leadership experience in brand strategy, brand marketing, or launch leadership within DTC apparel, fashion, or lifestyle brands. Ability to define brand positioning, product storytelling, and drop strategy within a competitive apparel landscape. Elevated creative taste with experience directing campaigns, styling, and premium visual systems. title: Director, Integrated Campaigns
description: (Expected to work East Coast hours with flexibility across time zones) Role Overview We are seeking a strategic, analytical, and execution-oriented Director of Integrated Campaigns to drive multi-channel, ICP-focused campaigns that generate Stage 2 qualified pipeline and support revenue growth. This role blends big-picture strategic planning with hands-on campaign execution , rigorous performance measurement , and cross-functional collaboration . The ideal candidate will have deep experience with digital channels, strong analytics and reporting capabilities, impeccable communication and writing skills, and proven leadership managing marketing professionals. This individual will partner closely with Product Marketing , Sales , SDRs , Partners , and Marketing Operations/RevOps to ensure campaigns are well aligned, impactful, and measured against business outcomes — with success defined by measurable increases in qualified pipeline within our Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) . Key Responsibilities Integrated Campaign Strategy Architect, deploy, and optimize multi-channel integrated campaigns across digital (paid, email, content), events, webinars, partner channels, and ABM-oriented programs that drive Stage 2 qualified pipeline in our ICP. Deploy campaigns for new logos, qualified opportunities in the funnel and existing customers. Build campaign frameworks, GTM plans, goals, KPIs, and SLAs aligned to quarterly and annual objectives; measure and iterate based on data and business priorities. Ensure consistency of messaging and orchestration across all channels and at all stages of the buyer journey. Partner closely with GoFish digital agency to ensure digital marketing channels are optimized and to align paid search with organic search. Leverage AI vendors and technology to execute campaigns more efficiently, reach new buyers and support ABM efforts. Analytics, Attribution & Reporting Own weekly performance reporting and quarterly business review presentations to marketing and executive leadership, including pipeline contribution, conversion metrics, and ROI. Use dashboards to monitor campaign performance, lead quality, and funnel progression. Partner with RevOps/Marketing Ops to maintain lead routing and funnel hygiene. Cross-Functional Enablement & Collaboration Collaborate with Product Marketing to translate value propositions, ICP segmentation, and competitive differentiation into campaign messaging and content. Enable Sales and SDRs with campaign collateral, messaging templates, talking points, and training so they can engage leads effectively and convert them to pipeline. Establish and maintain strong alignment on goals, workflow SLAs, and feedback loops with Sales leadership. Present on weekly Pipeline Day call to review recent leads and cover upcoming activities with Sales. Channel Execution & Optimization Lead execution and optimization across digital channels including paid search, paid social, display, content syndication, email nurture, and SEO-driven tactics. Ensure testing, segmentation, and personalization tactics are in place to improve performance and engagement. Partner with GoFish digital agency to optimize existing paid digital channels (paid social, display, paid search) and test new digital channels (direct-to-publisher engagements, podcasts, CTV, Reddit, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, etc.). Leadership & Team Development Manage, mentor, and develop at least one direct report, championing best practices, career growth, and results. Drive process improvements and standardization of campaign playbooks, documentation, and operational excellence. Required Qualifications 7+ years of experience in integrated campaigns, demand generation, growth marketing, or related roles — ideally in B2B SaaS or technology marketing. Proven track record of leading multi-channel integrated campaigns that generate measurable Stage 2 qualified pipeline or equivalent funnel outcomes. Strong analytics and reporting expertise, with the ability to build dashboards, interpret funnel metrics, and derive insights to inform strategic decisions. Deep experience with Salesforce CRM and Marketo (or equivalent MAP) — required. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience crafting compelling campaign copy, executive summaries, and performance write-ups. Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners and influence outcomes without direct authority. Nice-to-Have Experience Familiarity with Qualified chatbot platform , Demandbase (or other ABM platforms) , and Heap or similar analytical tools. Experience with ABM, existing-customer and partner-centric campaigns. Prior experience in cybersecurity or enterprise B2B purchasing cycles. Success Metrics Achievement of quarterly pipeline generation targets within ICP segments. Improvement in lead quality and conversion rates from lead to meeting to qualified opportunity to closed won revenue. Efficiency gains in campaign ROI and channel performance over time. Positive feedback from Sales and SDR teams on campaign enablement and pipeline support. About HUMAN Security HUMAN was founded in a Brooklyn sci-fi bookstore with a hacker mindset and the lofty mission of making the internet safer by putting humans first. Today, our Human Defense Platform safeguards enterprises from sophisticated bots, fraud, and account abuse, verifying the humanity of more than 20 trillion interactions per week for the world’s biggest brands and internet platforms. HUMAN is an equal opportunity workplace dedicated to protecting the internet's integrity for everyone. We believe in putting people first, embracing diversity of thought from our global teams, and welcoming all individuals to share their unique experiences as we fight cybercrime together. We support our Humans with a comprehensive total rewards package for personal and professional development, including well-being and learning stipends, flexible work options, and dedicated time off. While our HQ is in NYC, we have teams worldwide. HUMAN is growing fast, and there’s never been a more meaningful time to join us. If you thrive on solving complex problems and want to help shape the future of cybersecurity, join us as we build a safer, more human internet. If you are an individual with a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please contact us directly. Compensation Range: $150K - $185K

This role is critical in expanding our reach through storytelling and performance-driven media. This role is being offered on a contract basis with an expected duration of 3-4 months with potential to expand based on business need and requiring 40 hours of work per week. Acorns is a remote-first organization , offering the flexibility to work remotely while providing optional access to office space in Irvine, CA. What you will do at Acorns: Manage and scale our Podcast program by overseeing our existing agency partner to optimize performance and reach new audiences. Drive CTV strategy and execution , working directly with partners like Hulu and Paramount to expand our television presence and track attribution. Lead the Creator Program , focusing on scaling a diverse range of micro to large-scale creators to grow both our organic and whitelisted paid social presence. Collaborate cross-functionally with Data Analytics to measure performance, Compliance to ensure all content meets financial regulations, and Brand/Creative to develop high-performing assets. Deliver on Growth OKRs by relentlessly pursuing progress and optimizing spend across emerging and established channels. Execute social media whitelisting strategies to amplify creator content and drive efficient customer acquisition. What you will bring to Acorns: Proven experience in Paid Media , specifically within Podcast, CTV/Linear TV, or Creator-led marketing. Deep industry knowledge of social media whitelisting and the nuances of working with creators to drive measurable performance. Strong analytical skills , with the ability to partner with data teams to interpret results and make data-driven investment decisions. Experience navigating compliance-heavy environments (FinTech or Finance experience is a plus). Hunger to deliver game-changing products and marketing campaigns. Exceptional drive and precision in delivery , ensuring all campaigns are launched accurately and on time. A belief that your work is tied to your life's mission and a desire to help families build wealth. Optimistic about the potential of societal change through financial wellness. You are not expected to have experience with all listed requirements. If you feel passionate about Acorns' mission, vision, and values, please apply. What we offer: Competitive hourly rates. Talented and motivated team who care deeply about one another, our mission, and our customers. The rare opportunity to create a new world, solving big societal challenges through meaningful work. About Acorns: Acorns is a financial wellness app that helps everyday people and families save and invest money for the long term. Since 2014, Acorns has grown into a global company with multiple life-stage products serving the needs of kids, teens, adults, and parents. Named one of Time’s “World’s Best Brands of 2024,” Acorns has helped over 14 million people save & invest over $25 million dollars, much of it from spare change and small amounts. Compensation Information: The pay range for this position at commencement of employment is expected to be between $55/hour and $60/hour ; commensurate with experience and qualifications. Company reserves the right to modify hourly rate at any time, including for reasons related to individual performance, Company or individual department/team performance, and market factors. Work Authorization: Acorns participates in E-Verify to confirm the employment eligibility of all newly hired employees. For more information about E-Verify, please visit www.e-verify.gov.

title: Search Engine Optimization Specialist
description: Baseballism is seeking a full-time, on-site SEO & AI Search Optimization Specialist to lead our search visibility strategy across traditional search engines and AI-powered discovery platforms. This role will focus heavily on modern search dynamics including AI ranking, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), entity optimization, semantic search, and visibility within large language model ecosystems. This is not a back-office SEO role. This position will collaborate across marketing, creative, e-commerce, product, and leadership teams to drive digital awareness, traffic growth, and long-term brand authority. The right candidate will be a strategic thinker, technical executor, and internal educator who helps shape how Baseballism shows up across the evolving digital landscape. Key Responsibilities AI Search & AEO Leadership • Develop and execute strategies to improve Baseballism’s visibility in AI-generated search results (Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, etc.). • Implement Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) strategies focused on structured content, entity authority, and semantic depth. • Optimize for zero-click search, featured snippets, knowledge panels, and conversational search experiences. • Monitor and adapt to algorithm shifts in both traditional and AI-powered search environments. Technical & On-Site SEO • Lead technical SEO initiatives including site architecture, crawl optimization, indexing strategy, internal linking structure, and schema markup. • Optimize product pages, collections, landing pages, and editorial content for high-intent search queries. • Implement structured data and schema to enhance search visibility and machine readability. • Partner with developers to ensure site speed, Core Web Vitals, and mobile performance are best-in-class. Content & Entity Strategy • Collaborate with the content and creative teams to build high-authority baseball-focused content hubs. • Conduct advanced keyword and entity research aligned with brand positioning and product strategy. • Identify opportunities to rank for baseball lifestyle, youth apparel, and culture-driven search terms. • Build evergreen content strategies designed to establish Baseballism as the authoritative baseball lifestyle brand online. Digital Awareness & Cross-Platform Optimization • Drive search visibility beyond Google — including YouTube, TikTok search, Pinterest, marketplace search, and emerging AI platforms. • Align SEO initiatives with paid media, email marketing, and social campaigns to amplify reach and impact. • Support product launches, seasonal campaigns, and key growth initiatives with search-first strategy. • Track, analyze, and report on organic performance metrics, AI citation visibility, and overall digital footprint. Cross-Functional Collaboration • Work closely with Marketing, E-Commerce, Product Development, Creative, and Leadership teams to align digital growth initiatives. • Educate internal teams on modern SEO and AI search trends. • Serve as the internal authority on search performance and organic growth strategy. • Contribute to strategic planning for long-term digital expansion. Qualifications • 3–5+ years of experience in SEO, with demonstrated success in driving organic traffic growth. • Deep understanding of technical SEO, structured data, and semantic search. • Experience optimizing for AI-driven search results and answer engines. • Strong analytical skills and proficiency with tools such as Google Search Console, GA4, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar. • Familiarity with schema implementation, entity mapping, and content architecture strategy. • Experience in DTC e-commerce is strongly preferred. • Passion for baseball culture and sports apparel is a plus. Key Attributes • Strategic thinker with a strong technical foundation. • Data-driven but brand-aware. • Comfortable working cross-functionally in a collaborative, on-site environment. • Proactive, innovative, and adaptable to the rapidly evolving AI search landscape. • Committed to continuous learning in search, AI, and digital marketing. What Success Looks Like • Measurable growth in organic traffic and revenue. • Increased visibility in AI-generated search summaries and answer engines. • Improved rankings for high-value baseball and apparel-related search terms. • A clearly defined and defensible digital authority position within the baseball lifestyle space. Why Baseballism Baseballism is more than apparel — it is a culture brand built on storytelling, tradition, and respect for the game. This role offers the opportunity to shape how the brand is discovered in a world where AI-driven search is redefining digital awareness. We are looking for someone who sees the future of search clearly and wants to build something lasting within it.

title: Ecommerce Manager
description: ABOUT FILSON In 1897 C.C. Filson began outfitting prospectors headed for the Gold Rush with tools and apparel strong enough for the toughest spots on earth. More than 100 years later, Filson remains committed to providing pioneers of all fields around the world with unfailing goods made from the highest quality materials available. Filson recognizes that it takes a world-class team to make a world-class product. As we build on the Filson legacy of American craftsmanship, we are looking for individuals who share our dedication to innovation, excellence, and superior customer service. We don't work in silos but rely on open collaboration. It's a jackknife mentality – the ability to accomplish more than what's asked of you – that defines our culture of versatility and perseverance. Our outfitter mindset is deliberate and strategic. We are a small company, on purpose. We're the ones doing the work, from product design and marketing, to cutting, sewing, and restoration. With more than 125 years under our belt, we still have a start-up mentality. Driven by an independent spirit and an uncompromising attitude that demands we lead with conviction. and launched on time Serve as the primary point of accountability for ecommerce issues, bugs, and operational breakdowns Monitor and resolve data or system issues across Shopify, OMS, inventory feeds, and analytics Merchandising & Launch Management Oversee ecommerce merchandising execution, prioritization, and quality Ensure seasonal launches, capsules, and campaigns are fully merchandised and quickly Conduct post-campaign and post-launch retrospectives, documenting learnings and next steps Maintain dashboards and reporting cadence for stakeholders Vendor & Agency Management Manage quality, and budget Cross-Functional Coordination Serve as the day-to-day ecommerce lead for Marketing, Creative, Technology, CX, Retail, and Planning teams Coordinate dependencies and timelines to ensure ecommerce initiatives launch cleanly Escalate decisions or trade-offs when needed, with clear recommendations Team Leadership Manage and develop direct reports, providing clear priorities, feedback, and accountability Build repeatable processes to improve speed, clarity, and execution quality QUALIFICATIONS 4–7+ years of ecommerce experience, preferably in DTC or retail Strong hands-on experience with Shopify Proven ability to run day-to-day ecommerce operations independently Experience managing ecommerce launches, promotions, and site updates Strong understanding of ecommerce KPIs and funnel performance Experience working with CRO, analytics, and experimentation programs Comfortable coordinating across Creative, Marketing, Tech, and Operations Experience managing vendors or agencies Strong organizational skills with a bias toward action and follow-through Ability to make decisions with incomplete information and own outcomes Outdoor, apparel, or premium consumer brand experience is a plus Note: This job description is not meant to be all-inclusive. description: DESCRIPTION Amazon Devices is one of the most innovative and fastest growing businesses at Amazon. The Amazon Devices team develops relationships with retailers to expand product selection and distribution in physical locations where customers can experience all of Amazon's devices first hand in the real world. As an Account Manager, you’ll be responsible for both managing existing accounts and onboarding new accounts for Amazon Devices. You will build creative solutions for business requirements across all of Amazon's exciting new Devices. You will own the creation of sales and marketing strategies, promotional activities and deployment strategies. This will require you to work with both internal and external teams’ product, business development, sales, and logistics teams to drive financially viable, high volume programs that provide our channel partners, distributors, and end users with top-tier business solutions. You will represent your business's success in meetings with leadership and meet regularly with our channel partners' leadership to develop long term strategies as well as drive short term program executions. Other responsibilities include: Manage existing relationships to sell and promote Amazon devices and accessories. Negotiate trading terms (e.g., discount rate, advertising, placement, returns provisions, payment terms, freight terms) for assigned accounts Lead internal and external business reviews covering strategic initiatives Create initial sales forecasts, determine allocations, and plan shipments for each SKU Optimize allocation of marketing development funds Collect business and market intelligence You will get a chance to show off your abilities including: Executive communication skills, both written and oral/presentation. Solid understanding of financial analyses Independence and flexibility High attention to detail and proven ability to manage multiple, competing priorities simultaneously Willingness to roll up one's sleeves to get the job done Well-timed tenacity and conviction BASIC QUALIFICATIONS 3+ years of digital advertising and client facing roles experience Bachelor's degree Experience analyzing data and best practices to assess performance drivers PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS 3+ years of client or vendor facing roles with a focus in relationship management and negotiation skills experience Experience aggregating and analyzing internal and external campaign metrics to assess performance drivers and identify insights Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several US geographic markets. The base pay for this position ranges from $59,400/year in our lowest geographic market up to $127,000/year in our highest geographic market. Pay is based on a number of factors including market location and may vary depending on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Amazon is a total compensation company. Dependent on the position offered, equity, sign-on payments, and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package, in addition to a full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits. For more information, please visit https://www.aboutamazon.com/workplace/employee-benefits. This position will remain posted until filled. Applicants should apply via our internal or external career site.

title: Search Engine Optimization Manager
description: About the Company We are Jakala: an award-winning organization that offers world's leading brands support in the strategy, analytics, digital and technology fields. Our mission is to create competitive advantage for our clients, with a measurable impact on the top line, through the best combined use of Data, Advanced and Location Analytics, technologies, content and customer experience design. We are 3,500 people in 20 countries around the world, growing at a fast-pace. About the Position We’re setting up our Digital & Media operations in the US market and are looking for an experienced SEO Manager to join our growing team of experts at our Miami office. The SEO manager will be responsible for leading the client relationships, managing the development and execution of their SEO strategy, as well as supporting the Global Team on occasion with pitch meetings, and mentoring junior members of the team. Key Responsibilities: Develop excellent relationships with the clients, through developing an in-depth understanding of their businesses, challenges they face, and spotting future opportunities Lead the planning and implementation of innovative SEO campaigns towards the KPIs set for the SEO services, and ensure the service is delivered against these goals and contractual requirements Perform SEO focused analysis on industry, competitors, keywords and search trends to inform and influence SEO strategy and optimization proposals Required Experience: Produce detailed and strong analytical tech and editorial SEO audit Perform SEO focused analysis on industry, competitors, keywords and search trends to inform and influence SEO strategy and optimisation proposals Analyse business requirements and match them with data to propose the best performing SEO project’s solutions Optimize texts, semantical tags and metadata to better match users’ queries and increase website’s ranking Monitor site health and directly fix technical issues Follow pre-launch and launch of new websites from a SEO-compliancy point of view, supporting developers, and creating efficient migration and redirect plans Develop outreach opportunities, perform backlink analysis and identify and apply a strategic approach to building strong profiles for clients in obtaining new high-quality links Develop and apply local SEO strategies, including managing GMB accounts, analysing the performance, and producing content. Deliver best practice SEO across Google’s YouTube Channel and support with video content strategies with SEO performance focus Introduce continuous innovation to our SEO services, by onboarding new methodologies, tech partners and best practices Plan, attend and lead client meetings, QBRs and annual reviews Review the current operational flows, propose and implement improvements where relevant Qualifications and Requirements: 7+ years of previous track record in SEO, covering: Technical SEO management for e-commerce and/or multi-country websites Search tools (e.g. Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and etc.) Analytics tools (e.g. Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, and etc.) Local SEO strategies Developing SEO content and link building strategies Reporting on SEO performance, potentially via using data visualisation tools (e.g. Google Data Studio, Tableau or Datorama etc.) Client management Working knowledge of ranking factors and search engine algorithms and up to date with the latest trends and best practices in SEO Knowledge of various CMS for administrating meta changes, copy changes, links and image tagging Basic knowledge of HTML, CSS and other web programming platforms would be considered a plus High level of proficiency in MS Office A passion for Digital Marketing, innovative digital solutions, and uncovering trends in data with analysis Experience in managing, training and upskilling team members Excellent organizational, project management and problem-solving skills Confident and articulate presenter, with the ability to present to senior clients and internal agency teams Self-motivated and able to work under own initiative Ability to meet deadlines in a fast-paced dynamic industry Must be able to travel at least 15-25% of the time Nice to Haves: Combined experience of working in an agency environment and as a freelance Additional languages (e.g. Italian, German, French, Spanish, etc.) Does this sound like you? Send your resume over. We’d love to chat! At Jakala, we are committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all individuals regardless of their race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other legally protected status. We celebrate diversity and strive to create an inclusive workplace where all employees feel valued and respected. We also understand that accommodations may be necessary to ensure equal access to employment opportunities. If you require accommodations to apply for a position or participate in the hiring process, please contact us at jobhelp@jakala.com. Note: Because of the high frequency of travel required by this role, our ideal candidate will live near a major travel hub. Preference will be given to applicants in or around major cities with airports.

title: Sr. Manager, SEO
description: Reporting to: Director, Ecommerce Job Description: Lumens is hiring a Sr. Manager, SEO to drive growth in our SEO program and lead a small team of passionate marketers. Optimizing organic traffic and revenue has always been core to our marketing strategy, and all levels of the company are vested in the success of SEO. The Sr. Manager, SEO is a key leader on the eCommerce team and will shape our future SEO/AIO strategy across technical SEO, content, and backlinking initiatives. This role will develop a program that drives growth in organic revenue and ensures Lumens is recognized as a leader and authority in our space. This data-driven and analytical SEO leader will establish targets for key SEO & AIO metrics, and measure performance improvements against those targets. Responsibilities: Meet or exceed revenue and growth goals Define and align SEO/AIO strategy across Marketing, Merchandising, Product, Technology, and Site Experience teams Lead the Technical SEO/AIO roadmap planning process, converting strategies into a single product delivery roadmap that is aligned with program goals. Develop and communicate detailed Technical SEO business requirements Research SEO/AIO best practices and Google algorithm updates to understand the implications of site projects; partner with the Product and Technology teams to communicate SEO best practices for web development; participate in the site release process to QA projects in the releases for SEO compatibility Develop onsite product and content optimization plans with the Brand and Site teams Lead, develop and measure the SEO/AIO content strategy Lead, manage and develop a small team Create a focused backlinking strategy and execution plan to obtain high quality backlinks from high domain authority sites. Own SEO/AIO related tools, and constantly leverage to monitor SEO-related issues, performance, and program opportunities Establish targets for key SEO/AIO metrics, measure performance improvements against those targets; develop monthly reporting to show progress against goals Responsible for team career development and morale Inform company of industry updates; be proactive in SEO/AIO research and engage with SEO blogs / forums to keep ahead of the curve and up to date on industry changes. Core Characteristics: Ability to develop SEO/AIO strategy, break down to achievable tasks/projects, set goals, regularly measure progress against goals, and reprioritize as needed Results oriented with a growth mindset Strong organizational skills Capable of working across multiple software applications and projects (eg., SEMrush, Botify, GSC, AirOps) Takes initiative; is resourceful in finding solutions Impactful and able to influence and drive tech prioritization Excellent verbal and written communication skills Comfortable in a dynamic, growing company environment A personal interest in modern design, furniture and home furnishings is encouraged Qualifications: 8+ years of SEO experience, within e-commerce / retail space Demonstrated strength in analytical abilities Team management experience Experience in working cross-functionally effectively in a mid-size organization Location: Willingness to work out of the corporate office located in San Francisco / Sacramento (with some work from home flexibility). Compensation: $120,000 to $140,000 annually + bonus Benefits: access to health benefits + 401k + 3 weeks annual paid time off About Lumens: As North America’s premier destination for design aficionados, architects, interior designers, and contract professionals, Lumens offers a discerning edit of modern lighting, furniture, and décor. Sourcing more than 400 of the most exceptional international design brands, our curated product assortment features iconic designs as well as the latest in contemporary interior decor. Our team includes certified interior designers and ALA-certified product specialists, and we’re looking for fellow design enthusiasts to join us. Since June 2021, Lumens has been part of Flos B&B Italia Group, a leading global high-end design group operating a portfolio of legacy brands in the world of luxury furnishings and lighting. Driven by a purpose of “We design for a beautiful life,” the Group includes some of the most distinctive European cultural heritage products and designs for the planet, people, and culture. The Group includes FLOS, B&B Italia, Louis Poulsen, Maxalto, Arclinea, Azucena, FENDI Casa, Audo Copenhagen, and Lumens. Commitments: People are at the heart of our business. We strive for an empowering and positive culture and working environment for our people. Therefore, creating a strong, diverse and inclusive team is essential for Lumens. For us, your expertise and motivation matter most. Lumens is also committed to reducing our environmental impact and preserving our planet. We support brands and products with sustainability attributes at their heart, and we are harnessing our scale and ethos of innovation to bring customers services and solutions that respect the environment. For our employees, our initiatives include green campuses and flexible working to reduce our carbon footprint.

title: Director of Events
description: Director of Events (Restaurants) We are seeking an experienced and creative Director of Events to lead and grow the events business across our restaurant concepts. This role is ideal for a hospitality professional with a strong background in restaurant events, private dining, and brand-driven experiences. About the Role The Director of Events will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the events strategy, from sales and client relationships to execution and team leadership. This individual will work closely with operations, culinary, marketing, and ownership to ensure seamless, high-quality events that drive revenue and elevate the guest experience. Key Responsibilities Develop and execute the overall events strategy for restaurant locations Lead and manage the events sales process, including inquiries, proposals, contracts, and follow-ups Build and maintain strong relationships with corporate clients, planners, and VIP guests Oversee planning and execution of private events, buyouts, and special activations Collaborate with culinary and operations teams to ensure flawless event delivery Manage and mentor events and/or sales team members Track event performance, budgets, and revenue goals Identify opportunities to grow private dining and events revenue across concepts Qualifications 5+ years of experience in restaurant events, catering, or hospitality sales Proven success driving events revenue in a restaurant or hospitality environment Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills Client-focused mindset with excellent attention to detail Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-volume environment Flexible schedule, including evenings and weekends as needed Why Join Us Opportunity to shape and grow a dynamic events program Collaborative, hospitality-driven culture Competitive compensation and growth potential If you are passionate about hospitality, events, and creating memorable guest experiences, we’d love to hear from you. 👉 Apply or message us directly to start the conversation.**

title: Marketing Content Manager / Copywriter – Wallet, Payments, and Commerce
description: Summary The Wallet, Payments, and Commerce (WPC) Marketing team is seeking a Marketing Content Manager to develop and expand worldwide consumer messaging programs. Our mission is to build thoughtful, incredible customer experiences that inform and engage users of Apple Wallet and associated products, including Apple Pay. You will write and maintain product benefit statements, marketing claims, and messaging frameworks in collaboration with marketing leads, creative partners, and product managers. You will craft messaging strategy across multiple channels and markets, with a focus on marketing effectiveness and user impact. You will have an opportunity to create work that will reach national and global audiences. Our team values collaboration, curiosity, and clarity, and we’re excited to welcome someone who shares these values. Description Write and expand messaging for mid- and lower-funnel channels across multiple channels and contexts Develop and maintain cohesive content strategies across products and platforms Manage messaging frameworks to ensure clarity and consistency Establish standard methodologies for content and message testing and optimization Partner with marketing leads on brief creation and test definition, and with data sciences leads to incorporate performance takeaways Conduct regular audits of messaging to find opportunities and improvements Minimum Qualifications Proven experience writing marketing copy that drives engagement or conversions Track record of managing content projects from ideation to execution Experience and skill communicating and collaborating with cross-functional teams Background in content strategy or marketing communications Preferred Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience Enthusiasm for Apple products and services Ability to identify and articulate key messages clearly and succinctly Experience delivering high-quality consumer marketing programs Collaborative, adaptable, and solutions-oriented mentality Familiarity with AI tools and openness to exploring how technology can improve our work Pay & Benefits At Apple, base pay is one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This provides the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role. The base pay range for this role is between $117,400 and $176,800, and your base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Apple employees also have the opportunity to become an Apple shareholder through participation in Apple’s discretionary employee stock programs. Apple employees are eligible for discretionary restricted stock unit awards, and can purchase Apple stock at a discount if voluntarily participating in Apple’s Employee Stock Purchase Plan. You’ll also receive benefits including: Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits, a range of discounted products and free services, and for formal education related to advancing your career at Apple, reimbursement for certain educational expenses — including tuition. Additionally, this role might be eligible for discretionary bonuses or commission payments as well as relocation. Learn more about Apple Benefits. Note: Apple benefit, compensation and employee stock programs are subject to eligibility requirements and other terms of the applicable plan or program. Apple is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity. We seek to promote equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics. Learn more about your EEO rights as an applicant. Apple accepts applications to this posting on an ongoing basis.

title: Creator — Real Estate & Investment Content
description: Atlas Realty Capital Atlas Capital enables global Indians to co-invest alongside leading real estate developers in India. Atlas Capital is not a listings platform and not a brokerage. It’s a private investment platform that uses content to educate, build credibility, and attract serious long-term investors before capital is ever discussed. Our audience already understands investing — they simply want to understand how deals actually work : risk, structure, incentives, and outcomes. If you enjoy explaining complex financial ideas simply, thinking like an investor, and creating content that earns trust over time, you’ll fit right in. title: Senior Proposal Specialist
description: Headquartered in Phoenix, Arizona, Haydon is a family-owned business established in 1991 on a foundation of grit and long-lasting relationships. The company serves a wide scale of markets with its all-in-one construction solutions including: Building (Commercial), Heavy Civil, Pre-Construction, Landscape and Building Information Modeling. Haydon’s unique full-service structure for all major scopes of work enables clients to receive detailed expertise during pre-construction, minimized schedule risk during construction and the best overall value on projects. With its full-service landscaping division, Earthscapes, and its Origin modeling and design subsidiaries, Haydon offers a full suite of premier modern construction solutions. Whether it’s the roads we drive on or the buildings we work, shop, learn, play and receive care in, Haydon is the one contractor that serves the entire spectrum of the construction industry – and it does so with heart. The company employs quality, passionate people, innovative technology, an open mind and a can-do attitude to construct projects that serve our entire community. For more information, please visit haydon.com JOB PURPOSE / POSITION OVERVIEW The Senior Proposal Specialist will support all aspects of the proposal design and development process. This position reports to the Proposal Manager and will work collaboratively with the rest of the business development and marketing team. Working hand-in-hand with operations leaders, project management, estimators, and field supervision, the Senior Proposal Specialist will help to strategically position Haydon Companies to win qualifications-based opportunities. DUTIES / RESPONSIBILITIES Proposal Strategy and Planning: Collaborate with senior leadership, project teams, and subject matter experts to develop strategic approaches for proposal development. Analyze project requirements, evaluate client needs, and identify key differentiators to create winning proposal strategies. Define proposal timelines, deliverables, and resource allocation to meet submission deadlines. Track and maintain database of upcoming projects provides updates associated with leads and opportunities utilizing Haydon CRM software. Proposal Development and Execution: Lead and manage the end-to-end proposal development process, including RFP/RFQ analysis, storyboarding, content development, and review cycles. Coordinate and collaborate with cross-functional teams, including technical experts, estimators, schedulers, and other stakeholders, to gather information and develop compelling proposal content. Ensure compliance with client requirements, addressing all evaluation criteria and adhering to proposal submission guidelines. Conduct thorough reviews and quality checks to ensure the proposal's accuracy, clarity, and consistency. Proposal Content Creation: Write, edit, and refine proposal content, including executive summaries, project descriptions, qualifications, and technical responses. Develop persuasive and client-focused narratives that effectively communicate our company's value proposition, experience, and capabilities. Tailor proposal content to address specific client needs, aligning with project objectives and requirements. Proposal Evaluation and Feedback: Participate in the evaluation of proposal performance, identifying areas for improvement and lessons learned. Solicit feedback to continuously enhance proposal quality and win rates. Maintain a library of reusable proposal content, templates, and best practices. Perform other duties or special projects as assigned MINIMUM EXPERIENCE REQUIRED (KSA’s, Education, YOE) Bachelor's degree in marketing, communications, or a related field (preferably in the Architecture/Engineering/Construction industry). 3+ years of marketing and leadership experience in B2B professional services. Experience in proposal management, preferably within the construction industry, with a focus on alternative delivery methods such as CMAR. Ability to leverage a network of clients and relationships. Understanding of A/E/C industry terminology and procedures. Proficient with Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign, Photoshop and Illustrator) and Microsoft applications. Excellent writing and editing skills, with the ability to craft clear, concise, and persuasive content. Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail to managing multiple projects simultaneously. Proficiency in proposal management software, MS Office Suite, CRM, and other relevant tools. Understanding construction industry standards, regulations, and best practices. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels. PHYSICAL DEMANDS / WORKING CONDITIONS (environment, noise level, public interactions) Office working environment with occasional travel to job sites Computer work, sitting Lift and move up to 15 pounds Ability to wear required PPE on job sites Benefits at Haydon/Earthscapes: Health Options Medical, Dental, & Vision Critical Illness, Hospital, Accident Short-Term / Long-Term Disability Infertility Treatment Coverage Worklife Balance Professional Development Teamwork / Camaraderie Retirement Planning EEO Statement Haydon Companies is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. We are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect where equal employment opportunities are available to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, age, physical and mental disability, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information, military and veteran status, and any other characteristic protected by appliable law. Visa and Sponsorship Candidates must have current and ongoing authorization to work in the United States without employer sponsorship. Except where expressly indicated in the job posting, the company is unable to sponsor or assume sponsorship of an employment visa at this time

title: Influencer, Affiliate & PR Manager

affiliate program across Impact and publisher partners Recruit and activate new affiliate partners and shopping editors Develop exclusive offers, launch moments, and incentive programs to drive calendars Track impact of editorial coverage through traffic, affiliate revenue, and share of voice Support planning and execution of press previews, influencer events, and activations Podcast and title: Customer Activation Manager | Public Sector
description: About Ramp At Ramp, we’re rethinking how modern finance teams function in the age of AI. We believe AI isn’t just the next big wave. It’s the new foundation for how business gets done. We’re investing in that future — and in the people bold enough to build it. Ramp is a financial operations platform designed to save companies time and money. Our all-in-one solution combines payments, corporate cards, vendor management, procurement, travel booking, and automated bookkeeping with built-in intelligence to maximize the impact of every dollar and hour spent. More than 50,000 businesses, from family-owned farms to e-commerce giants to space startups, have saved $10B and 27.5M hours with Ramp. Founded in 2019, Ramp powers the fastest-growing corporate card and bill payment platform in America, and enables over $100 billion in purchases each year. Ramp’s investors include Lightspeed Venture Partners, Thrive Capital, Sands Capital, General Catalyst, Founders Fund, Khosla Ventures, Sequoia Capital, Greylock, Redpoint, and ICONIQ, as well as over 100 angel investors who were founders or executives of leading companies. The Ramp team comprises talented leaders from leading financial services and fintech companies—Stripe, Affirm, Goldman Sachs, American Express, Mastercard, Visa, Capital One—as well as technology companies such as Meta, Uber, Netflix, Twitter, Dropbox, and Instacart. Ramp has been named to Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies list and LinkedIn’s Top U.S. Startups for more than 3 years, as well as the Forbes Cloud 100, CNBC Disruptor 50, and TIME Magazine’s 100 Most Influential Companies. About The Role Customer Success Managers at Ramp are responsible for the successful implementation and adoption of Ramp’s products. In this role, you will partner with public sector organizations to deliver measurable value through the successful adoption of Ramp’s solutions. As an early member of our Public Sector team, you will help shape Ramp’s methodology for activating and growing relationships with government agencies, educational institutions, and nonprofits. You’ll guide customers through onboarding, ensure long-term success, and serve as a trusted advisor to help them modernize financial workflows. What You’ll Do Lead Onboarding & Implementation: Manage the onboarding and rollout of Ramp for newly signed public sector customers, ensuring compliance with government implementation and security requirements. Drive Adoption Across Complex Stakeholders: Partner with multiple departments and decision-makers, such as finance, procurement, IT, and compliance, to align Ramp with organizational objectives. Serve as a Trusted Advisor: Act as a strategic consultant and extension of your customers’ teams by advising on best practices, workflow optimization, and change management specific to the public sector. Ensure Compliance & Security Alignment: Support customers in adhering to relevant regulations (e.g., FedRAMP, state procurement policies) while integrating Ramp into their ecosystem. Maximize Utilization & Value: Proactively identify opportunities to expand Ramp adoption, drive product utilization, and deliver measurable ROI for public institutions. Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work closely with Account Executives, Product, Engineering, and Compliance teams to deliver tailored solutions and resolve customer needs. Collect & Share Customer Insights: Surface feedback from public sector clients to influence product roadmaps and participate in workshops to shape Ramp’s long-term offering for this vertical. Manage Multiple Priorities: Balance implementation, follow-ups, reporting, and proactive engagement across a portfolio of accounts with varying complexity and timelines. What You’ll Need 7+ years of experience in implementation or engagement management in a B2B SaaS or public sector–focused technology company. To be based in the Washington, DC metro area. Strong program/project management skills, including leading complex technology deployments with multiple workstreams and compliance considerations. Excellent communication and relationship management skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for diverse audiences. Demonstrated success working with technical counterparts in Product, Engineering, and Security to troubleshoot and co-develop solutions. Ability to navigate public procurement processes, manage long sales/adoption cycles, and anticipate organizational needs. High adaptability and comfort working in a fast-paced, mission-driven environment. Nice to Haves Bachelor’s degree from an accredited university. Experience working with public sector organizations (federal, state, local, or education) and managing relationships with numerous stakeholders. Experience with government financial systems, ERP, or accounting platforms (e.g., Oracle, PeopleSoft, Workday, SAP). Familiarity with public sector compliance frameworks (e.g., FedRAMP, SOC 2, HIPAA, CJIS). Project management certification (e.g., PMP, CAPM, or PMI-ACP) Background in finance, accounting, or government procurement. Experience Scaling Customer Success Practices At a High-growth Company. Benefits (for U.S.-based full-time employees) 100% medical, dental & vision insurance coverage for you Partially covered for your dependents One Medical annual membership 401k (including employer match on contributions made while employed by Ramp) Flexible PTO Fertility HRA (up to $10,000 per year) Parental Leave Unlimited AI token usage Pet insurance Centralized home-office equipment ordering for all employees Health and Wellness stipend In-office perks: lunch, snacks, drinks, and more Budget for intra-office travel Relocation support to NYC or SF (as needed) Referral Instructions If you are being referred for the role, please contact that person to apply on your behalf. Other Notices Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records. Ramp Applicant Privacy Notice

title: Marketing Manager
description: Marketing Manager This is an onsite position. The position of Marketing Manager is one which offers an exciting and rewarding career opportunity in an ever-broadening industry. You’ll be managing the marketing, advertising and promotional activities for your clients. You’ll be expected to take steps to measure, enhance, and enrich the position and image of your clients’ organizations through agreed-upon goals and objectives. Most importantly you’ll be responsible for managing and continually improving your client relationships. Successful MadAve Marketing Managers are perceived by the majority of their clients not as vendors, not even as marketing consultants, but as trusted marketing advisors. We’ll expect you to come with proven marketing skills and a history of working effectively with creatives as well as Director and C-Level Executives. You should be an intelligent, goal-oriented, strategic thinker who takes complete ownership of your accounts and exhibits great communication skills. You’ll need to display strong team leadership skills and excel at program, project, and event management. You should also display competence with traditional marketing as well as digital marketing strategies. Most importantly, you should be a creative, curious, and hardworking marketer who is a passionate brand advocate with an overwhelming desire to grow your clients’ businesses. You’ll also be expected to provide documentation boasting a career in successful marketing management. At MadAve Marketing Management, we provide comprehensive marketing management services for companies with understaffed, underperforming, or non-existent marketing departments. The compensation range is $55,000 - $75,000 and consists of base and performance pay, and other earned monetary and non-cash awards. We’re a dynamic, fast-paced, and professional (yet politically incorrect) family of marketing agencies. We serve hundreds of clients in the United States and abroad. MadAveGroup has been named “One of the Best Employers in Ohio”, “Toledo’s Best Ad Agency”, and for seven consecutive years, a Toledo Blade "Top Workplace". We’ve also been recognized hundreds of times with local, regional, national, and international awards for creative and marketing excellence. Our agencies provide a fun, family-oriented corporate culture that fosters creativity, camaraderie, and personal growth. We believe our employees come first, their families second, and work third. Personal growth and career advancement are paramount. Internal promotions are valued over outside hires. And extensive personal and career development training is not only offered but mandated. In addition to base pay, all employees receive substantial performance pay. Team members are encouraged to set aside an hour each workday (at full pay) to exercise in the company gym. A corporate personal trainer is also available. Additionally, employees can earn financial awards by participating in quarterly Fitness Days. Employees also receive access to The MadAve Social Club, a 2,000 square foot employee lounge which features chess and poker tables, shuffleboard, darts, foosball, and Crokinole. Other benefits include a generous 401K retirement plan, health, vision, and dental insurance, generous vacation time, regular work-related social events, flexible work arrangements, and the very popular President’s Club; an all-expense-paid vacation to an exotic locale. MadAveGroup is a family of internationally recognized marketing agencies, each with its own unique specialty. Our clients benefit from the convenience that comes with having access to a diverse group of marketing agencies owned by the same organization; the consistency of working with one account team for all their marketing needs; and from the confidence of partnering with seasoned professionals to manage their strategic, tactical and brand marketing.
